sections.text: Refrigerator is a 37 technology structure in Palworld, and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
The Accumulator is used to store excess power generated by the Power Generator and the Large Power Generator.
When the Power Grid of a Base is producing less Power than required, the Accumulator will release stored Power, making the Grid more stable and avoiding outages.

Palworld offers achievements that you can unlock by completing various tasks and goals in the game. These achievements can be a fun way to test your skills, explore different aspects of Palworld, and track your progress.
There are two main categories of achievements in Palworld:
• Pal Capturing: These achievements reward you for catching different types and quantities of Pals. They range from catching your first Pal to becoming a seasoned Pal Tamer by catching a large number of unique Pal species.
• Gameplay Progression: These achievements focus on completing various in-game activities and demonstrating mastery over different systems. They can involve defeating tough bosses, crafting a lot of items, collecting resources, and even venturing into some of the more unusual aspects of Palworld (like, ahem, capturing a human).

rows.t: Caught Pal for the first time

Palpagos Guru / Guru of the Islands

Defeated 6 tower bosses in hard mode

Alpha Pals are larger Pals which appear at predefined locations in the world visible on the world map, obtained from hatching Pal Eggs or spawn at the end of Dungeons.
Some of them can be encountered as overworld bosses. They will be marked on the map, along with their level.
Most overworld Alpha Pals roam in the wild freely, while some instead appear as domains that can be entered to battle the Alpha Pal.
Each overworld Alpha Pal always has its own scripted place on the map and respawns 1 hour after being defeated.
The Alpha versions of Pals that do not have an Alpha version in the overworld (such as Foxparks) appear randomly as a boss at the end of Dungeons. There is also a small chance to acquire an Alpha Pal from an Egg, regardless of whether or not the parents were Alphas themselves.
Alpha Pals are larger than regular Pals, akin to Lucky Pals, and have a red Alpha icon next to their names and icons.

sections.heading: Differences
sections.text:
Prior to Early Access 0.2.4.0 Alpha Pals did not have higher stats (HP, attack, defense, etc) than regular Pals. 
Now Alpha Pals have a higher HP stat than regular pals.
Alpha Pals have a few differences compared to regular Pals:
• When killed, they grant 10x more experience than their regular variant would grant.
• The damage they receive is multiplied by a factor from 0.2 to 0.4 (depending on the specific Alpha Pal). This makes them last longer in combat.
• The capture rate is different, usually lower than the regular Pal. In most cases, the multiplier factor is 0.7 or 0.77.
• The possible drops are different for Alpha Pals. See Possible Drops section below.
In addition, when an Alpha Pal is caught, the icon is displayed next to its name in most interfaces.

sections.heading: Possible drops
sections.text:
Alpha Pals have additional possible drops beyond their normal counterparts. When defeated or caught, they will always drop Ancient Civilization Parts, and will sometimes drop items to be sold like Precious Pelt or Precious Dragon Stone.
Certain Alpha Pals have a small chance to drop Legendary Schematics. For example, the Beakon boss has a 3% chance to drop Handgun Schematic 4 when defeated.
When a specific Alpha Pal on the world map is defeated or caught for the first time, they provide the player with Ancient Technology Points and its respective Bounty token. 
These drops (including Schematics but excluding Ancient Technology Points) can be granted by Butchering Pals, allowing two chances to get the desired drops.

sections.heading: Sealed Alpha Pals
sections.text: Sealed Alpha Pals exist within their own Sealed Realms, which the player can enter to fight the boss in.

sections.heading: Titles
sections.text: The Alpha versions of each Pal have their own title. Following are the list of Pals with their Alpha Title:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• The Alpha icon will appear on overworld Pals that have a large level difference with the player. That icon will not render them Alphas, and will disappear when the player reaches a high enough level in comparison to the Pal. Indicating the icon is a metric of Threat assessment, rather than a status of a Pal itself.
 ◦ This also applies to Humans.
• When fighting overworld Alpha Pals, it's advised not to stray too far away from their primary spot, as the Alpha Pal might just retreat back to its spot in the middle of battle and recover all of its HP upon reaching it.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
It is obtained when defeating the Raid Bosses Blazamut Ryu, Bellanoir and Bellanoir Libero, or the main chest in the Oil Rig. It can also be obtained by clearing Pal Expeditions, with the amount received varying based on the expedition's difficulty. 
The amount looted from the Oil Rig is random, between 1 and 5.

sections.heading: History

• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Anubis (Japanese: アヌビス Anubis) is a Ground element pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Anubis is a jackal-like humanoid Pal that has a light-brown complexion overall, though the top half of its head and the "hair" or "veil" that drapes behind are the exceptions, being a dark brown instead. The eyes, nose, and inner canals of the tall, pointed ears are a sky blue in colour, and there is a round gold jewel upon the centre of its forehead. The aforementioned "hair/veil" ends in a broad, intricate, gold woven pattern, lined with small, round, sky blue gems. The tail is fairly long, and ends in a bushy dark brown tip. Perhaps due to its penchant for martial prowess, Anubis possesses fully developed hands, with a thumb and four fingers on each. The feet end in three toes each, and unlike an actual canine, appear to be more plantigrade rather than digitigrade.
Anubis is one of the few Pals that is depicted actively wearing clothing; its general attire roughly evokes the popular imagery of Egyptian-style dress, with sky blue accents and gold lining. The attire includes a broad, collared top resembling the wesekh worn by pharaohs, that drapes over the shoulders and exposes its midriff, sleeve-like gauntlets on the forearms, knee-length trousers, and anklet bands. The top is gold with a blue jewel at the collar, while the gauntlets and trousers are the same dark brown colour as its head and "veil". Anubis is fairly tall in stature, at about 180-185 cm in height, not counting the ears.

sections.heading: Behaviour
sections.text:
Anubis can only be encountered as an Alpha Pal in the Twilight Dunes, always spawning in front of a carved rock face monument of itself. 
Anubis is an aggressive Pal, and will attack the player character when nearby. It has quite the preference for various martial stances, possessing a naturally high Attack stat and a few Ground-type moves that are exclusive to it that make it great at inflicting damage, and can also occasionally evade enemy attacks whilst in battle, courtesy of its Partner Skill.
As a labourer, Anubis is the only Pal with a base Handiwork suitability of 4, making it the best factory worker in-game. This is coupled with decently high Mining and Transporting skills, which also makes it an excellent choice for mining and gathering ore, coal, or quartz deposits, or moving items around the base.
Whether working in a player's base or in combat, Anubis may prove to be one of the more versatile Pals in the game's roster.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Twilight Dunes (-134, -95) - Spawns as an Alpha Pals.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Guardian of the Desert - When fighting together, applies Ground damage to the player's attacks. Sometimes dodges attacks with a high speed sidestep while in battle.
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 4
• Mining 3
• Transporting 2
Possible Drops:
• Normal
 ◦ 3-5 Bone (100%)
 ◦ 1 Large Pal Soul (100%)
 ◦ 1 Innovative Technical Manual (5%)
• Boss
 ◦ 4-6 Ancient Civilization Parts (100%)
 ◦ 3-5 Bone (100%)
 ◦ 1 Large Pal Soul (100%)
 ◦ 4-6 Precious Entrails (100%)
 ◦ 1 Ring of Earth Resistance +2 (1%)

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• It is obviously based on the Egyptian god of the same name, which is actually the Greek and Latin name of the deity.
• Anubis is the Pal that is prominently featured as the Statue of Power.
 ◦ This choice could be intentional, as the fact that the statues require souls and effigies from the player to enhance stats could be alluding to Anubis being the god of funeral rites and judgement of the dead in ancient Egyptian cultural practice.
• Anubis was featured in Paldeck No.010 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
• Wild caught Anubis will always have the Earth Emperor Passive Skill.

Armor in Palworld is divided into 3 main components:
• Body
• Head
• Shields
• Terraria Collab
Armor provides defense against foes and the weather, though keep in mind that some armor is also geared specifically for particular biomes: Cold Resistant armor types are best for protection against cold/snow environments while Heat Resistant is for hot/desert environments. Going into these biomes without the right armor will cause one's health to steadily decrease over time until death or leaving that biome.
All base armor is unlocked with Technology Points at certain tiers, with all but shields and cosmetic types (like Tocotoco Cap) having additional schematics that can be found in game to make a better version, albeit requiring additional materials to make (and subsequently repair).

sections.text:
Arrow is ammo in Palworld.
These specifically are the weakest arrows among the types available in game. They can only be used with Old Bow, Three Shot Bow and Crossbow.
Can also be put in a chest next to a Mounted Crossbow for a Pal with the Handiwork suitability to use.

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.text:
Technology tier; 3
Technology points; One (1)
Per set; 
• One (1) Wood
• One (1) Stone

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
Craft:
Note; Arrows are Produced in Sets of 3. When crafting, you are picking the number of sets. (Set x 3 = number of arrows)
• Primitive Workbench
• High Quality Workbench
• Production Assembly Line
• Production Assembly Line II
• Weapon Workbench
• Weapon Assembly Line
• Weapon Assembly Line II
Drop:
• Robinquill
• Robinquill Terra
Farming:
• Vixy (Ranch)

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: Despite Feathers existing, they are Not required to craft a set of Arrows.

Arrows are a type of ammunition used with bows. It is the first type of ammunition players can get starting out.
• Arrow - used in Old Bow, Three Shot Bow, and Crossbow.
• Fire Arrow - used in Fire Bow and Fire Arrow Crossbow.
• Poison Arrow - used in Poison Bow and Poison Arrow Crossbow.
Arsox (Japanese: ブルフェルノ Bullferno) is a Fire element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Arsox resembles a ram with wool made of fire, a pair of rocky horns on its head, and a set of brown hooves and ears.
Arsox is a medium sized ram-like pal, it's body is primarily tan with spots of red hair mainly on it's back and the sides of it's head, the head has a pair of large horns like a ram's and just above each foot is a iron band, however Arsox's most notable feature is the mane and tail made out of fire.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Arsox exhibit a temperamental nature, readily attacking players upon sighting them. This Pal can be frequently found imprisoned by members of a faction.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
Arsox can be commonly found situated across different regions of the map. Some potential locations to find this pal include:
• trapped in cages at enemy bases and imprisoned by members of the Rayne Syndicate.
•in the area south of the astral mountains near no man’s trail (daytime only)
•In the area north of the bamboo groves and west of the twilight dunes
•in the scars of war area, located east of the Frostbound mountains

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Warm Body - Can be ridden. Keeps the rider warm in cold environments. (Requires Arsox Saddle → Unlocks at Lv. 16)
Work Suitability:
• Kindling 2
• Lumbering 1
Possible Drops:
• Horn
• Flame Organ

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words arson and ox.
• Its Japanese name may come from the words bull and inferno.
• Arsox was featured in Paldeck No.038 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: An Arsox sleeping in a Hot Spring by standing in it.

sections.text: Arsox Saddle is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It is a tier 16 Technology and requires 1 point to unlock after catching an Arsox.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used to unlock Arsox's Partner Skill, Warm Body, which allows players to ride it and have increased resistance to cold environments while doing so.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Assault Rifle is a ranged weapon in Palworld, it is the main weapon of the PIDF.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: The Assault Rifle is available to unlock at level 45, and you are able to get it through crafting at a Weapon Assembly Line. There are also 4 schematics available that you can find throughout the world that offer different rarities of the weapon.

Assault Rifle Ammo is a type of ammo in Palworld.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It's ammo primarily for the Assault Rifle. Crafting one unit will result in 5 pieces of ammo.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Assault Rifle Schematic 1 is a schematic in Palworld. Obtained as a possible drop from the Blazamut Alpha. It is required to craft the Uncommon variant of the Assault Rifle.

Assault Rifle Schematic 2 is a schematic in Palworld. Obtained as a possible drop from the Blazamut Alpha, or as a reward in Treasure Chests at the end of Dungeons. It is required to craft the Rare variant of the Assault Rifle.
Assault Rifle Schematic 3 is a schematic in Palworld. Obtained as a possible drop from the Blazamut Alpha. It is required to craft the Epic variant of the Assault Rifle.
Assault Rifle Schematic 4 is a schematic in Palworld. Obtained as a possible drop from the Blazamut Alpha. It is required to craft the Legendary variant of the Assault Rifle.
Astegon (Japanese: ジオラーヴァ Jiorava) is a Dragon / Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Astegon bears a resemblance to a robotic dragon with jet black wings patterned with red rubies and has set of purple crystal-like claws for both the hands and feet.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is usually aggressive and will attack the player on sight.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
• The Alpha Astegon can be found within the Destroyed Mineshaft, located within Mount Obsidian. (-578, -418)
• No. 3 Wildlife Sanctuary

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Black Anklyosaur - Can be ridden as a flying mount. Increases damage dealt to ore while mounted. (Requires Astegon Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 47)
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 1
• Mining 4
Possible Drops:
• Pal Metal Ingot
• Pure Quartz

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Its Japanese name may be partially based on Ravana (the main antagonist of the Hindu legend, the Ramayana) and/or ravager.

images.caption: An Astegon resting in the High Quality Hot Spring.

sections.text: Astegon Saddle is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 47 Technology item and requires 4 points to unlock after catching a Astegon. It is crafted in the Pal Gear Workbench.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used to unlock Astegon's Partner Skill, Black Ankylosaur.

sections.text:

Average Feed Bag is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Requirements;
sections.text:
• Technology Tier 20
• Small Feed Bag
• Two (2) Ancient Technology Points

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Crafting the Average Feed Bag increases the number of different types of food storage slots from 1 to 2.
Useful for having a primary food source you have a lower quantity of, and a backup food like Baked Berries,
Alternatively, having a high-quantity food in the first slot allows you to use the second slot as additional Backpack space for a food of higher Satiety.

sections.heading: Drawbacks
sections.text:
Things to consider when using multiple food sources at the same time include
• Weight; how much does all food combined add to my total carry weight?
• Priority; Which food do I want my character, and my pals, to consume first. keeping in mind Pals and Player eat automatically when hunger reaches ≤50%, so if you have a high Satiety food in the first slot, your party may consume foods too early to fully appreciate the satiety value of mid to late-game recipe foods.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text: • Baked berries in the first slot mean your party will consume the least valuable food first.

sections.heading: See also
sections.text:
• Small Feed Bag
• Large Feed Bag
• Huge Feed Bag
• Giant Feed Bag

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Axel Travers (also dubbed "Axel Traverse") is the current leader of the Brothers of the Eternal Pyre and owner of the electric-dragon Pal Orserk. His tower is located on Mount Obsidian in the west.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Axel is a young man with an athletic build. He has light blue eyes, tanned skin, and a zigzag tattoo on his biceps. He has sideburns and sports a black beanie with yellow and purple zigzag patterns on it. Dual colored hair can be seen from underneath his beanie. His hair is purple with yellow tips that resemble lightning bolts. 
Axel wears a purple sleeveless crop top with a high collar that has yellow and black lines at the hem and with the sides of the neck having a yellow zigzag design. His yellow khaki pants have white lightning bolts on it and have purple suspenders with yellow zigzag designs on them. He also wears purple basketball trainers with yellow lines on them. 
Axel accessorizes with three rings on both hands, bracelets on each wrist, a lightning-shaped necklace and earrings.

sections.heading: Biography
sections.text:
The villagers of Fisherman's Point village are fire worshippers and are part of the Brothers of the Eternal Pyre. They operate as a religious group who believe in purification by fire as they view the fire as a holy entity that, while it’s cruel, they also consider it beautiful and want to use it to cleanse the world from threats such as human manipulation, particularly genetic experiments conducted by groups like the Pal Genetic Research Unit (PGRU).
Axel Travers has been appointed as the new high priest of the Brothers of the Eternal Pyre. The villagers look to him for guidance, as he has journeyed to the tower on Mount Obsidian for a prayer but because of his long absence, he has become somewhat of a mystery to them. Viewing him as being elusive, they started to believe he is conducting a holy mission.
Under his leadership, the Brothers of the Eternal Pyre have become more extreme in their ways than they used to be with their old leader. They have also started to have more direct conflicts with the PGRU than they used to, increasing in intensity of bloodshed.
Axel has a very strong fighting spirit. He is a fierce and confident fighter, always looking forward for a worthy challenge to prove his skills and strength. Because of his nature, he has a strong thirst for battles and wants to fight worthy opponents. This thirst makes him desire these battles to be legendary-like, as he doesn’t want weak opponents who give up easily, with a knockout being the only result that will satisfy him.
Axel loves rapping and includes Orserk in his raps, praising his powers over lightning. He seems to have a strong bond with Orserk and is confidence in him as he boasts about his lightning abilities and how powerful they are, trusting he can put an end to any battle quickly with his powerful lightning attacks and believing they are undefeatable together.

sections.heading: Story events
sections.text: (story mode wip)

sections.heading: Missions
sections.text: (wip)

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Axel is one of the original five tower bosses who were first released alongside Marcus, Zoe, Lily, and Victor.
• In early access, he was released with the fewest journals compared to the other tower bosses, having only one.
• Despite Axel being the leader and the high priest of the Brothers of the Eternal Pyre, which is a religious faction whose religion is focused around fire worship, he is not using a fire type Pal instead, he is using the electric type Pal Orserk.
• Axel's placeholder name was "Mysterious rapper" during the game's early development.

Axel Travers's Diary - 1 is a journal in Palworld.
It's written by Axel Travers.
Azurmane (Japanese: オーマサンダ Ohmasanda) is an Electric elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Azurmane is a unicorn pal that has black lightning marks on its blue fur, has a golden metallic horn and its has a blue-ish electrically charged mane and tail.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: When it sees the player it immediately becomes hostile.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
Azurmane can be found in Feybreak island(except the lighter colored area) at night. *Note as of march 2025 there is a glitch were they do not spawn, so you have to get them through either the boss pal or breeding 
Azurmane is available as a level 57 Field Alpha Boss located south-west of the Hidden Passage to the Oculus Gate teleport point in Feybreak, with coordinates (-1106, -1316).

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Plasma dash Allows for fast movement and air dashing.
Work Suitability:
• Generating electricity 4
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops: 
• 2-3 Electric organs

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:

sections.text: Azurobe (Japanese: アズレーン Azurain) is a Water/ Dragon element pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Azurobe is a pal that takes on a serpentine appearance. Its long snake-like body is primarily blue with a lighter blue underside. It has white ribbons and various matching white patterns that surround its body. Azurobe has long flowing indigo hair on its head. Next to its pink eyes, Azurobe has what look to be dark blue fins that droop down to the sizes. The tip of Azurobe's tail features a bright pink pearl attached to it.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Azurobe prefers the deeper water. It is a pacific pal that will flee from the player unless attacked.

Baked Berries are a the primary consumable ingredient for early game Palworld.
Easily produced in mass quantities, Baked berries are cheap to produce, fast to cook, and nutritious enough to stave off starvation for early to late game. Once automated with Berry Plantations a well planned Base can sustain its workers with extra Baked Berries for the Player to take on their adventures.
They can be obtained by Cooking Red Berries at cooking stations such as a Campfire.

They can be obtained by cooking Mushroom at cooking stations such as a campfire.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Barricade Set is furniture in Palworld.

As an open-world survival game, building various structures is a significant part of Palworld.
All buildings/structures are unlocked with Technology points.
__FORCETOC__

sections.heading: Palbox
sections.text:
Building will typically revolve around your Palbox. Anything built outside of the area of the Palbox that is designated with a faint blue outline will slowly deteriorate. Some buildings, such as the Ranch have to be built within this boundary. Pals will also try and stick to this boundary, actively avoiding fights and items that aren't inside.
The Palbox itself is able to level up when the player meets the requirements for each level. This will often require the player to build certain buildings that are typically relevant to the progression of the game, such as: Primitive Furnace or an abundance of Straw Pal Beds. Upon leveling up the Palbox, you will be allowed to use more pals within your base, up to a maximum of 15 and 20 with adjusted world settings. You will also be granted the ability to build an extra 2 "Bases" alongside the first. This is important for Farming, quick transport or specific material farms, such as an Ore farm.

sections.heading: Construction
sections.text:
Pals can be used as the labor in construction in large numbers. Labor laws are not expected to apply to Pals.
Certain Pals can be used for building and assisting in various construction tasks. These Pals are essentially creatures in the game that have unique abilities or traits that make them suitable for helping players construct buildings, gather materials, or perform other construction-related activities. 
Pals like Lifmunk will keep you stocked with wood, while Fuddler can collect stone and ore. Set them to automated gathering mode.

sections.heading: Types
sections.text:
Buildings come in multiple types.
Production:
• These structures are used for resource gathering, such as logs or stone or creating compound resources, such as ingots or nails. Items in this category include things like workbenches, mills, furnace, logging camps, etc.
Pal:
Structures related to Pals go here. These include ranching, breeding, condensing, directing, hatching, sphere assembly, etc.
Storage:
• A wide variety of buildings used to store other items. From chests to cabinets to containers.
Food:
These structures are all about food generation and processing. These include plantations, campfires, and cooking pots.
Infrastructure:
These structures include things like beds for both the pals and yourself as well as heating and cooling elements to make your base run smoothly no matter where you set up.
Lighting:
These structures include things like torches and lamps to light up your base.
Foundations:
These structures are used for building houses and walls around your base. These include the structure sets, gates, and defensive walls.

sections.text:
This is the third structure set available in game. It requires one to unlock the Metal Structure Set (Tier 30 Technology item for 4 points) and allows one to then build the following:
Defenses:
These structures include things like the alarm bell and traps to keep your pals on high alert or to catch small pals when they invade.
Other:
These structures include a bunch of production boosting items that you can place around your base, such as a silo for planting or a mining cap and helmet for mining.
Furniture:
These structures are for sprucing up your home and bedazzling it to make it the way you want it, things like the tavern furniture set and house plants reside here.

sections.text: Base Raids are a challenging and exciting aspect of Palworld. They add a layer of risk and reward to your gameplay, forcing you to consider your base's location, defenses and the capabilities of your Pal companions.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text:
• Base raids are events where groups of Humans or hostile Pals, known as raiders, who attacks your base. Their goal is to damage your structures and potentially defeat you and your Pals.
• Raids can occur at any time but it usually triggers when you are at your own Base.
• Raids will end when one of the following conditions are met:
 ◦ All of the raiders are taken out.
 ◦ All of the pals at your base are taken out.
 ◦ The raiders retreat and despawn. This happens if the raid goes on for too long.
• Unlike in a lot of other games, Raiders will not simply fight to the last man standing. Raiders will turn tail and retreat (and once far enough away, despawn) once one of the following conditions are met:
 ◦ less than half to a quarter (Exact number needs confirmation) their starting number remain.
 ◦ A few minutes (Exact number needs confirmation) have passed. Once this timer is up, they will retreat regardless of the current status of the raid.
• A base must be considered 'raidable' by the AI. Beyond certain unknown conditions to determine type (as of yet unknown,) a base is 'raidable' only if it can reach the base on foot. Bases that are placed on top of mountains or pleateus surrounded by cliff faces on all sides will never get raided because the AI calculates that it cannot reach this base. 
 ◦ This isn't perfect, however. Some bases can trigger raids yet still be 'unraidable' as the pathway to your base can force them to waste time traversing to your base causes them to time out before they ever reach the base.
• The composition of the raiders varies depending on your location and progression in the game. Early on, you might face weak land-based Pals. Later, you'll encounter stronger and more diverse enemies, including flying Pals. 
 ◦ You can disable Base Raiding in the World Settings in single player or dedicated servers.

sections.heading: Additional Tips
sections.text:
• Raids can be a good source of resources. Defeated raiders often drop items like Pal Spheres, Treasure Keys, Ammos and various other things which you can use for crafting and other purposes.
• You can also capture raiders and add them to your Pal collection. However, be aware that they might be difficult to tame due to their hostile nature.
• Walls are an excellent way of deterring raids. Even basic stone walls have very high defense, making just about every attack against them do only 1 damage. Furthermore, enemies will attempt to melee their way through obstacles instead of using attacks, further reducing their chances for a breech. A good wall can hold off even a high level raid.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Bellanoir is a tall, witch-like humanoid with a black gown-like body. Around its waist, there are red, eye-like patterns. Instead of legs, blueish-purple flames flicker beneath its skirt. Its arms have frayed sleeves ending in three white triangles, resembling hands. The left eye has long eyelashes and a red pupil, while blue hair covers the right eye. An antler-like protrusion extends from the left side of its forehead, starting blue and fading to light purple at the tip.

It was written by Bjorn Seligsson.

The Black Marketeer or Black Market Merchant is an NPC who buys and sells Pals in exchange for Gold Coins, much like a standard Pal Merchant. Unlike his more lawful counterpart, though, the Black Marketeer has no regard for the rules and regulations on selling Pals, and thus can be found selling rare and high-value Pals that you wouldn't see most other merchants selling (such as those found only in the Wildlife Sanctuaries). Because of this, Black Marketeers are a great way to get ahold of powerful Pals that are generally hard to find out in the open...if you can find where they're hiding.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Unlike all the other merchants in the game (whom are clearly palette swaps of the same model), the Black Marketeer has a distinct appearance that differs drastically from his fellow shopkeepers. He takes the form of a large, heavyset man who wears a black cloak over his standard uniform. His hood covers the top half of his face, though his beady yellow eyes still shine through the shadow created by the hood. He sports sickly grey skin and a huge, toothy grin, and he holds an old-fashioned smoking pipe in between his teeth.

sections.heading: Locations
sections.text:
Like other merchants, Black Marketeers generally appear in random spots on the map; but due to the seedy nature of their services, Black Marketeers are much more elusive and generally harder to find than any other type of merchant. However, there are a select few locations where you'll always find a Black Marketeer camping out. These locations include abandoned mineshafts which feature long, winding tunnels that lead to a room containing a Black Marketeer, along with a slew of cages, guillotines, and other items scattered all over the room. A source of light may be recommended regardless of the time of day, as due to occasional lighting problems, the mineshafts may be rendered pitch-black or fully illuminated (its not certain which one is intended). Some of these locations are as follows:
• A short way below the Desolate Church Fast Travel point, to the west and hidden underneath a cliff.
• To the west of the PIDF Tower entrance, wedged inside a canyon within the Desiccated Desert(298,344)
• Inside the Desolate Church on the left of entry.
• Alongside the northern outside wall of Duneshelter (350, 370)

sections.heading: Dialogue
sections.text:
Black Marketeers will greet you by saying:
:"I trade in any kind of Pal... Whether it’s stolen Pals, or even prohibited types... Take a look..."
You can then trade with them, or leave:
:"Buy contraband"
:"Sell contraband"
:"Leave"
::"Heheheh, looks like meat's on the menu today..."

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Just like any other human NPC, Black Marketeers can be captured with Pal Spheres and brought back to your base, where they will always be available for you to buy from and sell to them at your leisure.
 ◦ Attacking or attempting to capture him will result in him fighting back by whipping a minigun out of hammerspace and shooting the player with it.

 ◦ Introduced.

Blazamut (Japanese: ボルカイサーVolkaiser) is a Fire element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Blazamut is a large bipedal beast with a gray mane that ends in ash black, red skin, a set of curved lava rocks as horns, and lava rocks also surrounding its forearms. It has three toes and darkened claws on each toe. On its nose is a yellow horn, the same color as its eyes.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words blaze and Bahamut.
• Its Japanese name may come from volcano and Kaiser (emperor in German) which is also the correct pronunciation of Caesar in Latin that happens to be the etymological origin of Kaiser. This Roman name, which meant "head of hair", got turned into a title in Latin first and then phonetically spelled out in German to mean the same.
• Blazamut was featured in Paldeck No.070 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Blazamut Ryu (Japanese: ボルドラゴ Voldrago) is a Dragon and Fire Elemental Subspecies Pal. Is a Sub-Species of Blazamut and was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.text: Blazamut Ryu can only be obtained through a raid. It is level 55 and weak against Water and Ice types. However, when its HP drops to 50,000 out of its total 520,000, it becomes temporarily immortal, recovering around 150,000 HP and changing to an Electric type.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Blazehowl is a lion-like Pal distinguished by its prominent mane composed of flames, which constantly emit a steady glow. It has a pair of large, curved black horns on its head and a set of flaming wings that allow it to fly. Its body is covered in black fur, marked by orange stripes that are most prominent on its legs, back, and tail. sections.heading: Behavior

Its entire body is tan, sporting a lighter underbelly. It sports a headcrest made up of four green leaves, which hangs over three orange petals on the back of its neck. All four of its feet have two grey, hoof-like toes. It has a long, reptilian tail, which is decorated with four green leaves in a symmetrical formation on its tip.

Breeding is a mechanic in Palworld. Through breeding you can get various passive skills or Subspecies Pals.
To breed Pals together you will need a Breeding Farm. Once you place the Breeding Farm you must assign a male and female Pal. The Breeding Farm also needs to be supplied with Cake. After some time they will produce a Pal Egg which can then be incubated.
There are 34,969 breeding pair combinations, and we simply cannot list them all!

sections.heading: How Breeding Works
sections.text:
The way breeding works is rather simple. Each Pal has a hidden breeding power number between 1 and 1500. The lower the number the more rare or powerful the Pal is. 
The game will then pick the Pal with the closest power to that average. As an example, if the baby Power ends up being 1015, and the closest values are 1010 (Felbat) and 1020 (Robinquill), the baby will be Robinquill. The 'tie breaker' (since 1015 is equal distance between 1010 and 1020) just comes down to the which Pal comes first in the game file's index. In this case Robinquill comes first in the game files so it gets picked over Felbat.
The way this works means you can never breed a Pal stronger/rarer than your strongest/rarest parent. For example, if your starting parents have a power of 570 (Anubis) and 1460 (Cattiva) the result will be the following: (570 + 1460) / 2 = 1015 (Robinquill).
If you then bred that baby at 1015 with your starting 570 Anubis: (570 + 1015) / 2 = 792.5 (which is closest to Croajiro at 795).
If you continue this multiple times:
• (570 + 795) / 2 = 682.5 (Univolt at 680)
• (570 + 680) / 2 = 625 (Omascul at 630)
• (570 + 625) / 2 = 597.5 (Incineram at 590)
As you can see you can get closer and closer to your 570 Anubis but you can never breed something stronger/rarer. But this also shows you can take one strong/rare Pal and breed it with its offspring and quickly start making Pals near that strength.

sections.heading: Gender Ratios
sections.text: Most Pals have a gender ratio of 1:1 Male to Female. These are the outliers.

sections.heading: List of Special Combinations
sections.text:
Below are the special combinations that do not use the breeding power method to determine the result.
All combinations have been repeatedly tested to ensure results.
This can be seen in this chart.
• Note: Katress/Wixen results is dependant on the genders of the parents. Female Wixen gives Wixen Noct and Female Katress gives Katress Ignis.
Additionally, 12 other Pals can only be born from two parents of the same species:
Chikipi
Blazamut Ryu
Paladius

Jetragon
Bellanoir Libero
Selyne

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 19 technology item and costs 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: In order to breed the player will need 1 Male and 1 Female Pal assigned to the Breeding Farm, and 1 cake. Place a cake into the box attached to the Breeding Farm once the two pals are assigned and they will produce an egg over time without consuming sanity. The egg can be hatched at an Egg Incubator. Pals will produce an egg every 5 minutes, and the breeding time depends on the work speed set by the Monitoring Stand.

It has a green body and red hair, both of which have thorns dotted across them. The top of its head has three yellow stamen sticking out from within the hair.

Broncherry (Japanese: ラブラドン Loveladon) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Broncherry is a sauropod-like Pal with a striped body that is colored with yellow and green hues. Its pink petal-like extensions encircle the base of its neck and form two parallel rows along the upper neck, loosely resembling the appearance of certain dicraeosaurids. Along its tail a row of leafy appendages is present on each side.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Broncherry are peaceful Pals that typically refrain from aggression unless provoked. They are usually encountered wandering alone in the wilderness.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Overaffectionate*Can be ridden (Requires Broncherry Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 20)
• While in team, Broncherry helps carry supplies, increasing the player's max carrying capacity.
• At level 1 with the Broncherry Saddle, the skill adds 100 additional weight. 
Work Suitability:
• Planting 3
Possible Drops:
• Broncherry Meat 
• Tomato Seeds

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Broncherry's name may come from the words brontosaurus and cherry. Perhaps coincidentally, this name also sounds similar to the french expression Mon Chéri (my dear), a phrase used to express endearment or deep affection, referencing the general love-theming of this Pal.
• Its Japanese name may come ラブラブ raburabu (Wasei-eigo from "love-love"), this refers to acting lovey-dovey or being infatuated. Additionally, its probably combined with the suffix -odon that is used in the scientific names of multiple large animals.
• Broncherry's Partner Skill, Overaffectionate, has two effects. The passive effect is always active, and, while in the player's party, grants the player a +100/+110/+120/+130/+140 bonus to Carrying capacity, scaling with Partner Skill Level. This bonus stacks with other Pals in the party for an additive effect. This is the second largest passive Carrying capacity boost in the game, tied with Broncherry Aqua, Kingpaca, and Kingpaca Cryst, and below Wumpo and Wumpo Botan. The active effect requires the Broncherry Saddle to use, and allows the player to mount the Broncherry and manually activate its moves.
• Broncherry has a base Stamina of 100, and a Mount Speed of 500, making it the slowest Mount in the game, tied with Sweepa and Broncherry Aqua.
• Broncherry is a large Grass Pal with a decent move pool. The ability to mount Broncherry once the saddle is crafted is useful to manually activate its moves in combat, but its viability is limited by its low Mount Speed.
• Broncherry was featured in Paldeck No.048 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Broncherry Aqua (Japanese: スプラドン Spladon) is the Water element variation of Broncherry.

Along its tail, rows of blue leafy appendages are present on each side. Additionally it has a crystalline blue semi-sphere located on the top of its snout and a similar tear shaped piece on the center of its lower neck.

The Brothers of the Eternal Pyre are a religious faction who worship fire and believe in the purifying power of flames. They see themselves as protectors of the natural world and view the use of technology for genetic manipulation, practiced by factions like the PGRU, as an abomination. The faction is led by the enigmatic Axel, a skilled warrior who fights alongside his powerful Dragon/Electric-element Pal companion, Orserk.

sections.heading: Members
sections.text:
• Axel Travers - Leader 
• Brothers of the Eternal Pyre Martyr (Assault Rifle)
• Brothers of the Eternal Pyre Martyr (Flamethrower)

title: Brothers of the Eternal Pyre Martyr

sections.text: Brothers of the Eternal Pyre Martyrs are a type of enemy affiliated with the Brothers of the Eternal Pyre.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: The Martyrs behave identically to Syndicate Cleaners, using flamethrowers to spray massive streams of fire that deal heavy damage and inflict burns if you get caught in the range of their attack. However, unlike Syndicate Cleaners which may randomly appear in various parts of the world, Martyrs only appear exclusively in Mount Obsidian, where they tend to appear in small groups that patrol the territory. Wearing heat-resistant armor is highly recommended for dealing with the Martyrs, otherwise their flame attacks can cause serious damage and bring your health down in a matter of seconds.

Bushi (Japanese: ツジギリ Tsujigiri) is a Fire element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Bushi is a tall humanoid Pal that mostly mirrors the classic image of the wandering Samurai archetype. Most of Bushi's skin is a light beige in colour, though the forearms, legs, and top half of the head are a dark grey. Its torso is wrapped in white sarashi bandages, covering the point where the beige on the body changes to dark grey. Its legs are long and hollow, and flare out at the ends, resembling a hakama. There are triangular "cutouts" where the feet would be. The bottom rims of these legs are the same beige colour as the head and upper body. Held in its left hand is an orange and white katana and scabbard, which it always keeps by its left hip when it is not resting. Adorning the shoulders is a red thigh-length cape with orange guards attached to the sides.
Perhaps the most striking feature is the large wide-rimmed circular hat, resembling that of an ashigaru's jingasa or sandogasa, from which an orange flame billows from the top. A red band below the hat keeps it secured on Bushi's round head, which is connected to the body by a thin neck, and features two light-red eyes and a small mouth, from which a piece of straw is often carried.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal usually attack the player while he spot him. It treats its battles with precision and will perform dash-like strikes, but it is also known to shoot elemental blades of all element types. It is one of the few Pals that is capable of wielding almost all elements naturally.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Brandish Blade - When activated, attacks targeted enemy with a powerful Iaigiri.
Work Suitability:
• Kindling 2
• Handiwork 1
• Transporting 2
• Lumbering 3
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Bone
• Ingot

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Bushi may be inspired by a rōnin, a lone, wandering swordsman. In historical contexts, the term specifically referred to an armed man who has either been outcasted or gone rogue, or has no fealty to any liege lord, which would make such a man very low on the social caste system of feudal Japan compared to normal samurai, and not worthy of any respect. Rōnin are often depicted as wearing large, wide hats to cover their entire heads from the public, as they were considered so lowly and shameful to the point that the common folk were not allowed to look upon their faces.
• Bushi's Japanese name, Tsujigiri (literally "crossroads killing"), is named after the act of samurai testing a new sword or martial technique by killing a random unsuspecting commoner, usually at night, due to commoners not being of the military caste, and thus having little-to-no rights. The practice was declared illegal by the Edo period, in 1602.
 ◦ Bushi's English name, by comparison, is simply a synonym for any feudal-era warrior. It is the word which the term bushido, the traditional Japanese code of warrior ethics and expectations, is derived from. In historical usage, it is not interchangeable with the word samurai in the Japanese language.
• Bushi was featured in Paldeck No.067 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: An Alpha Bushi refining some Ingots.

sections.text: Bushi Noct (Japanese: ヤミウチ Yamiuchi) is a Fire and Dark elemental Pal sub-species of Bushi. It was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Bushi Noct is a tall, humanoid Pal. On the top of its head, it wears a beige like hat that is held in place by violet straps that go under its chin. The hat is wide brimmed, and drapes down. On the top of this hat, is a flame that burns with magenta, blue and a black-purple colour. It has light blue skin on its face, and narrowed golden eyes with white slitted pupils. It appears to look like its wearing opera gloves, and does indeed have five fingers. It wears a blue-purple cape with a dark purple design on the back. Around its torso are white bandages. The lower half of its body makes it look like it wears flared pants that conceal its feet. The lower half of its body is black, tipped with a teal band that wrapped around the bottom. Teal triangles occasionally point up from the bottom. It appears to carry a sheathed katana with it at all times, only drawing the sword for combat.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This is an aggressive Pal and attacks the player on sight, regardless of what has happened so far.

Cake is an ingredient in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It is made in a Cooking Pot or any higher-level stove. It is also dropped from Lovanders with a 1% chance.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
To have a fully operational cake-baking farm, one must have the following;
• Player level 15, to construct at least one Wheat Plantation and Mill.
• At least one Beegarde assigned to a Ranch for Honey harvesting.
• At least one Chikipi assigned to a Ranch for Egg harvesting.
• At least one Mozzarina assigned to a Ranch for Milk harvesting.
• At least one Pal with one or more of the following Work Suitability of 1 or greater assigned to the Base; Transporting, Watering, Gathering, Cooling, Kindling.
• At least one Berry Plantation.
• A Cooler Box to store ingredients until baking begins.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used when breeding Pals in a Breeding Farm. The Cake does not spoil in the Breeding Farm's chest. It can also be eaten to satisfy hunger, or SAN.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.

Campfire is a structure in Palworld that lets you cook dishes.
It's a tier 2 technology item and requires 1 point to unlock. Once unlocked, one can make a number of cooked dishes with it provided they have the necessary ingredients.
If you stand on the campfire, you will be set on fire. Since fire harms many enemies but doesn't anger them, you can use the campfire to take down the health of difficult enemies. It is also a source of warmth, due to the open flame, though not as effective as a Heater, and doesn't provide as much light as a Mounted Torch/Lamp.
A pal with Kindling can help cook the dish. 
The campfire is superseded by the Cooking Pot and Electric Kitchen. It is for early game, and can only cook common recipes. The Cooking Pot (makes uncommon) should be used once you reach mid game, and the Electric Kitchen (makes rare+) for late game.
Despite being a campfire, it does not require wood to keep it burning, only ten to create in the Base Building menu.
Caprity (Japanese: メリゴート Berrygoat) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Caprity is a goat-like Pal. It has a large, circular bush on its back, the leaves triangular and pointed. On the bush, it grows cherry red circular berries. Its head is a lime green colour, with long, fluffy ears that point outwards to the sides. The fluffy ears are tipped with a darker green colour. On the top of its head, are two curled lime green tufts that curl upwards. On the sides of the tufts, it has two long brown horns that curl backwards. On each horn, it has thin horizontal black stripes. It has golden eyes with slitted pupils, and dark salmon pink fur around the eyes that resembles eyeliner. It has a strawberry pink nose shaped like an upside down triangle, and a cat like smile on its face. The lower half of its face is white. Around its neck is a huge fluffy collar. The neck fur has white in the middle, the ends finishing with a dark lime green colour. It has a thick, stubby body with stubby legs. Its back is a leaf green colour, the lower half of its body being lime coloured. Around its legs are a white fur colour, with dark brown goat hooves. It has a long tail that looks like a leaf.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: A normally non-hostile Pal that may flee from the player, usually encountered in pairs. Will retaliate when struck.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Berry Picker - Sometimes drops Red Berries from its back when assigned to Ranch. 
Work Suitability:
• Planting 2
• Farming 1
Possible Drops:
• Caprity Meat
• Red Berries
• Horn

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Their name may come from caprinae, a sub-family mostly comprising of sheep and goats, and pretty.

It has golden eyes with slitted pupils, and dark salmon pink fur around the eyes that resembles eyeliner. It has a strawberry pink nose shaped like an upside down triangle, and a cat like smile on its face. The lower half of its face is white. Around its neck is a huge fluffy collar. The neck fur has white in the middle, the ends finishing with a dark magenta colour. It has a thick, stubby body with stubby legs. Its back is a midnight purple colour, the lower half of its body being violet coloured. Around its legs are a white fur colour, with dark brown goat hooves. It has a long tail that looks like a leaf.

Cattiva (Japanese: ツッパニャン Tsuppanyan) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Cattiva is a pal that resembles a small bipedal pink cat. It has four thick whiskers on its face with two on each side. Cattiva's forehead sports an exaggerated tuft of fur. Cattiva has a tail that is short and curly in appearance. The ears of Cattiva are triangular and inside of its ears are black. Cattiva has an underbelly that is cream. Cattiva often is seen in a confident expression and has bright blue eyes. When its mouth is open, two fangs can be seen inside Cattiva's mouth.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
• In the wild this Pal will run away if it spots the player.
• While working at the player's base this Pal tends to slack off a lot, take breaks frequently, and sometimes when angry will even go to bed in the middle of the day.

sections.text: • Windswept Hills

sections.heading: Dungeons

Passive Skill:

Work Suitability:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• "Cattiva" may come from "cat" and the Italian word "cattiva," meaning bad.
• Its Japanese name may partially come from 突っ張り tsuppari, this term and its relatives can mean multiple things like a slapping attack (in the context of sumo wrestling) similar to Cattiva's Punch Flurry and also being generally defiant or unruly, which might be a reference to Cattiva's tendency to taunt and act tough. Additionally, its Japanese name likely combines the aforementioned concepts with にゃん nyan (onomatopoeia for the cry of a cat).
• Cattiva was featured in Paldeck No.008 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Some expeditions in Pal Expedition Station yield Cavern Mushrooms. A sufficiently strong expedition party sent to an early-game expedition can easily bring back 60 mushrooms.

Cawgnito (Japanese: マスクロウ Maskrow) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Cawgnito is a greyish-black avian Pal resembling a Corvidae bird species, such as a crow or raven. The skull-like ivory-colored face resembles a mask with a long beak, and the hollow black eyes feature large red pupils that gleam brightly. It has plumage that resembles a large hat on the top of the head. The wings, when folded, resemble that of a cloak or cape, and the inner parts of the wings are a deep red. A gold gem, perhaps suggesting a brooch, is featured on the chest. From the waist down, Cawgnito's feathers are white and rugged in contrast to the smooth black feathers of the upper half. Cawgnito sports tan legs with two clawed talons at the front and one at the back on each foot.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Cawgnito, being a nocturnal Pal, can only be found at night. Cawgnito will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon. It's generally a pacifist Pal minding its own business unless attacked.
If employed as a Base Pal, cawgnitos will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds for sleeping. If not tasked with anything, it'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Spawn during the night, and in the dungeons in Mount Obsidian.

Work Suitability:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• The face's design may be inspired by the mask of a plague doctor.
• Its name may come from the words incognito and caw.
• Its Japanese name may come from mask and crow.
• Cawgnito was featured in Paldeck No.036 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Ceiling Lamp is a lighting structure in Palworld.
Celaray (Japanese: ルミカイト Lumikite) is a Water elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Celaray is a pal resembling a flying manta ray. Its body is light blue with large wing-like pectoral fins around three times the size of its body in length. On each fin there is three white spots of varying sizes. The underside of Celaray is a lighter blue. Celaray has long cephalic fins on its head resembling rabbit ears, the tip of the cephalic fins are a light yellow. The eyes of Celaray are red and on each side next to the eyes feature two markings that are light yellow like the tips of the cephalic fins. The bottom of Celaray has small pelvic fins that resemble hands or feet.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
A very carefree Pal that is almost always found near beaches and shorelines, never straying too far from the sea. It is perpetually floating, though it never gets that high off the ground. It even sleeps while floating!
Wild Celaray are generally pacific and may fly away from the player, only resorting to aggressiveness when attacked.

sections.heading: Trivia

• Its name seems to come from the word accelerate and (manta) ray.
• Its Japanese name may come from luminous and kite.

Celesdir (Japanese: セレスディア Celesdir) is a Neutral elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Celesdir is a deer pal with white fur covering most of its upper body. Celesdir has a pair of dual-pronged, bronze-colored antlers with white markings underneath said antlers. It has a patch of grey fur between its magenta eyes, a long, flowing white mane flowing down its neck and back, and two long and thin, dangling flaps of fur underneath the antlers that reach to the base of its forelegs. Celesdir has a thick tuft of fur surrounding its chest and a excess fur stretching backwards from the base of its flank. Celesdir's lower body starts out grey and eventually becomes brown towards its hooves. There is a pattern on the thighs of its hind legs, showing its white fur underneath, and making Celesdir look like it's wearing leggings of sorts. It has two long, light blue, aura-esque ribbons that flow aimlessly near its short, pointed tail.

Characters refer to the people and/or various individuals that players can meet while playing Palworld.

images.caption: Default Female and Male player character

sections.heading: The Player Character
sections.text:
Upon loading into a world for the first time, the player will be able to customize their own unique character by picking from certain options of different hairstyles, face shapes, skin tones, and eyes, as well as sliders to adjust body shape and voice options.
Note: Once created, no changes can be made to the character's physical appearance. There will possibly be a feature added in a later patch to allow you to change this. As of v0.2.0.6, this has been changed. The apperance can now be changed using the Antique Dresser.
Player characters have the option to band together and create Guilds while exploring the Palpagos Islands.
Player characters also have the option to name their Pals, which is limited to 24 letters.

images.caption: The default player character's appearance in the trailers.
Two players by a campfire, from an early Unity-based build of the game

sections.heading: Side Characters
sections.text: NPCs are found all throughout the Palpagos Islands. They are usually found sitting next to a campfire or in settlements, but there are some soldiers and traders that will wander around certain areas.

sections.heading: Fellow Survivors
sections.text:
Depending on where the player chooses to begin their adventure, they can encounter a single person nearby who will give them basic advice about the world (such as large Pals, nighttime, or certain areas being dangerous), before handing over a small amount of material after the player talks to them:
• Expedition Survivor - if the player starts at Windswept Hills, Plateau of Beginnings
• Cowardly Islander - if the player starts at Sea Breeze Archipelago, Reef
• Mercenary with a Wounded Knee - if the player starts at Sea Breeze Archipelago, Castaway Beach
• Cowardly Victim - if the player starts at Marsh Island
• Desert Runaway - if the player starts at Eastern Wild Island
• Vagrant Challenger - if the player starts at Forgotten Island
• Resigned Islander - if the player starts at Ice Wind Island If the player starts at Windswept Hills, Flying Fish Coast, there will be no one nearby to interact with.
Note: Attacking a survivor will cause PIDF soldiers to suddenly spawn nearby and promptly attempt to shoot down the player.

sections.heading: Merchants
sections.text:
The Pal Merchant will allow you to sell Pals and buy Pals you may not already have. This will not include illegal pals from Restricted Zones. Can be found in Duneshelter, Fisherman's Point, Small Settlement or wandering in the world. 
Currently, Red Wandering Merchants are unable to inflict damage as a combatant & not useful for anything other than purchasing Items. this may be true for other Merchants as-well

sections.heading: Islanders and Settlers
sections.text:
Some of the locals can be found scattered around the Palpagos Islands. These NPCs generally only have dialogue, but occasionally, some will give the player items for speaking with them.

Chikipi (Japanese: タマコッコ Tamakokko) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Chikipi is a pal resembling a white chicken. Its body is vaguely egg-shaped and it has grey oval eyes. The beak of Chikipi is yellow and features a red wattle. The feet of Chikipi are orange with two clawed toes in the front and one toe at the back of the foot. The top of a Chikipi's head has a large comb that is red just like its waddle.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Chikipi may sporadically lay eggs on the ground, which can be picked up by the player. This Pal retaliates only when attacked, though neighboring Chikipi will become aggressive upon witnessing another Chikipi being harmed by the player.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Windswept Hills

sections.heading: Dungeons
sections.text: • Grass Dungeon Boss

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Egg Layer - Sometimes lays eggs when assigned to Ranch.
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
• Farming 1
Possible Drops: 
• Normal
 ◦ Egg
 ◦ Chikipi Poultry
• Boss
 ◦ Ancient Civilization Parts
 ◦ Egg 
 ◦ Chikipi Poultry
 ◦ Precious Plume 
 ◦ Ring of Resistance
Farming Produce 
• Egg

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• It was previously called Chickegg.
• Chikipi's name may come from “chicken”, “chick”, and “chickpea” due to this Pal's small size.
• Its Japanese name may come from 卵 tamago (egg) or simply 玉 tama (sphere/ball), and コッコ kokko (onomatopoeia for the sound a chicken makes).
• Chikipi has:
 ◦ The lowest stat total of all Pals, at 180.
 ◦ The lowest HP of all Pals at 60, tied with Fuack, Sparkit, Killamari, Tocotoco, Swee, and Flambelle.
• Chikipi was featured in Paldeck No.031 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: The size difference between a Lucky Chikipi and a normal Chikipi.

Chillet (Japanese: オコチョ Okocho) is an Ice / Dragon element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Chillet resembles a member of the Mustelid family crossed with a Wyrm, a long-bodied wingless dragon. Its body is cyan in color with a pale blue underbelly and dark blue markings, some of which resemble scales or ice crystals. This Pal possesses cheek tufts made of two distinct mini-tufts, and simple anime-styled eyes. It has long ears ending in the previously mentioned dark blue markings. Along the back there are several draconic spines.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Wild Chillet are found in groups near some snowy areas. This Pal is generally pacific and may run away from the player, only resorting to violence after being attacked.
Chillet can also be encountered as a Level 11 Alpha Pal in Windswept Hills.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Spawns in the cold area near the Free Pal Alliance tower or can be caught as a level 11 alpha near the starting spawn.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Chillet is a very useful companion in early-game, and remains useful throughout most playthroughs. Chillet's attacks are powerful for its relatively low starting level, and it can serve as a fast mount with its ability. An easy way to level up a Chillet is to go outside while it is night, as its attacks are strong against the numerous Dark types that appear at that time.
Partner Skill: 
• Wriggling Weasel - Can be ridden. (Requires Chillet Saddle → Unlocks at Lv. 13)
• Applies Dragon Damage to the player's attacks while mounted.
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
• Cooling 1
Possible Drops:
• Leather

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words chill and ferret combined.
 ◦ Okocho may be derived from 尾 o (tail) and kocho-kocho (onomatopoeia for tickling or fiddling).
• In both the Official Valentines Art Giveaway poll results and the concurrent unnofficial Palworld Reddit Community Poll, Chillet was voted the #1 favorite pal.
• If you use rocket slam while mounted and jump at the start or the middle of the attack it will cancel the animation and there will be no end lag, letting you fling yourself without the end lag.
• Summer Chillet was one of the three skins obtained from Palworld Twitch Drops. The other two are Golden Relaxaurus and Sailor Quivern.
• Chillet was featured in Paldeck No.062 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.file: Palworld Twitch main Chillet Quivern Relaxaurus.png

images.caption: A Chillet being used to cooldown a room of incubators

This Pal possesses cheek tufts made of two distinct mini-tufts, and simple anime-styled eyes. It has long ears ending in the previously mentioned dark pink markings. Along the back there are several dark pink markings.

Chromite is a material in Palworld.
Cinnamoth (Japanese: パピテフ Papitefu) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Cinnamoth is a sandy yellow colour, with a round head and body. It has long, oval shaped arms and legs, with a white-yellow fluffy collar around its neck. It has two tufts of fluff on both of its cheeks, and oval shaped emerald eyes. It has two feather like antenna, the antenna a sunny orange colour tipped with a maroon colour. The maroon colour is separated from the sunny orange colour with a white stripe. Cinnamoth have large insect wings. The top two wings are sunny orange in colour, with an 'm' shape that points inwards. These 'm' patterns are maroon in colour. There are white stripes on the edges of the top wings, the sides of the top wings a maroon colour. The lower wings are circle shapes, with two parts that stick outwards at the bottom. The lower wings are more of a sunny yellow colour with a maroon circle on the wings. There are white lines on the wings, with two circles that point downwards. The circle-like wings are lined with a maroon colour, the maroon colour ending mid way on the tails of the wings. The tails of the wings have white stripes on them, and end with a sunny yellow colour.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Cinnamoth are usually found in groups and will flee from the player on sight. These Pals will often use Poison Fog to damage opponents.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Mysterious Scales - When activated, attacks targeted enemy with Poison Fog.
Work Suitability:
• Planting 2
• Medicine Production 1
Possible Drops:
• Honey
• Lettuce Seeds
• Wheat Seeds

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words cinnamon and moth.
• Its Japanese name may come from the words Papillon and てふてふ tefutefu (Butterfly in historical kana orthography).

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Claw Crane is decorative piece of furniture in Palworld. It is unlocked as part of theAmusement Furniture Set and is a technology tier 41 item that costs 1 technology point to unlock.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: the Claw Crane is a stylised purple claw machine featuring a pile of Grizzbolt plushes on the inside of the machine. The back wall of the machine has a pattern of yellow lightning bolts against a purple backdrop, and the side of the machine includes stickers. The Claw Crane can not be used by the player, However, as it is just a decor item for a base.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• the Claw Crane plays a special looping sound effect once built, and this sound effect is unique to the claw crane
• The Grizzbolt plushes featured in the Claw Crane are the same ones seen hanging from Zoe’s waistband during the boss battle at the Tower of the Rayne Syndicate
Climbing is a mechanic in Palworld. It can be done on pretty much any vertical surface.

sections.heading: Mechanics
sections.text:
While climbing, the player can go up, down, left, and right. Moving costs stamina, and when the player runs out of stamina, they will lose grip and fall. Since stamina isn't gained while falling, it isn't possible to break the fall damage with a glider. It's possible to, however, use a Grappling Gun to clasp to a wall near the ground and reset the momentum.
Stamina isn't lost if the player isn't moving.

sections.heading: Abilities
sections.text:
While climbing, the inventory can be opened.
The player isn't able to open the build menu or throw Pal Spheres or captured Pals. However, the build menu can be opened before, allowing building placement during the climbing.

sections.heading: Uses
sections.text: Climbing can be used to avoid fall damage.

The Cloth Outfit is a type of body armor in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 4 for 2 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Cloth Outfit is a beginner armor piece that provides minimal protection as well as Lvl. 1 Cold Resistance. While it isn't the best armor for combat, its innate cold protection helps to keep the user warm during the cold nights in places like the Windswept Hills. However, it should not be used in especially cold places like the Frostbound Mountains.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Cloth Outfit is the first piece of body armor players can unlock.
• The Cloth Outfit's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Chillet boss.

Coarse Ammo is a type of ammo in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 21 for 1 Technology Point.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Coarse Ammo is used in the Musket and the Makeshift Handgun. It is fairly cheap to craft, though can be difficult for players without easy access to Gunpowder to make. Fortunately, Coarse Ammo can bought from many Wandering Merchants around the Palpagos Islands.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• Coarse Ammo is the only type of bullet used in more than one gun and one of the few ammo types used in more than one weapon, with the others being Arrows, Fire Arrows, and Poison Arrows.
• If you lack the means to craft or buy Coarse Ammo, it can also be found by killing Syndicate Thugs.

Cooking is a mechanic in Palworld that allows the player to create dishes using various ingredients.

sections.heading: About
sections.text:
By combining multiple ingredients together, the player can create new, more nutritious food items. These items also often last longer before they spoil.
Cooking can be done at any Cooking Station, with different stations having varying speeds. Higher tier cooking stations also unlock new recipes.
Slow; Campfires
Faster; Cooking Pots,
Fastest; Electric Kitchens
These Cooking stations can be used by the player or by any Pal with a Kindling Work Suitability.
The higher a Pal's Work Speed, and level of Work Suitability in Kindling, the faster the Cooking Process will be.

Cooking Pot is a structure in Palworld that can be used to cook dishes.
It is a tier 17 Technology and costs 2 points to unlock.
It cooks faster than the Campfire and, unlike the campfire, does not set the player or Pals on fire if they stand on it.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: The Cooking Pot is used to craft foods.

Cooler is a structure in Palworld.
It's a tier 18 technology item and requires 2 point to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: Coolers require a pal with Cooling. The level most likely does not matter, as far as I see. Coolers, like heaters, are used to help with incubating Pal Eggs in an Egg Incubator. While heaters are more efficient at this, it can be useful if you have a pal that keeps trying to heat the heater near the Egg Incubator. Keep in mind they have a small cooling radius. It can also help keep you cool in a volcanic base, although the small radius makes only the Electric Cooler viable. Useless if you don't live there at high levels due to the Electric Egg Incubator.

Cooler Box is an item in Palworld.
It's a tier 13 technology item and requires 2 point to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: Used to store up to 10 Items. When maintained by a Pal with the Cooling Work Suitability, keeps food products fresh from decaying.

is the work suitability of a certain specific pal to cool down certain facilities.
When assigned to the Cooler Box or Refrigerator, the skill level will extend a food items expiration timer. The Refrigerator however only has increased storage capacity over the Cooler Box, and does not further extend a food items timer. Both items also do not drain a Pals Sanity meter, and they will in fact regain Sanity back as they work on it. The Cooler however does still drain the Sanity meter. 
Work Speed buffs do affect the extent of cooling when working at a Cooler Box or Refrigerator, but do not affect the Cooler at all.
The duration food items are extended to per Cooling skill level start at 200 Minutes, and each level afterwards adds a further 200 minutes;
• Level 1 = 200 Minutes
• Level 2 = 400 Minutes
• Level 3 = 600 Minutes
• Level 4 = 800 Minutes
• Level 5 = 1000 Minutes*
With Artisan, Serious, Lucky and Work Slave, these are increased to;
• 420 / 860 / 1280 / 1720 / 2140
• Available only to the Frostallion through the Pal Condenser.
The Cooler is used to lower the temperature in a fixed area that appears to be around 15 meters in radius. The Cooler however does not have stacking effects, having more than one Cooler in a spot will not lower the temperature in that area any more than having only one. The Cooling skill also does not affect this, the temperature will not go any lower regardless of number or Cooling skill level, nor does the radius of a Cooler extend. This also applies to the Egg Incubator, only one Cooler will affect it where having more than one will do nothing, and Cooling skill level does not affect it in any way. The distance at which the Incubator is placed does not matter.
As it is considered a neutral temperature space, the Cooler does not affect an enclosed, indoors room or building, it does however interestingly, still cool the area outdoors of this space, even through walls, and will affect any incubators in this outdoors area.
To manage food spoilage effectively, it's important to keep an Ice-type Pal dedicated to the container. Using Vanwyrm Cryst is recommended due to its nocturnal nature. The introduction of the Monitoring Stand makes it significantly easier to assign Pals, ensuring they remain stationary and allowing for more efficient management and placement. Additionally, you can also reset the food timer by sorting the container, which extends the duration.

Crafting gear and items will be an important mechanic in Palworld.

sections.heading: Crafting Workbenches
sections.text: In the game, there are several types of workbenches, each with its unique functions and crafting capabilities. Here's a comprehensive list of the workbenches and the items they can produce:

sections.heading: Workbenches
sections.text:
• This is the basic crafting station for entry-level items.
• Crafts items like basic torches, axes, and clubs using simple materials like wood and stone.
• Higher levels can be swapped out for production assembly lines that can produce higher level items.

sections.heading: Weapon Workbenches
sections.text:
• Specializes in crafting basic melee and ranged weapons.
• Crafts swords, spears, and bows using materials like metal, wood, leather, and gunpowder.
• Higher levels can be swapped out for weapon assembly lines.

sections.heading: Medicine Workbench
sections.text:
• Focuses on crafting medical items.
• Crafts life-saving potions, bandages, and antidotes using natural ingredients like herbs, mushrooms, and honey.
• Higher levels can be swapped out for electric versions.

sections.heading: Furnaces
sections.text:
Primitive Furnace
• Uses heat to process raw materials.
• Produces charcoal and ingots from wood and ore.
• Higher levels can be swapped out for electric versions.
Improved Furnace
• Allows refinement of Refined Ingot.
Electric Furnace
• Allows refinement of Pal Metal Ingot and Plasteel.
• Requires Generating Electricity.
Gigantic Furnace
• Allows refinement of Hexolite.
• Requires electricity.
• Allows for up to 4 Pals to work simultaneously, increasing crafting speed.

sections.heading: Crusher
sections.text:
• Breaks down wood and stone.
• Produces Fiber and Paldium Fragments.

sections.heading: Repair Bench
sections.text:
• Repairs damaged equipment and tools.
• Repairs items like swords, armor, and pickaxes using materials like metal, wood, leather, cloth, and repair kits.

sections.heading: Pal Gear Workbench
sections.text:
• Enables the creation of Pal specific items.
• Crafts gloves, saddles, and weapons to use your Pal's special ability.

sections.heading: Sphere Workbench
sections.text:
