sections.text: Crime is a central mechanic in Palworld, introducing a layer of challenge and consequence to the player's freedom of action. While the game encourages exploration and creativity, engaging in illegal activities triggers the intervention of the Palpagos Island Defense Force (PIDF), making crime a high-risk venture. The "Criminal Activity Underway" warning serves as the game’s way of informing players when they’ve crossed legal boundaries, offering a brief chance to cease unlawful actions before facing the consequences.

sections.heading: Mechanics of Crime
sections.text: The crime system in Palworld revolves around player actions being witnessed by NPCs. The system is designed to simulate a realistic response to illegal activities, with varying consequences based on the severity of the offense. Here’s how it works:

sections.heading: Witnessing and Charges
sections.text:
• Requirement for Witnesses: For a crime to result in charges, a non-hostile NPC (such as an islander or PIDF officer) must witness the act. If no NPC sees the player, no charges are incurred.
 ◦ Example: Attacking an NPC who hasn’t seen you yet or capturing a Pal in a Wildlife Sanctuary without being detected will not trigger charges.
• Escalation: If witnessed, the game tracks the severity of the crime and assigns charges accordingly, triggering a response from the PIDF.

sections.heading: The PIDF Response
sections.text:
• Hostility Trigger: Once charges are assigned, PIDF officers begin spawning nearby to pursue and attack the player.
• Dynamic Spawning: The PIDF will continue spawning as long as the player is wanted. If the player moves far enough away, the current officers will despawn, but new ones will spawn near the player shortly after.
• Scaling Response: The number and level of PIDF officers scale with the severity of the player’s crimes and the accumulated charges.

sections.heading: Criminal Activities
sections.text:
The following actions are considered crimes in Palworld:
• Assault: Attacking non-hostile NPCs, such as merchants or islanders, constitutes assault. This includes attempting to capture humans using Pal Spheres.
 ◦ Note: If a player’s Pals deal damage to NPCs, it does not count as assault.
• Trespassing: Entering Wildlife Sanctuaries without permission triggers a level 1 Trespassing charge.
• Poaching: Capturing Pals within Wildlife Sanctuaries leads to a level 1 Poaching charge.

sections.heading: Criminal Activity Warning
sections.text: • "Criminal Activity Underway": This warning appears when the player engages in a potentially illegal act. It serves as a notification rather than an immediate punishment. Players can cease the illegal activity (e.g., stop attacking or leave a restricted area) to avoid escalation. The warning disappears after a short period if no further illegal actions are taken.

sections.heading: Consequences of Crime
sections.text: If a player continues their illegal activities despite the warning, the situation escalates into the "Wanted" status. This marks the point of no return, as the PIDF actively hunts the player.

sections.heading: Wanted Status
sections.text:
• Trigger: The "Wanted" status activates when the player persists in committing crimes or directly engages with PIDF officers.
• Aggression: PIDF guards will spawn aggressively and attack the player on sight.
• Combat with PIDF: Players can fight back, but this increases the Wanted level. However, using Pals to fight the PIDF does not raise the level, offering a strategic workaround.

sections.heading: Removing the Wanted Status
sections.text:
Players have several options to clear their charges and escape the PIDF’s pursuit:
• Death: Allowing the PIDF to defeat you results in charges being cleared upon respawn.
 ◦ Multiplayer Exception: In multiplayer sessions, being downed (but not killed) is sufficient to remove charges.
• Evade and Wait: Losing sight of the PIDF and remaining hidden long enough causes charges to dissipate naturally.
• Save and Reload: In single-player mode, saving the game, exiting, and logging back in resets the Wanted status.

sections.heading: Navigating Crime: Tips and Tricks
sections.text:
• Avoid Detection: 
 ◦ Use stealth to capture Pals or attack NPCs without being seen.
 ◦ Plan your actions carefully in areas with high NPC activity.
• Strategic Combat:
 ◦ Use Pals to fight PIDF officers instead of engaging directly to avoid escalating charges.
 ◦ Equip strong Pals to withstand waves of PIDF enemies.
• Escape Routes: 
 ◦ Familiarize yourself with the terrain to evade pursuing officers effectively.
 ◦ Utilize mounts or Pals with high mobility to outrun the PIDF.
• Minimize Risk:
 ◦ Avoid Wildlife Sanctuaries unless necessary, as these areas automatically trigger charges.
 ◦ Refrain from targeting merchants or essential NPCs to maintain access to services.

images.caption: An indicator that shows the player is Wanted for three charges of Assault.

sections.text: The Crossbow is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 13 for 2 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Like the Old Bow and Three Shot Bow, the Crossbow is a good early-game ranged weapon that fires Arrows. However, it deals much higher damage per shot and can hit targets from further away, though this increase in power comes at the cost of a longer reload time.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: • The Crossbow's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Bushi boss.

Crouching is a mechanic in Palworld that lets players crouch.
At low speeds, activating crouching will cause the player to crouch. At higher speeds or on a slope, the player will start to slide.

sections.heading: Crouching
sections.text:
When crouched, the player moves at a slower speed. Since the player is lower to the ground, it can be used to dodge attacks from enemies.
To stop crouching, the player can roll, jump, or mount a pal. Using a grappling gun will not cause the player to stop crouching.

sections.heading: Sliding
sections.text:
When sliding, the player moves in a straight line until they lose enough velocity or the sliding is interrupted. If the player is going down a slope, they will accelerate. You can still slide uphill after sprinting, but will quickly lose momentum and stop sliding. If the player stops sliding due to low momentum, they will end up in a crouched position.
The player can interrupt sliding by rolling or jumping. Jumping will allow the player to keep their momentum. This can be used to glide at high velocities. After rolling or jumping, the player won't be crouched and instead can continue walking.
While sliding, the player can still throw Pal Spheres and use weapons.

Cryolinx Terra (Japanese: グランドラー Groundrar) is a Ground elemental Pal and a sub-species of Cryolinx.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Cryolinx Terra is a tall, lizard-like Pal with a slim build. It has a flat head, with the sclera in its eyes appearing black. The eye colour itself is golden, with slitted black pupils. It has two golden fangs that point downwards, appearing like large triangles. Sticking out from the sides of its mouth are long, golden spikes. On the top of it head, it has a spiked stripe running from nose tip to tail tip. It has horizontal spiked stripes running across the vertical line. Near the end of its head are two long, tall mud brown spikes tipped with gold. It has a pair of long, mud brown arms. These arms have three horizontal gold stripe patterns on the back, with golden spikes sticking out of the stripes. It has three long, sharp golden claws on both hands, having a white, paw-like pattern on the hands. It has a tall, lanky mud brown body with a white underbelly. It has short, stocky legs that are mud brown in colour. It has a golden stripe pattern on both legs, golden spikes sticking out of the legs. It has a golden band around both legs, the legs ending with three long, sharp claws. It has a long tail that points up, the tail covered with golden spikes. These spikes don't sit on the top, but rather the sides.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This is an aggressive Pal that will attack the player on sight.

sections.text: Daedream (Japanese: ネムラム Nemuram) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Daedream is a floating imp-like Pal that is black to dark purple in colour. It has two long ears and two small blue horns on the top of its head. The eyes are a reddish-pink with light eyelashes and are shaped to appear sinister- or malicious-looking, while its mouth bears a couple of exposed fangs. The hands and feet are of a lighter purple that somewhat resemble hooves. Around its neck features a woolly collar that is blue, like its horns. The flowing, woolly hair of Daedream is bright and colourful, featuring a cosmic starry pattern that moves and illuminates in varying gradients of pink, blue, and purple. This hair is clearly visible during the deep nighttime hours when the Pal is active (it ignores any lighting effects), extending to over twice the length of this Pals body.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal, being a nocturnal species, can only be found at night or in dungeons. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon. They are generally quite docile, curious creatures until attacked.
If employed as a Base Pal, Daedreams will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate them, as they never sleep. If not tasked with anything, they'll simply wander about the base playfully. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals. 
If following the player around via Daedream's Necklace, there will be occasions where Daedream's model will interfere with the placement of facilities and structures, so until a future fix is added that removes model collision during this state, or something along those lines, it is advised that a player put away his/her Daedream from the party until after construction is finished.
At the moment, it is not actually able to put players or other Pals to sleep, as its entry claims.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Dream Chaser - While in team, appears near the player. Follows up player attacks with magic bullets. (Requires Daedream's Necklace→ Unlocks at Lv. 8)
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 1
• Transporting 1
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Venom Gland
• Small Pal Soul

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:

• Its Japanese name may come from 眠り nemuri (sleep) and ram.
• Daedream was featured in Paldeck No.029 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Daedream's Necklace is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 8 Technology item and requires 2 points to unlock. It is crafted in the Pal Gear Workbench.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
It is used to unlock Daedream's Partner Skill, Dream Chaser. 
If a player is carrying a full party of Daedreams, only one of these is actually needed to have all five out at once; crafting five separate necklaces for each one is not required.
Passive skills on the Daedream(s) that boost the player, such as Motivational Leader (+25% to player Work Speed), will be applied.

Dark is one of 9 elements found in Palworld.
Pals of this elemental type are usually nocturnal; meaning they naturally spawn during the night, do not need to sleep and will continue to work in bases day and night (exceptions to this rule exist, such as Lovander who is a Neutral type but nocturnal). As a result, this makes this type more susceptible to Sanity drain.

sections.heading: Generic properties
sections.text:
Pals working at the player's base with the Dark element will keep working during the night, whilst others go to sleep, and so they don't need beds unless they're overworked. 
As for the current version of the game, they do not go to sleep during the day time,leaving them prone to being overworked.
Unlike other elements, which resist the element they're strong against, Dark does not resist Neutral.
Dark Pals are immune to the Blind status effect.

sections.heading: Battle Properties
sections.text: It is strong against Neutral Pals and weak to Dragon Skills.

Dazzi (Japanese: カミナラシ Kaminarashi) is an Electric element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Dazzi is a lavender-coloured oni- or jinn-like Pal. It is rather small, roughly 80-90 cm tall compared to a player character, possessing a slender humanoid body with two arms which end in hands and no visible legs. Around their chest is either a purple bandeau or a stripe that resembles one. Its lower half from the hips down is obscured by an amorphous white cloud. Whether or not it possesses legs hidden in the cloud or not is unknown/up to interpretation. The head is fairly large and round, dominated by white hair matching the colour of their clouds. The back of the hair features a ponytail that somewhat resembles a lightning bolt. On the top of its head, a single purple horn can be seen jutting out through the white hair, the fringe of which obscures the eyes.
Dazzi is constantly depicted with a grinning expression displayed on its face, often putting their hands toward their mouths, serving to emphasize their impish and mischievous nature.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal is quite curious by nature and will often follow around any players caught within their field of vision for a while. Care must be taken when attacking a group of these, as the pressure from their various area-of-effect skills will build up quite fast if the player isn't tactful or quick enough; at the worst case scenario, a group of hostile Dazzis can effectively paralyze-lock a player with repeated electric attacks, whittling the player's health down while doing so.
If following the player around via Dazzi's Necklace, there will be occasions where Dazzi's model will interfere with the placement of facilities and structures, so until a future fix is added that removes model collision during this state, or something along those lines, it is advised that a player put away his/her Dazzi from the party until after construction is finished.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• During the game's early development, Dazzi was referred to as "Raijindaughter"; Raijin (also called Raiden) being the Shinto deity of lightning and thunder, who was sometimes depicted with a chariot or lower half made of clouds. This placeholder name survived in the form of the Japanese name of Dazzi's partner skill, Kaminarimusume (literally "lightning daughter", where "lightning" could also be read as "thunder god"), which in turn was reinterpreted in the English locale as "Lady of Lightning".
• Its Japanese name may come from 雷 kaminari (lightning) and 嵐 arashi (storm)
• Dazzi was featured in Paldeck No.035 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Dazzi Noct (Japanese: ヤミナラシ Yaminarashi) is a Dark and Electric elemental Pal and a sub-species of Dazzi.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Dazzi Noct is a humanoid Pal, resembling a young girl. It has a circular head, its head snow white in colour. Its eyes are covered by the hair that grows on its head, the hair pitch black in colour and tied in what looks like a ponytail. On the top of its head is a short, nubby golden horn. All that's seen on Dazzi Noct's face is a grin that one can only assume as evil. Around its thin torso, Dazzi Noct has a golden band. The rest of its body is hidden inside a puffy, black stormcloud.

Depresso (Japanese: ンダコアラ Ndakoala) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Depresso is a gloomy-looking pal resembling a bipedal navy blue mammal. It always is seen with a grouchy expression with lavender eyes. It has short closed ears that that have dark purple tips. Around its neck it has a dark blueish-grey pattern. The tail of Depresso is bushy in appearance and like the neck pattern also is blueish grey. The top of Depresso's head features a large tuft of dark purple fur.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
• Depressos can only be found during the night.
• Wild Depressos will immediately attack whenever a player lands in their line of sight, but will run away if the player is accompanied by stronger Boss/Shiny Pal party member. 
• As a Base Pal, like all nocturnal Pals, Depressos will still continue working even during the night time.

Digtoise (Japanese: ドリタス Dritoise) is a Ground element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Digtoise is a quadruped with a robust, armored appearance. Its primary body color is a brownish-orange, with a cream-colored underbelly. The eyes are a vivid green, set within a sharply contoured face that features a pointed snout.
Its most distinctive feature is the heavy shell, which is a dark hue, almost black. The shell is outfitted with several silver, triangular spikes that mimic the look of metallic drills. Its sturdy limbs end in broad paws, each equipped with dark claws. The overall design merges organic and mechanical aesthetics, giving Digtoise an imposing and battle-ready look.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Digtoise exhibit a violent temperament, often attacking players for just wandering near them.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Drill Crusher - When activated, performs Shell Spin, follows the player while spinning, and mines ores efficiently. (Requires Digtoise's Headband→ Unlocks at Lv. 19)
Work Suitability: 
• Mining 3
Possible Drops:
• Ore
• High Quality Pal Oil

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name is a combination of the words dig and tortoise.
• Its Japanese name may come from drill and tortoise.
• Its design and rapid spinning may be a reference to Gamera, the iconic turtle kaiju who may have also inspired Bowser.
 ◦ Its description as "a walking contradiction" is a reference to a Chinese legend of a merchant who sold what he claimed to be an indestructible shield and an unblockable spear. When someone asked what would happen if the two items were turned on each other, the merchant confessed his fraud. This story is the etymological basis for the Mandarin word for "contradiction".
• Digtoise was featured in Paldeck No.023 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
• Digtoise is planned to appear in Terraria as part of the Palworld x Terraria Collab.

images.caption: thumb|A (named) Digtoise enjoying the Hot Spring.
An animation of Digtoise in Terraria.

sections.text: Digtoise's Headband is a key item in Palworld.
Dinossom (Japanese: アロアリュー Aroaryū) is a Grass / Dragon element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Dinossom is a bipedal dinosaur-like pal. It is green with a darker green pattern on its back. Its underbelly is white in appearance. Dinossom has antennae that are long and curly. The most distinctive feature of Dinossom is the large spotted starfish shaped flower on the top of its head that is pink with a yellow crown shaped center and four dark green leaves underneath. It has arms and legs that are tipped with three pinkish red claws on each limb. Dinossom has innocent looking eyes that are pink. Like its antennae, the tail of Dinossom is long and curly and running along the tail can be seen scales that resemble sprouts.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Dinossom will be curious towards the player by default, with all nearby Dinossom becoming aggressive towards the player if attacked. In some areas it usually spawns in pairs.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Fragrant Dragon
• Can be ridden. Enhances Grass attacks while mounted. (Requires Dinossom Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 19)
Work Suitability: 
• Planting 2
• Lumbering 2
Possible Drops:
• Wheat Seeds

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Dinossom name may come from the words dinosaur and blossom.
• While Dinossom is seen working at a bench in early trailers, in its release it cannot do so as it's only capable of Planting and Lumbering.
• Dinossom was featured in Paldeck No.011 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Dinossom Lux (Japanese: ビリビリュー Biribiryū) is the Electric element variation of Dinossom.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Dinossom Lux is a bipedal dinosaur-like pal. Unlike its counterpart Dinossom, it is yellow with a darker yellow pattern on its back. Its underbelly is white in appearance. Dinossom has antennae that are long and curly. The most distinctive feature of Dinossom Lux is the large, pink, spotted starfish shaped flower on the top of its head with a brown crown shaped center and four dark brown leaves underneath. It has arms and legs that are tipped with three pinkish red claws on each limb. Dinossom has innocent looking eyes that are pink. Like its antennae, the tail of Dinossom Lux is long and curly and running along the tail can be seen scales that resemble sprouts.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Dinossom Lux are usually pacifistic. This Includes the Alpha Dinossum Lux, allowing the player to loot the cave without a fight if they so choose.

Direhowl (Japanese: ガウルフ Gaurufu) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Direhowl is a wolf-like Pal. It has a black furred body, with a long white and fluffy tail. Its underbelly is white in colour. Its legs are rather thin, white in colour. It has three black claws on each leg. It has a big, fluffy white mane, and a black head. The inside of its ears are a pink colour and a triangle shaped black nose. It has narrowed yellow eyes, giving it a mean look. It has a white snout, white fur on its face giving it a mask-like look.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Typically, a violent Pal that will attack the player on sight, emitting loud snarls as a sign of hostility. In the wild, Direhowl hunt smaller Pals such as Flopie and Jolthog Cryst in packs, encircling the prey.
In addition to its hunting behavior, this Pal will often show up to scavenge the remains of both Pals and humans shortly after conflicts conclude.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Direhowl Rider - While in team, can be summoned and be ridden on. Moves slightly faster than most mounts. (Requires Direhowl's Saddled Harness→ Unlocks at Lv. 9)
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Leather - (1-2) 100%
• Ruby - 3%
• Gold Coin - (10-20) 3%

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This Pal is blamed for the lack of humans in the Eastern Wild Island.
• Direhowl name appears to be a play on the name dire wolf, an extinct species of wolf, and the word howl.
• The Japanese name may be a mix of がるる　garuru (Japanese onomatopoeia for the sound a wolf makes), and 負 fu which means Negative in Japanese, or possibly wolf.
• Direhowl was featured in Paldeck No.076 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Dr. Brawn is an NPC that has a chance to spawn in most incident zones throughout the map. 
Once an in-game day, with a pal active, Dr. Brawn will perform an experiment on it, with 2 following results:
Positive
• +15 IVs to all stats (if there are already 4 positive passives on the pal)
• +15 IVs to all stats and adds any* positive passive (If there is at least one negative passive on the pal, Dr. Brawn will prioritize and replace the negative passive with a positive one)
• Does NOT include exclusive passives [Lucky, Legend, 20% and 30% elemental passives], however, DOES have a chance to add rainbow passives
Negative
• -30 IVs to all stats (If there are already 4 negative passives on the pal)
• -30 IVs to all stats and adding any negative passive (prioritizes replacing a positive passive with a negative)
Spawn Code
PalPassive_Doctor - Character ID

Dumud (Japanese: トドドドン Todododon) is a Ground and Water element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Dumud is a greyish brown fish-like pal that bears resemblance to both a mudskipper and blobfish. Its body shape is prominently amorphous in appearance. It has a perpetually pained looking expression with vacant eyes and a wide mouth with four visible fangs. It has large pectoral fins and a tail fin that it uses to support itself and move with. The top of its head has a single dorsal fin present. From the middle of its eyes to its stomach, Dumud has a peach colored underside.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Dumud are generally passive, acting curious towards the player, but will retaliate when struck.

Dungeons are semi-randomly spawned locations within the world. There are only a set amount of them open at any one time; when one closes, another one opens up somewhere else.
Dungeons come in hidden tiers, with each dungeon sharing its own pool of possible Alpha Pals that can spawn inside, depending on the biome the dungeon is located at. At the end of each dungeon is one guaranteed Alpha Pal, alongside two guaranteed chests.
There is no time limit on completing dungeons. If the player dies within a dungeon, the game will boot him/her out and open the map, prompting a choice of respawn location. The player's belongings will be dropped at the dungeon's entrance for their owner to pick up.
There is a bug which occurs where, if the Alpha Pal encountered isn't the desired species, a player can walk all the way back to the dungeon's entrance (but not outright leave), then return to the boss room, and it will be replaced with another Alpha Pal from the same dungeon pool.
A few Pals can only be found by venturing into dungeons, such as Mau and Killamari. It is also possible to find Alphas of certain species during the times of day they are not normally available.

Early Access 0.1.0.0 (Xbox), also known as the Early Access Release, was a major release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 22nd of January 2024.

sections.heading: Xbox Update
sections.text:
• An update has just been released for the Xbox and Microsoft Store PC versions.
• Numerous bugs have been fixed, including sound issues.

sections.heading: Known Bugs
sections.text: The following bugs have been identified in this version and we are currently working on a fix. We apologize for inconvenience.

sections.heading: Xbox
sections.text: • The game launches to a static black screen.

sections.heading: Microsoft Store
sections.text:
• The game launches to a static black screen.
• The control pad is not recognized. 
• The key guide display is not displayed correctly.
• There is no button to quit the game.

Early Access 0.1.1.0 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 23rd of January 2024.

sections.heading: Xbox Update
sections.text:
• Updates have been released for Xbox and Microsoft Store PC versions. 
• This update fixes some of the previously mentioned issues.
[The following issues have been fixed]
Xbox
• When starting the game app, it goes black and cannot proceed further.

sections.heading: Microsoft Store
sections.text:
• When starting the game app, it goes black and cannot proceed further. 
• Controller is not recognized 
• Key guide display is not displayed correctly 
• Exit game button is not implemented

Early Access 0.1.1.1 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 26th of January 2024.

sections.heading: Changelog
sections.text:
• Fixed a bug where the loading screen did not end when logging into the world.
• Fixed a bug where the world date was not displayed correctly and appeared to reset.
• Corrected various incorrect text.
• Countermeasures against various cheats and exploits.

Early Access 0.1.1.2 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 29th of January 2024.

sections.heading: Xbox / Microsoft Store Update
sections.text: • Fixed some issues which negatively affected game stability.

Early Access 0.1.1.3 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 1st of February 2024.

sections.heading: Major fixes
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where the game would crash under certain conditions
• Fixed an issue where if another player’s pal/base pal had HP 30% or less, it could be captured by using a sphere.
• Fixed an issue where enemy pals would get stuck in walls due to charge attacks.
• Implemented the first fix for an issue where the game would always crash and save data would be corrupted when the guild’s total number of pals captured reached around 7000.
• The game no longer crashes even if the total number of captured objects exceeds 7000. However, save data that has already been in this state (in the case of a server, the server’s world data) prior to this update will still be unable to load. We are continuing to work on fixing this issue permanently.

sections.heading: Key configuration
sections.text:
• Added support for mouse side keys and numeric keypad in keyboard key configuration.
• Further improvements are currently planned, such as support for interact key configuration.

sections.heading: Player issues
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where players on dedicated servers and co-op (online) were receiving damage twice.
• Specifications have been changed so that you can move at extremely low speed even when you have exceeded the weight limit.
• Players will no longer pass through walls when dismounting.
• Fixed an issue where capture power strengthened by Lifmunk Effigies was reset when using memory reset drugs.
• Fixed an issue where armor could be equipped in the wrong slot.
• Adjusted camera positions of several Pals to make it easier to see when mounted.

sections.heading: Base issues
sections.text:
• Specifications have been changed so that Pals who are manually assigned to a breeding farm will not become hungry and their manual assignments will not be removed.
• The key to lift Pals has been changed from F → V (Pad: X → Y) in order to prevent accidents.
• Reduced the speed and range of fire spreading on wooden buildings.
• Fixed an issue where manual work assignments could not be set at all under certain circumstances.
• Fixed an issue where extremely high-level Relaxaurus would attack the base during raids.
• Added several measures to prevent base Pals from getting stuck.
• Fixed an issue where Pals would get stuck while transporting items and would drop them on the spot.
• Fixed an issue where Pals at the base would continue to cut down trees that were already cut.
• Fixed an issue where Pals at the base were on the verge of death due to unexplained falling damage.
• Fixed an issue where Pals at the base would float under some conditions.
• Fixed an issue where if a farm was built on the second floor or above, work pals would be unable to move on the floors below.

sections.heading: Other
sections.text:
• Corrected incorrect text
• Measures have been added to prevent cheating and stealing other players’ Pals.

Early Access 0.1.1.4 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 7th of February 2024.

sections.heading: Major fixes
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where the game would always crash and save data would be corrupted when the total number of Pals captured by the guild reached approximately 7000.
• In the previous patch, save data that had already been in this state (for servers, the server's world data) remained in a broken state that made it impossible to load, but after applying this patch it will be resolved and will load properly.
• Fixed an issue where some weapons equipped by other players would disappear when a player used a grenade in multiplayer.
• Fixed an issue where, although the displayed capture probability increased when the capture power was strengthened with Lifmunk Effigies, the capture probability did not actually increase at all due to an internal processing bug.

sections.heading: Base related
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where if a Pal that was manually assigned to a breeding farm went to sleep, it would not wake up forever.
• Fixed an issue where no wood would drop when Pal at the base felled a tree.

sections.heading: Others
sections.text: • Implemented countermeasures against some cheats and exploits.

Early Access 0.1.2.0, also known as the Early Access Release, was a major release of Palworld.
It was released on 19th of January 2024.
This version of Palworld was mainly about fixing bugs and general improvements. It was released on 25th of January 2024.

sections.heading: Improvements
sections.text:
• Countermeasures against various cheats and exploits.
• Corrected various incorrect text.

sections.heading: Fixes
sections.text:
• Fixed a bug where the loading screen did not end when logging into the world.
• Fixed a bug where the world date was not displayed correctly and appeared to reset.
This version of Palworld was mainly about fixing some major bugs and fixes, such as base issues and player issues. It was released the 1st of February 2024.

 ◦ However, save data that has already been in this state (in the case of a server, the server's world data) prior to this update will still be unable to load.
 ◦ We are continuing to work on fixing this issue permanently.

sections.heading: Key Configuration

 ◦ Further improvements are currently planned, such as support for interact key configuration.

• Fixed an issue where capture power strengthened by Lifmunk Effigies was reset when using memory reset drugs. 
• Fixed an issue where armor could be equipped in the wrong slot. 
• Adjusted camera positions of several Pals to make it easier to see when mounted.

sections.heading: Base Issues
sections.text:
• Specifications have been changed so that Pals who are manually assigned to a breeding farm will not become hungry and their manual assignments will not be removed. 
• The key to lift Pals has been changed from F → V (Pad: X → Y) in order to prevent accidents. 
• Reduced the speed and range of fire spreading on wooden buildings. 
• Fixed an issue where manual work assignments could not be set at all under certain circumstances. 
• Fixed an issue where extremely high-level Relaxaurus would attack the base during raids. 
• Added several measures to prevent base Pals from getting stuck. 
• Fixed an issue where Pals would get stuck while transporting items and would drop them on the spot. 
• Fixed an issue where Pals at the base would continue to cut down trees that were already cut. 
• Fixed an issue where Pals at the base were on the verge of death due to unexplained falling damage.

• Fixed an issue where if a farm was built on the second floor or above, work pals would be unable to move on the floors below.

sections.heading: Other
sections.text:
• Corrected incorrect text 
• Measures have been added to prevent cheating and stealing other players' Pals. 
• Many other minor fixes such as the addition of a UI key guide.

sections.heading: Unlisted Changes
sections.text: 0.1.4.0 contains several changes which were not listed in the official Patch Notes above.

sections.heading: Pal Active Skills
sections.text:
• The values for several Pal Active Skills were changed:
 ◦ Bee Quiet: Type changed from Neutral to Grass, Power changed from 200 to 250.
 ◦ Implode: Power changed from 180 to 230.
 ◦ Megaton Implode: Power changed from 250 to 500.
 ◦ Sand Blast: Cooldown changed from 5 to 4, Power changed from 25 to 40.
 ◦ Stone Blast: Power changed from 45 to 55.
 ◦ Wind Cutter: Power changed from 50 to 30.
 ◦ Forceful Charge: Cooldown changed from 25 to 28, Power changed from 90 to 120.
 ◦ Mystic Whirlwind: Cooldown changed from 15 to 10.
This version of Palworld was mainly about fixing some more things. It was released the 7th of February 2024.

sections.heading: Major Fixes
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where the game would always crash and save data would be corrupted when the total number of Pals captured by the guild reached approximately 7000. 
 ◦ In the previous patch, save data that had already been in this state (for servers, the server's world data) remained in a broken state that made it impossible to load, but after applying this patch it will be resolved and will load properly. 
• Fixed an issue where some weapons equipped by other players would disappear when a player used a grenade in multiplayer. 
• Fixed an issue where, although the displayed capture probability increased when the capture power was strengthened with Lifmunk Effigies, the capture probability did not actually increase at all due to an internal processing bug.

sections.heading: Base related
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where if a Pal that was manually assigned to a breeding farm went to sleep, it would not wake up forever. 
• Fixed an issue where no wood would drop when Pal at the base felled a tree.

sections.heading: Others
sections.text: • Implemented countermeasures against some cheats and exploits.

sections.heading: February 8th 2024 Hotfix
sections.text: • Improved optimization processing.
This version of Palworld was a large update to fix a lot of things. It was released the 27th of February 2024.

sections.heading: Major Fixes
sections.text:
• Implemented backup of save data on the world selection screen. 
• Resolved issue of unnecessary data accumulating in save data.

sections.heading: Key Configuration
sections.text: • Added fully supported keyboard key configuration

sections.heading: Guild
sections.text: • Modified specification to allow guild members to be removed when not logged in.

sections.heading: Player Issues
sections.text:
• Changed long-press operation during player tasks to allow continuous work by pressing the work button once (This is switchable in the options settings)
• Fixed issue where riding in prohibited hunting areas would not result in a crime.
• Improved interaction accuracy with adjacent objects.

sections.heading: Pals
sections.text:
• Changed specifications so that when a Pal is instructed to "attack aggressively," the Pal will attack enemies indiscriminately even if they are not in combat.
• Corrected an issue to allow Pals of rank 1 or higher to be selected as concentrated material (higher-ranked Pals yield more points when concentrated)
• Fixed bug where if a large amount of experience points were gained and the level increased to 50 at once, Pals would not learn any active skills.
• Mitigated an issue where using Mossanda's partner skill "Grenadier Panda" on a dungeon boss would cause the boss to get stuck in walls

sections.heading: Base Issues
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where base Pals would get stuck on top of a logging site, etc.
• Added measures to prevent the issue where base Pals would wander around and get stuck at the border of the base area.
• Fixed an issue where Pals at the base would get stuck repeatedly starting Transporting tasks and the light bulbs icon would appear over and over again.
• Added measures to prevent the issue of base Pals getting stuck in the farm on a roof.
• Fixed an issue where work Pals would get stuck in the summon space of Palbox when restarting the server on a dedicated server.
• Adjusted the default work priority of base Pals (Anubis will prioritize manual labour)
• Significantly relaxed building restrictions for stairs and triangular roofs.
• Fixed an issue where sound would play in an infinite loop when Pals at the base used certain active skills.
• Specifications changed so that all changes in environmental temperature are added up (if you put 4 campfires next to each other, the surrounding area will have the temperature of a volcano)
• Implemented "relax" working style on the monitoring stand.

sections.heading: Balance Adjustment
sections.text:
• Significantly strengthened mining power for Digtoise partner skills 
• Fixed the abnormally high selling price of nails 
• Significantly increased the number of police officers who appear when a crime is committed.
• Added electric shock effect to Free Pal Alliance's crossbow.
• Adjusted the flight distance of Single-shot Sphere Launcher and Scatter Sphere Launcher. 
• Increased the SAN value recovery amount of high-quality hot springs.

sections.heading: Network Related
sections.text:
• Renovated the server lobby.
• [Search by server name] will now work.
• Added the ability to view online players on a dedicated server. (On community servers, this will only be displayed if the setting is enabled)

sections.heading: Others
sections.text:
• Fixed the issue where Day 5 Memo was not placed.
• You can now adjust the in-game brightness in the settings.
• Fixed many minor bugs.
• Corrected various incorrect text.
• Improved measures against cheating.
• We are currently working on an issue where auto-save fails on the Xbox and Xbox Game Pass versions. We expect that this will be resolved in an upcoming update.

sections.heading: v0.1.5.0 Known Issues
sections.text:
• With the implementation of [Relax] work mode on the monitoring stand, the default work state of the base where a monitoring stand has already been placed is now set to [Relax]. As a result, the work speed of Pals at the base is slow. We apologize for the inconvenience, but please change the work mode from the monitoring stand. 
• There is an issue where Pals created through breeding retain their passives. We plan to roll back this change as soon as possible.

It was released on 27th of February 2024.

Pals

Balance adjustment

sections.text: This version of Palworld mainly focused on fixing various other bugs along with other balance changes. It was released on 29th February 2024.

sections.heading: Major fixes
sections.text: • Fixed various game crashes.

sections.heading: Balance adjustment
sections.text:
• Fixed a bug where breeding Pals always had fixed passives.
• Fixed an issue where the increase in condensation progress was incorrect when using Pal of rank 2 or higher as a condensation material (it will increase by the number of Pals used in previous condensation).

sections.heading: Dungeon issues
sections.text: • Fixed an issue where the innermost door would not open after defeating the boss of a random dungeon.

sections.heading: Pals
sections.text: • Fixed an issue where the name of a Pal would not change even after renaming them.

sections.heading: Server issues
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where it was not possible to search for spaces or Japanese/Chinese characters in the server list.
• Fixed so that if the server is no longer registered on the server list, it will be re-registered without needing to restart the server.
• Fixed an issue where the settings to enable RCON were not loaded from the configuration file.

sections.heading: Official statement
sections.text: "We are currently working on an issue where auto-save fails on the Xbox and Xbox Game Pass versions. We expect that this will be resolved in an upcoming update."

Early Access 0.1.5.1 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store.
It was released on 29th of February 2024.

sections.heading: Major fixes
Dungeon issues
sections.text: • Fixed an issue where the innermost door would not open after defeating the boss of a random dungeon.

sections.heading: Pals

sections.heading: Server issues

sections.text: Early Access 0.1.5.2 (Xbox) was a release of Palworld for Xbox/Microsoft Store. It was a minor hotfix without an equivalent Steam version and was released on 8th of March 2024.

sections.heading: Fixes
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where save data larger than 16MB would not save correctly;
• Fixed a multiplayer-related bug which caused the game to crash.
This version of Palworld released the first ever Raid Boss Bellanoir. It was released the 4th April 2024.

sections.heading: New Content
sections.text:
• Implemented the first Raid Boss. 
 ◦ You can summon Raid Boss Pals by using slabs at the new Summoning Altar.
 ◦ Raid Boss Pals summoned by slabs "Bellanoir's Slab, Bellanoir Libero's Slab and Bellanoir Libero (Ultra) Slab" are very powerful and can not be captured. Work alongside your base Pals to take on these powerful foes.
 ◦ Pal Eggs can drop after defeating Raid Boss Pals. 
 ◦ Bellanoir 
 ◦ Bellanoir Libero 
 ◦ The "extreme" version of the Raid Boss is incredibly powerful and difficult to defeat

sections.heading: New Items
sections.text:
• New Training Manual "Training Manual (S), Training Manual (M) Training Manual (L) and Training Manual (XL)" items have been added. These manuals allow you to give experience points to Pals.
• New item Ancient Technical Manual has been added. This manual gives you ancient technology points and can be randomly found inside dungeon chests.
• New Recovery Meds "Low Quality Recovery Meds, Recovery Meds and High Quality Recovery Meds" items have been added. Using these meds will slowly recover your HP over time.
• New item Homeward Thundercloud has been added. When used, this item will instantly move you to your nearest base.
• New item Ability Glasses has been added. When equipped, you can see Pal's stats.
• New stat boosting items have been added: "Power Fruit, Life Fruit, Stout Fruit". These items will boost Pal's stats when used.
• Added a new passive "Mercy Hit". Pals with this passive cannot reduce enemy HP below 1 when attacking.
• New item Ring of Mercy has been added. When wearing this ring, you cannot reduce enemy HP below 1 when attacking.
• New armour Multiclimate Undershirt has been added. Protect yourself against both the heat and the cold with just one slot!
• New building "Electric Egg Incubator" has been added. This incubator consumes electricity to automatically adjust the temperature to the optimum temperature for each egg.
• New building "Ore Mining Site and Ore Mining Site II" has been added. This mining site allows you to produce ores from the comfort of your base!

sections.heading: Pals
sections.text:
• Kelpsea can now produce Pal Fluids at the Ranch.
• Dumud can now produce High Quality Pal Oil at the Ranch.
• You can now reduce the weight of metal ore while riding Surfent Terra.
• Increased the amount of ore dropped while riding Astegon
• You can now raise Pal's rank to the maximum with a single synthesis using the Pal Essence Condenser. (Condensation progress is now accumulated in the individual Pal) 
• Negative Pal status will now be resolved after spending some time in the Pal Box.

sections.heading: UI
sections.text:
• While aiming a sphere, it will now display how many of the target Pal has already been captured.
• You can now check the cooldowns on partner skills for all your Pals on the main screen. 
• Equipment and item stats are now visible on the technology screen, even if you have not unlocked them first 
• The tutorial has been improved and renamed to "Journey". 
• You can now show/hide the "Journey" in the game options. 
• Damage number display size can be changed in the game options. (In Raid Boss battles, the damage numbers tend to overlap a lot and it may be difficult to see, so we recommend adjusting the size)

sections.heading: Player
sections.text:
• Items dropped by players after death on a dedicated server can now be picked up by anyone after 24 hours of real-time have passed.
• Added a new "sleeping" player emote.

sections.heading: Base Related
sections.text:
• You can now allow/disallow certain work for base Pals at the Monitoring Stand. 
• Chest filters have been added. Select item types to allow or disallow inside chests. 
• Crafted items are now transported from crafting facilities. Selecting "allow transport" when crafting will result in Pals transporting those items to chests when finished. 
• You can now edit your character's appearance at any time by using the "Antique Dresser"
• Building and building piece placement rules have been relaxed. 
 ◦ You can now connect stairs facing upwards.
 ◦ Roof pieces can now directly connect to foundations.
 ◦ Triangular walls can now be connected to stairs.
• You can now force a Pal to work and cancel their break by picking them up and throwing them towards a facility (Pals recover their SAN while taking a break, so be careful!)
• Fixed assignments remain fixed, even after bad events occur. Previously, some assignments would change due to certain conditions, but now they will remain fixed unless the Pal is placed inside the Pal Box.

sections.heading: Balance Adjustment
sections.text:
• Minimum heat and cold resistance have been added to various armour. You will no longer need to take off your heat-resistance armour when it is cold at night in the starting areas!
• Reduce the button press time in the egg incubator.
• Change the pattern for Jormuntide Ignis to something more unique.
• Added legendary blueprints for some firearms (Dropped from specific enemies)
• Corrected the selling price of diamonds.
• In single-player, it is no longer possible to select the initial spawn point for multiplayer.
• Blocked the back of the starting area with rocks to prevent players from getting lost or stuck.
• The increased attack power multiplier of partner skills that increase the player's attack power while riding has been uniformly reduced from 2.0 to 1.2.
• Eggs now have a small chance to produce Alpha Pals.
• Flying and floating Pals are now immune to falling damage.
• Shop price adjustments.

sections.heading: Bug Fixes
sections.text:
• Fixed a bug where treasure chests would become empty when dying in a dungeon, etc.
• Fixed an issue where the effect that increases the player's attack power while riding was duplicating and accumulating under certain conditions.
• Fixed an issue where players were not receiving loot when capturing Pals while mounted.
• Adjusted the HP of the Legendary Pal and fixed an issue where the difference in HP between the captured Legendary Pal and the bred Legendary Pal was too large.
• Fixed an issue where Pal would eat while riding. 
• Fixed an issue where spheres thrown close to wild pals would not hit and be lost.

sections.heading: Others
sections.text:
• Improved various Pal models and textures.
• Added and adjusted some sound effects.
• Many other minor bug fixes

sections.heading: Dedicated Server
sections.text:
• Fixed an issue where sorting did not work in the server list.
• Improved the server list to allow page transitions.
• Dedicated servers now support various log outputs.
• Implemented REST API ※ Please check the tech guide for details https://tech.palworldgame.com/.

sections.heading: Cheat Prevention
sections.text:
• Fixed a vulnerability that allowed Steam account spoofing.
• Fixed 7 other critical vulnerabilities.

sections.heading: Soundtrack
sections.text: • One new song has been added to the soundtrack. If you have already purchased the soundtrack, please update and enjoy the new song! ※ Please note that the soundtrack may take a few days to update

sections.heading: Known Bug
sections.text: • After the v0.2.0.6 update, it has been reported that there is an issue where eggs cannot be placed inside the incubator. We apologize for this inconvenience. The problem can be resolved by rebuilding the incubator.

sections.heading: Unlisted Changes
sections.text: v2.0.6 contains several changes which were not listed in the official Patch Notes above.

sections.heading: Pals
sections.text:
• Kelpsea Ignis can now produce Flame Organ at the Ranch.
• Vixy can now produce Giga Sphere and Hyper Sphere at the Ranch.

sections.heading: Base Related
sections.text:
• You can now connect stairs facing downwards when holding Alt.
• New Crusher recipe:
 ◦ Small Pal Soul x2: Medium Pal Soul x1
 ◦ Medium Pal Soul x1: Small Pal Soul x2
 ◦ Medium Pal Soul x2: Large Pal Soul x1
 ◦ Large Pal Soul x1: Medium Pal Soul x2

sections.heading: Balance Adjustment
sections.text:
• Breeding: Jormuntide + Blazehowl = Jormuntide Ignis.
• Single-shot Rifle Schematic 4 dropped from Verdash
• Double-barreled Shotgun Schematic 4 dropped from Vaelet
• Musket Schematic 4 dropped from Quivern
• Only wild eggs and eggs dropped by Bellanoir (10%) have a chance to appear as Alpha.

sections.heading: Bug Fixes
sections.text:
• Captured Tower Bosses have same HP as normal one.
• Jetragon's mount missiles will correctly attack the target you are aiming at.

sections.heading: Others
sections.text:
• Added 3 new hairstyles.
• Ragnahawk unable to pass player doors and dungeon doors.
• NPC Pal tamers all over the place.
• Wandering Merchant visit more often and selling Pal Fluids.
• Stuck Pals teleport to the Palbox.
• Fast Travel icon changed.
• Pal's base attack reduced from 100 to 50.

sections.heading: Optimization and Fixes
