Finsider (Japanese: ガブルミ Gaburumi) is a Water elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Finsider bears the resemblance of a creature in a yellow fish costume, with other colours such as orange and white also present on the costume’s design. Finsider has short, stubby legs and an orange tail at the back of its suit, bearing the semblance of a bipedal fish. In the “Mouth” of this pal, two yellow eyes can be seen peeking through, suggesting that there is something within Finsider’s costume. When aggravated, Finsider can be seen using its active skill, Trigger happy, in which it holds two guns and shoots fast jets of water at the player.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is aggressive and will attack on sight.

Fishing is a fun and exciting new activity that was added in the Tides of Terraria Update.
To fish you need bait and a fishing rod. Then you got to find fish shadows near lakes or oceans. 
Throw your lure into the water and finish a minigame to capture the Pals. The pal is automatically captured into your party/palbox.
There are different sparkles that can appear on the fish shadows.
• Purple sparkles mean the Pal captured will be an Alpha Pals.
• Green sparkles guarantee a Rainbow Passive.
• A Purple Geyser means the pal has the Lunker Passive Skill.

Around its neck is a thick brown collar bursting with magma. It doesn't appear to have legs, only having short, white arms. The body of this Pal is a burnt brown colour, the rest of its body nothing but squishy magma.

Flopie (Japanese: ポプリーナ Potpourrina) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Flopie is a joyful looking floating Pal that resembles both a rabbit and a cat. Its body is a light cream color with pink hands and feet. The soles of its lower limbs feature a simple pinkish red foot pad. The head of Flopie is covered by a bright pink fluffy hair ending in large pigtails and ears that are tipped with gradients of light orange. The insides of its ears like the bottom of its feet come in a pinkish red. It has two green leaves on its chest that give the resemblance to a bow tie or bonnet. It has a puffy tail that is a similar green to its leaves. On the side of its head, an orange hibiscus flower can be seen. When its eyes are open, they are shown to be a golden yellow, framed by winged eyelashes.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: A peaceful creature more commonly found wandering through the forests around Mount Flopie. This Pal will run away from the player on sight.

Food and Nutrition are closely-tied mechanics in Palworld, often collectively referred to as "Hunger" or "Satiety". Managing this is required to regenerate health and keep the sanity of your Pals high.

sections.heading: About
sections.text:
The food level of your character and Pals slowly drop over time. You can replenish your food by opening your inventory, right-clicking on a food, and then clicking on your character or a Pal in your party. Pals at your base can also increase their food bar by eating food from a Feed Box.
Pals that are starving tend to sleep instead of eat. Sleeping will regenerate health, but they will continue starving when they wake up again unless they eat.
When your food bar drops past halfway, you will receive a "Hungry" status as a warning. If you or your Pal's food bar reaches 0, they will get a "Starving" status and their health will slowly decrease until it reaches 1. It is currently not possible to die of starvation, but your health will not recover until you eat something.
To prevent getting your character or Pals hungry, consider investing in researching a Feed Bag. This lets you put food into a different slot in your inventory and everyone will automatically refill their hunger bar.

sections.heading: Getting food
sections.text:
For a list of consumable food items, see Ingredients
Food can be gathered from the wild (Red Berries), farmed (Berry Plantation), or harvested by catching or killing Pals that drop meat.

sections.heading: Food Spoilage
sections.text:
Upon acquiring a food item, a set timer counting in minutes:seconds is overlaid onto the food item's icon in the player's inventory. Once this timer reaches 00:00, the food item will disappear from the inventory, representing that the food item has spoiled/gone bad. 
If there are multiples of the same food item, they will degrade one at a time, with the timer resetting back to its original value with every instance that expires. Eating or feeding a Pal a food item that still has multiples remaining in the inventory will also reset this timer. Dividing the total amount will cause the new batch of the food item in question to start a new separate timer for itself upon being placed somewhere else, that begins at whatever time was left from the original batch upon splitting. If the new batch is merged with another batch of the same food item, the timer with the lesser amount of time left will take priority in replacing both upon merging batches. If the player drops the food item from their inventory, then picks up a new batch of the same food item from somewhere else, before going back to pick up the original batch, the timer will reset to that of the new batch. The timer will also be reset if the player presses the Sort button on the inventory the item is in.
Ingredients can be cooked to increase their nutritional value and lifespan before they spoil. 
Placing food in a Cooler Box or Refrigerator will vastly extend the spoiling timer, with the extended duration being dependent on the Cooling work skill level of the Pal tasked to it. If possible, do not store your perishables in a regular storage box!
Cakes placed in the Breeding Farm's dedicated chest will not have a spoiling timer/have their timer removed unless taken out again.

sections.heading: Other info
sections.text:
• Some foods like Cotton Candy can directly increase a Pal's sanity.
• Some Pals can have certain natures that cause their food meter to degrade slower (such as "Diet Lover") or faster (such as "Glutton").

It has eyebrows that are a lighter shade of yellow. On each side of Foxsparks' face, messy tufts of fur are visible. The stomach of Foxsparks like the inside of its ears are tan. It has a large bushy tail that like its ears, is tipped with a black pattern. A prominent feature on Foxsparks is being engulfed with flames that cover over its legs and the tip of its tail. The eyes of Foxparks come a crimson red color.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is a fairly timid creature that will usually flee away upon sighting a player. It is usually found travelling alone or in pairs.

The inside of its ears are a soft blue-white colour, with three aqua blue tufts on its cheeks. It has wide orange-red eyes. It has dark blue marks above its eyes, and a dark blue nose which is shaped like an upside down triangle. It has a blue-white underbelly, and the rest of its body is a aqua blue colour. Its paws are coated with a smoky light blue coloured flame. It has a long, bushy tail, ending with a dark blue zigzag. The tip of its tail is a smoky light blue coloured fire.

The Free Pal Alliance (FPA) is a faction that advocates freedom and ethical treatment of Pals. They oppose the exploitation and misuse of these creatures for personal gain or research, advocating for a harmonious coexistence between humans and Pals. The determined and compassionate Lily Everhart leads the faction alongside her loyal Grass-elemental Pal companion, Lyleen. They might seem like the most benevolent faction on the islands, but if you look closer, they're not as kind as you think.

sections.heading: Members
sections.text:
• Lily Everhart - Leader
• Free Pal Alliance Devout (Bat)
• Free Pal Alliance Devout (Crossbow)
• Free Pal Alliance Judicator
Free Pal Alliance Devouts are a type of enemy affiliated with the Free Pal Alliance.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
The Devouts are usually found in large groups in or near Free Pal Alliance outposts as well as during Raids, although sometimes one or two of them may be found wandering around out in the open.
They wield a variety of weapons including Wooden Clubs, Electric Arrow Crossbows, and sometimes Single-Shot Rifles.
While in combat, they tend to alternate between trying to keep their distance from the player and getting up close to attack, so the most effective way to take them down is by damaging them as much as possible before they run.

images.caption: thumb|A Free Pal Alliance Devout caught by a Hanging Trap during a Base Raid.

Furniture in Palworld is used primarily to decorate one's base, though a few items may have additional functions.
There are numerous aesthetics to choose from to decorate with.

sections.heading: Main sets
sections.text: These are the basic sets that have to be unlocked through the Technology tree. Once unlocked there, they'll automatically unlock a number of items in that set that are viewable via the build menu.

sections.heading: Storage
sections.text: This includes all the possible storage items unlocked via the above sets. On top of being decorative, these allow one to store items in them.

sections.heading: Lighting
sections.text: This includes all the possible lighting items unlocked via the above sets. These items provide a variety of lighting functions for one's base.

sections.heading: Furniture
sections.text: This includes all the possible decorative furniture unlocked via the above sets. These don't have any function other than to be purely aesthetic.

Futuristic Structure Set is a set of structures in Palworld.
It's a tier 61 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Like the other foundation sets, once unlocked, one can then build 8 basic structures, this time for futuristic polymeric materials. These include walls, foundation, roofs, stairs, and a door.
Like other structure sets, all structures need a form of contact with the ground. Walls and doors can be built when vertically connecting to a roof or foundation. If this foundation is broken, all walls connected to it will break if there is no additional gripping point above or below them (and drop their ingredints), even if they are touching another wall.

It is exclusive to Galeclaw and cannot be taught to Pals via a skill fruit.
Galeclaw (Japanese: エアムルグ Aimurgh) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Galeclaw resembles a medium-sized bird-of-prey, with white plumage covering the head, neck, tail, and chest areas, and brown feathers for the legs, body, and wing plumage. The beak is short and curved, and the feet bear four talons each; both are colored yellow. The eyes are a reddish-brown and narrow in shape. Two long, thin feathers sprout from the eyebrows, which have a gradient colouration of light blue or turquoise at the head to a lime green towards the tips. The topside plumage of its wings also bear a light brown colouring, while the underside plumage of the body and wings is mostly white with an alternating layer of brown feathers, accented with turquoise-coloured feather tips.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Galeclaws can be a very annoying nuisance to deal with for early-game players, as they like to harass their prey from above. Much like Direhowls, they often spawn in groups of three or more, and have a tendency to attack almost everything and anything that they come across. If one spots a player, expect the whole flock to pursue for a quite a long time, seemingly without getting tired.
Wild Galeclaw can be often found scavenging the corpses of humans or Pals leftover from a fight. While eating, these Pals won't become hostile towards near players but will readily start attacking after finishing the meal.
Players are advised to shoot at it with ranged weapons to get it to fall from the sky, as it will never come down on its own.
While its partner skill doesn't allow for true flight, it should still be an option to consider for an early-game player who wishes to get a relatively stamina-efficient method of air travel as soon as possible. Though, by the time the player reaches the level required for the item that activates its partner skill, there are probably better options available to them (such as a flying Pal that can actually serve as a mount).

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Galeclaw Rider - While in team, can be summoned and used instead of a glider. (Requires Galeclaw Gloves→ Unlocks at Lv. 23)
• Allows you to fire a gun while gliding with this Pal. 
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 2
Possible Drops:
• Galeclaw Poultry
• Leather

• Its Japanese name may come from air and मुरग़ा। murgh (chicken in Hindi).
• Galeclaw was featured in Paldeck No.021 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

is one of the work suitability skills a pal can have in Palworld.
Pals with this ability are able to harvest drops crops. You can throw them at a Red Berry Bush or deploy them at a base to work on a plantation. Gathering pals are best used when combined with watering and pals for them to maintain the plantations without manually having to work them yourself.

Gear is the essential components to progress in Palworld and includes a variety of Armor, Weapons, and Utility items.
All main gear has to be crafted via special workbenches, with better equipment requiring more advanced workbenches and materials to make them. One can also find and utilize schematics which can give access to better-tiered equipment for a particular piece of armor or weapon. As of v0.1.4.1, most craftable ranged weapons and armor have 4 schematic tiers, with legendary being the highest level.

sections.heading: Armor
sections.text: Armor provides defense against foes and the weather and comes in 3 basic forms: body, head, and shields.

sections.heading: Weapons
sections.text: Weapons are the basic tools used to defend and attack in the game. They come in a variety of melee and ranged versions, as well as a few special Pal-specific types.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text: Utility items are gear and other equipment used to help you on your journey through Palworld.

sections.heading: Pal Gear
sections.text: Pal Gear are unique items pertaining to each particular type of pal. These can come in the form of saddles for Rideable Pals, gliders, or a variety of weapons based on the particular pal the gear is for. For example, Mossanda can be equipped with their grenade launcher while Jolthog's gloves allows the Jolthog to throw electric grenades. All such gear must be crafted at a Pal Gear Workbench.

Giant Feed Bag is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Requirements;
sections.text:
• Technology Tier 45*Huge Feed Bag
• Five (5) Ancient Technology Points

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Crafting the Giant Feed Bag increases the number of different types of food storage slots from 4 to 5.
Useful for having a primary food source you have a lower quantity of, and three backup food like Baked Berries,
Alternatively, having a high-quantity food in the first slot allows you to use the remaining slots as additional Backpack space for a food of higher Satiety.

sections.heading: Drawbacks
sections.text:
Things to consider when using multiple food sources at the same time include
• Weight; how much does all food combined add to my total carry weight?
• Priority; Which food do I want my character, and my pals, to consume first? Keep in mind Pals and Player eat automatically when hunger reaches ≤50%, so if you have a high Satiety food in the first slot, your party may consume foods too early to fully appreciate the satiety value of mid to late-game recipe foods.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text: • Baked berries in the first slot mean your party will consume the least valuable food first.

sections.text:
Glass Structure Set is a set of structures in Palworld.
It's a tier 39 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Like the other foundation sets, once unlocked, one can then build 8 basic structures, this time for glass. These include walls, foundation, roofs, stairs, and a door.
Like other structure sets, all structures need a form of contact with the ground. Walls and doors can be built when vertically connecting to a roof or foundation. If this foundation is broken, all walls connected to it will break if there is no additional gripping point above or below them (and drop their ingredients), even if they are touching another wall.
Gliding is a mechanic in Palworld where you use the air to create horizontal velocity. Gliding can be done using Gliders items or Partner Skills from certain Pals in your party.

sections.heading: Mechanics
sections.text:
If you have the right equipment or pals, you can activate gliding by jumping (pressing spacebar) mid-air. If you're standing on the ground, you would first have to jump off the ground, and then jump again in the air.
Gliding consumes stamina. If the player runs out of stamina while gliding, they will begin to fall.
After activating the glider, it takes about 1 second before you can deactivate it again. The glider can be deactivated mid-air by jumping or crouching, and be reopened immediately after.
Gliders rotate similar to how the player moves, and can be changed direction instantly. When a glider is activated, the player will glide in the direction it last moved: When not pressing anything, the glider continues in the direction it is going. While activated, the glider will slowly start to gain more downward velocity. This can be reset by closing and reopening your glider.
You can't throw Pal Spheres or Pals in your party while gliding. You can still pick up pals in your base while gliding. You can also pat or feed them. This will deactivate your glider. You can also use emotes, allowing you to 'Sit on ground' while in the air.

sections.heading: Glider items
sections.text:
Gliders need to be equipped in the inventory to work.
Better gliders will go more horizontally and consume less stamina.

sections.heading: Pals with gliding skills
sections.text:
Some Pals have a Partner Skill to let the player use it as glider. For Pals to be able to be used as gliders, the player must first have the items to do so. Each pal has their own gear that the player must research and craft. When acquiring the item, it will go to the 'Key Items' tab in the inventory, and you can start gliding.
If your party has multiple Pals that can be used as glider, the game will go through your party from top to bottom and pick the first pal that can be glided with.

rows.t: Allows the player to move quickly while gliding.
Can float for long periods of time while gliding.
Carries the player up high while gliding.
Allows the player to fire a gun while gliding. This includes Grappling Guns
Carries the player up high while gliding.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
Tip 1: You can slide off a slope to build up momentum. While sliding, you can jump up and begin gliding while keeping your momentum. This lets you glide at higher speeds.
Tip 2: Using a Grappling Gun, you can gain some high momentum for longer distance gliding. Aim at the corner of a wall/high surface and cancel after gaining some speed, but before hitting the wall. Directing yourself away from the wall to prevent collision will let you glide through the air at high speeds.
A good testing environment is building the following with the Wooden Structure Set: A foundation, two walls on top of each other, and a roof. Standing some distance away and aiming at the bottom of the roof will make sure you don't have any obstacles after gripping. Then, cancel before getting close to the roof and activate your glider. Now watch as you glide through the air faster than a Jetragon[verify].

sections.text: Gobfin Ignis appears like a short, shark like creature that looks aggressive. It is has one, tall shark-like fin on the top of its head, the fin crimson in colour. It has two frills on the sides of its head. The top of these frills are crimson in colour, the frill itself a sandy yellow colour. It has narrowed eyes and black slits for pupils, appearing intimidating. It has softer crimson zigzag bolts on the sides of its head, the zigzags going over its eyes. Its top jaw has sharp crimson teeth, and its bottom jaw is a sandy yellow colour. It has a oval shaped body, and a sandy yellow belly. It has short and stocky legs, and long arms. Its short legs have three white claws on each foot. It has three thick fingers on each hand, and a soft crimson zigzag running across the right side of its torso.

Grappling Gun is the lowest tier Grappling Gun in Palworld.

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.text:
• Technology Tier 12 
• One (1) Ancient Technology Points
• Primitive Workbench

sections.heading: Stats
sections.text:
Distance; 5 Foundations (20 meters/65.6 Feet)
Cooldown; 12 seconds.
Durability; 150.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used as a means of transportation. Quickly pulls the player toward the targeted location, be it horizontal or vertical. Used in conjuncture with Climbing to reach higher places than the player's stamina would otherwise afford.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Grappling guns are items in Palworld that let you maneuver more easily around the terrain.

sections.heading: Obtaining
sections.text: To obtain it, the player must unlock it in the technology menu. It can only be unlocked using Ancient Technology Points. After unlocking it, it can be crafted at a workbench or assembly line, depending on the type.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
A grappling gun must be equipped as weapon before it can be used. When selected, it will show a crosshair. Left clicking will activate the gun: It will shoot a rope towards where you're aiming. If it hits world terrain, it will stick to it and pull you towards it. It cannot stick to man-made objects (excluding buildings), Pals, minerals, or terrain that is under water. You can cancel the grappler's pulling mechanic by rolling (default: "Ctrl").
Grappling guns have a cool-down before they can be used again. This cooldown only activates once it successfully stuck to the ground. They also have a seperate cooldown for each grappler, allowing multiple uses.
Grappling Guns can be used while weighed down. This lets you transport heavy loads even when you can't walk anymore.
It cannot be used while gliding, but it can be used while falling. Since the rope often goes slower than your vertical falling velocity, you cannot aim it at the ground to break high fall damage[source: I couldn't get it to work]. You can, however, aim it at nearby walls, if you know where to aim. You can also use it while mounted, but this will cause you to dismount your Pal. You must aim (hold right-click) to use it, though, because otherwise the game will try to use the Pal's attack instead of your gun.
If used while crouched, the player will still be crouched after grappling.

sections.heading: Types of grappling guns
sections.text: Grappling guns have different spherical ranges. The current unit used to show the range is the Wooden Structure Set's Wooden Foundation.

rows.t:
Weapon Assembly Line II or
Production Assembly Line II

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
Grapple Gliding: Using a grapple gun to gain speed before opening a glider to maintain speed. 
• Flat Ground/Slight descent- Use the grapple gun as far away from you as possible to build up speed. Right before you hit the ground cancel the grapple and open a glider. For testing use about 8 foundations lengths as a runway.
• Wall/Higher surface- Aim at the corner of a wall/high surface and cancel after gaining some speed, but before hitting the wall. Directing yourself away from the wall to prevent collision will let you glide through the air at high speeds. 
 ◦ A good testing environment is building the following with the Wooden Structure Set: A foundation, two walls on top of each other, and a roof. Standing some distance away and aiming at the bottom of the roof will make sure you don't have any obstacles after gripping. Then, cancel before getting close to the roof and activate your glider.
• Now enjoy gliding through the air faster than a Jetragon[verify].
Grapple-vator (Grapple Elevator): 
• Build a foundation with a wall. Build as high as you desire the elevator to go. Stand on the foundation and aim at the bottom edge of the wall. The lift animation will clip characters arm through the wall and you will start to rise up. Once the top of the wall reached you can cancel the grapple while walking forward to exit the Grapple-vator.
• For multiple floors you will need multiple grapple-vators.
• Not responsible for any cheese grating of face.

sections.heading: Known Bugs
sections.text: • While the Grappling Gun is stored on ones back, the model shown is of the Pistol, not the Grappling Gun. - Xbox Edition

Grintale (Japanese: ニャンギマリ Nyangimari) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Grintale is a Pal resembling a massive, bulky cat with a split tail. Its medium-length fur is of a dusty purple complexion throughout most of the body, including the head, enormous triangular ears, and back. Underneath this thick layer of purple fur are two more layers; a beige-coloured middle layer that encompasses the face, legs, and sides, and a black-coloured inner layer that covers the underside. The tail is black and cable-thin compared to the rest of the creature, and bears a purple band just before where the tail splits into two.
Its face is its most distinguishing feature, with a pair of giant, circle-shaped, glowing yellow eyes with white pupils, and of course, fitting of its name, a big wide grin underneath, full of comically big, triangular teeth.
It is much bigger than the player character, at roughly 200-210 cm at the head, not including the ears (assuming the player avatar is around 180 cm).

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
While this Pal can only be found at night, for some reason, they will be more commonly found sleeping than they will be roaming around. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon.
Despite being considered a nocturnal species, if employed as a Base Pal, they will actually sleep during nighttime, and thus will need a bed to accommodate them, perhaps a side effect of not being a Dark-element Pal.
And yes, the big yellow eyes do indeed shine brightly in the darkness of the night, though they won't really help the player much in terms of map visibility; the eyes don't actually light up the map's surroundings, akin to a car's headlamps to be more clear.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Plump Body - Can be ridden. Enhances Neutral attacks while mounted. (Requires Grintale Saddle → Unlocks at Lv. 13)
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 2
Possible Drops:
• High Quality Pal Oil

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• It appears to take inspiration from Alice in Wonderland's Cheshire Cat and/or possibly My Neighbour Totoro's Catbus.
 ◦ The split tail is a distinguished trait of nekomata, a type of bakeneko, or cat yōkai.
• Its Japanese name may come from にゃん nyan (onomatopoeia the cry of a cat) and gimmari (Korean fried seaweed rolls), the latter probably because of Grintale's body shape resembling the dish.
• Grintale was featured in Paldeck No.057 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: Alpha Grintale size comparison with a Player.

Grizzbolt (Japanese: エレパンダ Elepanda) is an Electric element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Grizzbolt is a bipedal Pal, looking rather bulky. It is mostly yellow in colour, with stubby legs and long arms. On its legs are three long, sharp white claws. Its paws are black, with black zigzags running up its arms. On the right arm, the black zigzag reaches its face, ending at its mouth. It has long, sharp white claws on its paws. It doesn't appear to have a neck, with two tufts on both sides of its head. It has a white belly with a black zigzag shaped bolt running across is diagonally. It has a upside down triangle shaped nose, which is black in colour. It has a wide smile on its face, and narrowed eyes. Its eyes are black slits. It has two yellow tufts on the top of its head, and long, thin black ears that are a pink colour on the inside.

This page will serve as a basic how to play guide for Palworld. While there is very little information available at this time, we urge you to check back often, as new information is being added all the time! Feel free to edit this guide with any tips, tricks, and suggestions.

sections.heading: Basic gameplay
sections.text: Palworld begins with the creation of the player character. Players can select from a number of presets that cover a variety of different body types. Changeable options are Body, Face Hair, and Voice. In another option, they can add the player character's name. When done, they can jump into the game world and learn the tricks of survival with the in-game Tutorial.

sections.heading: Controls
sections.text: PC keybindings & controller buttons by default

sections.heading: Tips and tricks
sections.text:
• Try to prioritize capturing many different Pals over multiples of the same one. Each Pal has unique abilities and labour skills to help you in your Base.
• Build your base near different kind of resources. Your Pals will gather them for you.
• Upgrade your base by doing the missions located in your Palbox.
• Craft a stone pickaxe so you can make Pal Spheres instead of relying on picking random ones off the ground and capture more Pals.
• Forage for food and build a berry plantation as soon as you are able. Eat regularly and keep your Pals fed to heal up.
• If any of your Pals die, put them into a Palbox. They will recover their health over time.
• Build a campfire and craft clothes and a torch to avoid being cold and gradually losing your health during the night.

Guided Missile Launcher is a ranged weapon in Palworld.
It requires Missile Ammo.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: The Guided Missile Launcher is available to unlock at level 55, and can be crafted at a Weapon Assembly Line II. There are also 4 schematics available that you can find throughout the world that offer different rarities of the weapon.

• Early Access 0.3.1
 ◦ Introduced.

Guild Chest is an item in Palworld.
It's a tier 41 Ancient Technology item and requires 4 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: Players and Pals can store items in the Guild Chest. It has 54 inventory slots that are shared by every Guild chest constructed.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: While not mentioned in the item description, food items kept in the guild chest do not rot.

sections.heading: Create/Join A Guild in Palworld
sections.text:
When players start a new game, they are automatically part of an Unnamed Guild. During multiplayer, a Guild is formed when more players join the Unnamed Guild. Commands to ask to join another player's Guild or to invite someone to your Guild will appear when you approach another player. You can join a guild by sending a request or by receiving an invite from another player.
• Join Or Ask A Player To Join When You Approach Them
• Join/Invite to Guild Option
• Cannot Join If The Max Number Of Bases Is Exceeded
Commands to ask to join another player's Guild or to invite someone to your Guild will appear when you approach another player. You can join a guild by sending a request or by receiving an invite from another player.
If a player who wants to join a Guild has already built a Palbox and exceeds the max number of Palboxes, a warning will be issued saying that they cannot join a Guild. Make sure to dismantle your own Palbox before asking to join a Guild.

images.caption: Guild UI showing guild name, change guild name, leave and members online & count
Guild with members showing online with a custom Guild Name
Leave Guild is available at the top right (corner) however, IF YOU ARE THE GUILDMASTER, you cannot leave.

sections.heading: Leave Guild From The Menu
sections.text: Select the Guild tab from the menu and press the red button in the upper right corner to bring up the warning screen. If you select "Yes" when the warning appears, you can leave the Guild you are currently a member of. If you are the Guild Master, you cannot leave the Guild you are in charge of.

sections.heading: Assigning Guild Master to someone else
sections.text:
First, select another member to be the Guild Master. If you are the lone member of a Guild, you will be unable to leave because by default you are its Guild Master.
The Guild Master has the power to remove members from the Guild.

images.file: Assigning Guild Master to someone else .png
images.caption: Assign Guild Master to someone else by clicking and selecting "MAKE GUILD MASTER" , then you are free to leave.

sections.heading: Pros:
sections.text:
• Pal trading is possible through base-working pals being added by one member, and removed by another.
• Storage containers are shared between Guildmates, so you can work toward mutual goals.
• Guildmate locations are indicated on the World Map.
• If your lifepoints drop to 0, you are not killed, instead the player falls into a crawl, and a timer counts down. Guildmates can revive you before this timer hits 0.

sections.heading: Cons:
sections.text:
• Palbox limits apply to the whole of the Guild; a maximum of 3 palboxes for use by all members are available, regardless the number of people in the Guild.
• Pals working the base can be taken by any member.
• Resources in storage containers (except password secured storage) are available to any member of the guild, making it possible (and even likely) that anything added to the Guild's storage will be gone before you get the opportunity to use them. 
• Should you decide to leave a Guild at any time, you will lose Base advancement, and return to the Base Level you had prior to joining; potentially restarting your base to level 1.

sections.heading: Source
sections.text: source: https://www.reddit.com/r/Palworld_Guilds/
Gumoss (Japanese: ナエモチ Naemochi) is a Grass / Ground element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Gumoss is an amorphous tan mollusk-like pal with a long orange tongue constantly sticking out. It features a vacant expression with its face having dot eyes and a cat-like smile. It has short stubby arms. On its head, it has what resembles a brown cap of an acorn with two green leaves.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Gumoss are normally non-hostile towards the player, only retaliating when attacked.

sections.heading: Utility

Work Suitability:

 ◦ Gumoss Leaf
 ◦ Beautiful Flower (with red flower)
• Boss
 ◦ Ancient Civilization Parts
 ◦ Berry Seeds
 ◦ Gumoss Leaf
 ◦ Precious Entrails
 ◦ Ring of Earth Resistance

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Gumoss rarely spawn with red flowers on their heads. This variant drops Beautiful Flowers when captured or killed.
• This Pal's name may come from gummy and moss.
• Its Japanese name may come from 苗 nae (seedling) and 餅 mochi (sticky rice cake).

The Handgun is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 29 for 3 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Like the Makeshift Handgun, the Handgun is a good close-quarters weapon with decent damage per shot and decent magazine size. However, it holds more shots than its makeshift counterpart and has higher durability, though this comes at the cost of dealing slightly less damage per shot than the Makeshift Handgun.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Handgun's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Beakon boss.
• Due to it being unlocked at a similar level to the Makeshift Handgun and the fact that it cannot be outright purchased, it is advised to skip the Makeshift Handgun and go straight for the Handgun.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The Handgun appears to have been visually based on the M1911, an American firearm known for being one of the most powerful pistols in the world.

Hangyu (Japanese: シメナワ Shimenawa) is a Ground element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Hangyu is a floating pal that bears resemblance to a noose. It has a tiny cream-colored body with short stubby feet. In contrast to their small body, Hangyu has a large ponytail and pair of arms. Its arms are striped like a candy cane being brown and cream. ending in three brown fingers it uses to grab things. Hangyu has a simple, smiling face with yellow eyes and rosy cheeks.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal is non-hostile towards the player unless it's attacked.
While its partner skill doesn't allow for true flight, it should still be an option to consider for an early-game player who wishes to get a relatively stamina-efficient method of air travel as soon as possible. Though, by the time the player reaches the level required for the item that activates its partner skill, there are probably better options available to them, such as a flying Pal that can actually serve as a mount.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Flying Trapeze - While in team, can be summoned and used instead of a glider. Carries the player up high while gliding. (Requires Hangyu's Gloves→ Unlocks at Lv. 20)
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 1
• Transporting 2
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Fiber

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Hangyu's name is likely a play on hang you, referencing its behavior.
• Its Japanese name may come from しめ縄 shimenawa (a rope used as a Talisman in Shinto religion).
• Hangyu was featured in Paldeck No.039 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Hangyu Cryst (Japanese: オバケナワ Obakenawa) is the Ice element variation of Hangyu.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Hangyu Cryst has a small, circular body and extremely long arms. Its body is periwinkle in colour, with short, triangular legs. It has raw salmon pink cheeks, and circle shaped yellow eyes with no pupils. Its mouth is cat shaped. It has a dark periwinkle mane on the top of its head. Its arms are a light periwinkle colour with thick, horizontal dark periwinkle stripes. It has three thick fingers on each hand. This Pal appears to float in the air all the time.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is non-hostile towards the player unless it's attacked.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Winter Trapeze - While in team, can be summoned and used instead of a glider. Carries the player up high while gliding. (Requires Hangyu Cryst's Glove→ Unlocks at Lv. 31)
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
• Cooling 1
• Handiwork 1
• Transporting 2 
Possible Drops:
• Fiber
• Ice Organ

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Its Japanese name may come from お化け obake (shapeshifting entities from Japanese folklore) and しめ縄 shimenawa (a rope used as a Talisman in Shinto religion), the latter also being Hangyu's Japanese name.
Hangyu Cryst's Glove is a key item in Palworld that allows a Hangyu Cryst to be used as a Glider.

Hangyu's Gloves is a key item in Palworld.
Hangyu's Gloves allows Hangyu to be used as a glider if it is in the players party, however it does not make much of a difference between the regular gliders and their higher forms, but would be a great option to be use if Hangyu is being used in the player's party.

Heater is an infrastructure structure in Palworld.
It's a tier 17 Technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Heaters require a pal with Kindling. The level most likely does not matter, as far as I see. Heaters, like coolers, are used to help with incubating Pal Eggs in an Egg Incubator and provides a safer alternative to campfires. While heaters are more efficient in that they induce more heat than a campfire, keep in mind they have a smaller heating radius. It can also help keep you warm in an arctic base, although the small radius makes only the Electric Heater viable. Useless if you don't live there at high levels due to the Electric Egg Incubator.

Helzephyr (Japanese: ヘルガルダ Hellgaruda) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Helzephyr has mostly black feathers with light blue lightning bolt patterns on its wings and tail. Its face is a deeper shade of light blue, and a vivid purple flame rises from the top of its head.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: A hostile Pal that will attack the player on sight.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Wings of Death - Can be ridden as a flying mount. Applies Dark damage to the player's attacks while mounted. (Requires Helzephyr Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 33)
Work Suitability:
• Transporting 3
Possible Drops:
• Medium Pal Soul
• Venom Gland

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Etymology - Hel is the name of the Goddess of Death in Norse Mythology, and Zephyr is a Greek word meaning "of the west wind".
• Its Japanese name may come from hell and Garuda, which is a large mythical bird that appears in Hindu and Buddhist mythology.
• Helzephyr was featured in Paldeck No.063 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Helzephyr Lux (Japanese: ライガルダ Raigaruda) is a Dark and Electric Elemental Subspecies Pal. It is a Sub-Species of Helzephyr.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Helzephyr Lux is a large, bird like Pal with pitch black feathers, and wings that spread out in the shape of a crescent moon. On its head, it appears to wear a golden helmet with two parts that stick outwards. The rest of the head seems to be ablaze with gold and orange flame. Its eyes are a sunny orange colour, with a small mane of black feathers. On its wings, it has a golden pattern that resembles lightning bolts. It has a long, dark and feathery tail with the same golden lightning pattern. Its talons are pitch black, with lightning yellow claws.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This is an aggressive Pal that will attack the player character on sight.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Found in the northern beach area of Sakurajima.
Helzephyr Saddle is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It is a tier 33 Technology and requires 4 points to unlock after catching an Helzephyr.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used to unlock Helzephyr's Partner Skill, Wings of Death.

Hoocrates (Japanese: ホウロック Houlock) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: This Pal is a monochromatic owl with white bushy eyebrows and dark eyes, evoking the appearance of a wise old man. Distinguished by its pointed hat that seems to be made out of feathers and a white belly bisected by a black stripe.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Nocturnal Pal that can usually be found walking about in low level beginning regions, acting pacific as long as it's not attacked.
This Pal, being a nocturnal species, can only be found at night or in dungeons. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon.
If employed as a Base Pal, Hoocrates will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate them, as they never sleep. If not tasked with anything, they'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Hoocrates's name may come from combining the famous Greek philosopher Socrates name with the word hoot.
• Additionally, the Paldeck's entry for Hoocrates references another famous philosopher, René Descartes, who famously created the philosphical statement "I think, therefore I am."
• Its Japanese name may come from ほうほう houhou (onomatopoeia for the hoots of an owl) and warlock.

Hot Spring is a structure in Palworld.
It's used to help recover SAN for working pals on a base. It's a tier 9 technology item and requires 2 points to be unlocked.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text:
• Pals will automatically use the Hot Spring when they need to relax or recover from injuries.
• It also helps to increase their happiness and friendship level. The basic Hot Spring can accommodate 2 small Pals or 1 large Pal at a time.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
• Place your Hot Spring in a central location where your Pals can easily access it.
• If you have a lot of Pals, consider building multiple Hot Springs or upgrading to the High Quality Hot Spring.

Huge Feed Bag is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Requirements;
sections.text:
• Technology Tier 35
• Large Feed Bag
• Four (4) Ancient Technology Points

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Crafting the Huge Feed Bag increases the number of different types of food storage slots from 3 to 4.
Useful for having a primary food source you have a lower quantity of, and three backup food like Baked Berries,
Alternatively, having a high-quantity food in the first slot allows you to use the remaining slots as additional Backpack space for a food of higher Satiety.

sections.heading: Drawbacks
sections.text:
Things to consider when using multiple food sources at the same time include
• Weight; how much does all food combined add to my total carry weight?
• Priority; Which food do I want my character, and my pals, to consume first? Keep in mind Pals and Player eat automatically when hunger reaches ≤50%, so if you have a high Satiety food in the first slot, your party may consume foods too early to fully appreciate the satiety value of mid to late-game recipe foods.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text: • Baked berries in the first slot mean your party will consume the least valuable food first.

Humans are characters that can be found scattered all over Palpagos Islands, with them mainly concentrating in settlements such as small towns and encampments.
Whether hostile or friendly towards the player, all humans except the leaders of factions can be caught by lowering HP and using a Pal Sphere, without needing to utilize glitches. Human catch rates are much lower than regular Pal's and may require higher-grade Pal Spheres.
While able to be caught, Humans cannot use the weapons they are holding - for example, if a Rayne Syndicate human with a pistol is caught, they will not have the ability to use it in combat. 
This makes humans sub-par when compared to even the most simple of Pals, with some exceptions. It is highly recommended to not waste time catching just any human in Pal World. Nevertheless, there are a few humans worth capturing in Palworld, some being merchants which can be deployed on the player's base to always have someone to buy from. 
Most humans, except leaders of factions, function like Neutral element Pals, receiving increased damage from Dark element skills.

sections.heading: Combat
sections.text:
If in the event a player chooses to use a captured human in combat, the results will vary greatly depending on their type & class.
Example: A level 10 Rayne Syndicate Grenadier with 200 Attack stats is observed to deal significantly more damage than a Crusher or any other type of enemy of similar level and attack damage stats. This makes the Grenadier the most viable human to have on one's team in the field and can be significantly more viable than using a Pal of similar level and damage stats.
Although Humans that are caught with any weapon, may not be able to use said weapon, the damage that is dealt does seem to scale based on what kind of weapon they are equipped with, when caught.
Currently, Humans that are equipped with a Pal skill, will typically become inert and rendered useless, until the skill is unequipped. In exception, Moonflower Genins and Jonins are able to use pal skills, although there is no animation for it. However, all humans can be enhanced with Pal Souls, similar to regular Pals.

sections.heading: Catching Humans
sections.text:
A captured human. On Palpagos Island, capturing humans in Palspheres is considered inhumane. — in-game text
While it may seem like a sick joke, human beings can be caught in Palworld. Just like with regular Pals, Pal Spheres are used to attempt to catch them, although they have a significantly lower catch rate. It's worth noting that the game usually considers the act taboo, and will start the progression of a wanted meter until the offending player dies.
Here is an in-game demonstration video showcasing on catching a human "Pal".
Human characters only know Punch, have Handiwork 1, and cannot be used in the Breeding Farm. They also cannot use their weapons (such as any guns they may be holding), nor can they be interacted with the same way as with Pals - this means it's not possible to feed, pet, or pick them up when nearby, or use the Command menu. 
Catching a Wandering Merchant, Pal Merchant, or Black Marketeer and assigning them to a base's working party will allow the player to access their shops from the comfort of their own base.
Faction Leaders cannot be caught without the aid of glitches.

Incident is a hidden mechanic that spawns specific entities, or groups of entities across the Palpagos Islands. These may range from spawning Pals, to hostile enemy Factions, as well as friendly humans, including merchants. Certain points across the overworld are hotspots for rare Incidents at a very low chance.
When an Incident occurs, it is chosen from a potential pool of encounters that is specific to that general region - for example, one can never find a naturally-spawning Jolthog Cryst Incident outside of Windswept Hills.
Randomly-spawning Item Merchants encountered in a region can sell the same set of items across multiple different encounters, such as the same three Skill Fruits and medicines.
It is not known if a Pal that appears via an Incident can be a Lucky.

sections.heading: Types of Incidents
sections.text: The game's code categorizes several different types of Incidents, though they all may appear in the same hotspots.

sections.heading: Pal Incidents
sections.text:
• Spawn Pal Battle: Pals engaged in combat can be encountered. These conflicts may involve Pals of the same species or entirely different ones, such as two Mammorest fighting each other or a solo Flopie being hunted by multiple Galeclaw.
• Spawn Pal Group: A specific group of Pals can be encountered. These may involve Pals of the same species or entirely different ones, such as or a Robinquill and a Bristla walking alongside each other or two Caprity walking in a group.
• Spawn Pal: A specific solo Pal can be encountered such as a solo Dinossom or a lone Relaxaurus.

sections.heading: Human Incidents
sections.text:
• Spawn Human Battle: Humans engaged in combat can be encountered. These only involves clash between human (and not the conflict between humans and pals). A prominent examples is the conflict between factions.
• Spawn Human Group: A specific group of Pals can be encountered, such as a group of specific faction members or a Wandering Merchant with armed guards.
• Spawn Human: A specific solo Pal can be encountered such as a Black Marketeer.

sections.heading: Outbreak Incidents
sections.text: • Spawn Herd: A large group of Pals may spawn all at once. Swarms may include Pal species not native to the region, such as Mossanda Lux in the Desiccated Desert. Not all outbreaks are of equal size. An outbreak of Arsox may come in a group of 7, whereas an outbreak of Pengullet may come in a group of 9.
Incineram (Japanese: ヘルゴート Hellgoat) is a Fire / Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Incineram is a large, intimidating Pal resembling a satyr. It seems fairly tall and is predominantly black in colour, with a bright red-orange hue covering its stomach, forearms and hands, hooved feet, muzzle, and the inside of its triangular ears. The head is fairly small and triangular in shape, with a tapering snout. Trailing from behind the head is a large waist-long bundle of black and red-orange hair that is tied into a short "tail" at the end. Around its shoulders, upper torso, and the base of its hooves are large tufts of white fur. It has long digitigrade legs, and its forearms are thicker than its biceps, ending in bony three-clawed hands each. There is also a small red claw at the back of each foot. It has lavender-coloured eyes that seem perpetually angry at all times.
Breaking up this colour scheme of white, black, and red are several bony protrusions found on the head and torso; namely, four parts wrapped around the sides in a ribcage-like fashion, a chest spike, and perhaps most distinctively, a pair of large goat-like horns that curve up and away.
Its overall silhouette is similar to that of a fellow Dark-element Pal, Loupmoon.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
While it can be found naturally in the most south-western parts of the map, players are most likely to have their first encounter with this creature as a nasty raider, whether they're helping Syndicate members in the early game, or leading an invasion of others of either its own kind, or a gang of Leezpunks at higher levels.
It is a swift, highly aggressive, and destructive combatant, especially devastating in groups. May woe befall those who built their buildings and foundations entirely out of wood when an invasion of these Pals occurs, without bothering to transition even some of their bases to more fire-resistant stone-built foundations yet. If such an event happens to someone, with their house, storage, and crafting facilities razed to the ground, and having to spend a long period of time scavenging up the resources left behind, including sending those that didn't make it back to the Palbox and re-organizing everything again, it is best to let that event be a wake-up call for deciding on increased importance in stone infrastructure and base defense as they recover.
In its own habitat, this Pal is active at any time of the day (due to it being a Dark element Pal rather than simply being nocturnal), so there is no need to worry about them disappearing when the sun rises in the horizon, if a player is seeking one out. Rather strangely, they can on occasion be found interacting with Leezpunks in the wild, suggesting an odd friendship of sorts. The two sometimes fight together in the wild, and as mentioned earlier, Leezpunk raids on player bases are often led by an Incineram. Befitting to its raiding behavior, Incineram are very aggressive, and will viciously go on the attack the moment a player is spotted. It has a preference for unleashing area-of-effect attacks from range, before closing in quickly with a melee attack, such as with its signature move "Hellfire Claw".
If employed as a Base Pal, Incinerams will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate them, as sleep is not a necessity. If not tasked with anything, Incineram will simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with other nocturnal and Dark element Pals.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Flameclaw Hunter - When activated, attacks targeted enemy with a powerful Hellfire Claw
Work Suitability:
• Kindling 1
• Handiwork 2
• Transporting 2
• Mining 1
Possible Drops:
• Horn
• Leather

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Incineram seem to be inspired by the pokemon houndoom since both are based on demonic creatures, share the same element (fire and dark type) and a similar aesthetic design and color scheme
• Incineram was named by the community during a Name This Pal event. The name is a portmanteau of the words incinerate and ram.
• Incineram seems loosely based off of depictions of Baphomet, an occult/satanic figure described by the Knights Templar in the 14th century, or perhaps the Jersey Devil, a North American cryptid.
 ◦ The former seems more likely, as it is called "Baphomet" within the game files.
• Incineram was featured in Paldeck No.005 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: An Incineram relaxing in a Hot Spring at night.

sections.text: Incineram Noct (Japanese: アビスゴート Abyssgoat) is the Dark element variation of Incineram.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: For the most part, this Pal is visually identical to an Incineram; the only notable difference, aside from every instance of the red-orange colour replaced with a violet colour, are the wide, branched antlers, instead of the original's goat-like horns.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
It is not naturally found in the wild, aside from specific Wildlife Sanctuaries. It is a highly aggressive and dangerous Pal when encountered.
It can occasionally appears during a Base Raid along with Incinerams and Leezpunks.
If employed as a Base Pal, Incineram Noct will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to be accommodated, as sleep is not a necessity. If not tasked with anything, it'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with other nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Wildlife Sanctuaries

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:

• Darkclaw Hunter - When activated, attacks targeted enemy with a powerful Hellfire Claw.
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 2
• Mining 1
• Transporting 2
Possible Drops:
• Horn
• Leather

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• If Incineram is inspired by depictions of Baphomet and the Jersey Devil, then Incineram Noct, or at least its Paldex entry, possibly takes its inspiration from Krampus, a Germanic Yule entity that accompanies Saint Nicholas and abducts ill-mannered children to punish them.
• Incineram Noct is the only subspecies to remove an element from the base Pal (Fire) to change a dual-element Pal into a single-element Pal, instead of changing one of the base Pal's existing elements or adding a second element to a single-element Pal.
• Incineram Noct was featured in Paldeck No.081 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Japanese-Style Structure Set is a set of structures in Palworld.
It's a tier 50 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Like the other foundation sets, once unlocked, one can then build 8 basic structures, this time for japanese-style wood and tatami. These include walls, foundation, roofs, stairs, and a door.
Like other structure sets, all structures need a form of contact with the ground. Walls and doors can be built when vertically connecting to a roof or foundation. If this foundation is broken, all walls connected to it will break if there is no additional gripping point above or below them (and drop their ingredients), even if they are touching another wall.

Jetragon (Japanese: ジェッドラゴン Jedragon) is a Dragon element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Jetragon is a flying jet-like draconic pal. It is a dull blue and has an off-white stomach and lower jaw. The top of its head features what resembles a black helmet with protruding horns that have bright red-orange stripes. It has bulky darker blue hands that sport two claws, the wrists of Jetragon have an orange stripe. The thighs of Jetragon feature hollowed out parts that resemble jet engines. The feet on Jetragon look similar to its hands but lack stripes. Jetragon's tail is long and greatly resembles the tail of an airplane, its tail is a darker blue and has off-white stripes present. Its back features what resembles a black seat and has four orange spikes. For each of these spikes, it features large bright magenta wings present.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: It tend to be aggressive towards the player upon sight, attacking unprovoked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Spawns on the far end of Mount Obsidian, closet to the Wildlife Sanctuary.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
As a Legendary Pal, it comes with the Legend passive skill.

sections.heading: Trivia

• Jetragon was featured in Paldeck No.050 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used to unlock Jetragon's Partner Skill, Aerial Missile, which allows players to ride it and directly control its attacks.
Jolthog (Japanese: パチグリ Pachiguri) is an Electric element Pal. It has an Ice element variation, Jolthog Cryst.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Jolthog resembles a small hedgehog with an off-white-colored body. It has various yellow and tan quills scattered across its back. Its ears are round and are tipped with tufts of fur. The inner parts of Jolthog's ears and its feet are black in appearance. The back of Jolthog features a short tail that curves upwards.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Jolthog are normally pacifists and are likely to run away from the player. When attacked, wild Jolthog retaliate.
This Pal can be often found imprisoned by members of the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Missing Information.

sections.text: • Missing Information.

sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Jolt Bomb - When activated, equips Jolthog to the player's hand. When thrown at an enemy, causes an electrical explosion upon impact. (Requires Jolthog's Gloves→ Unlocks at Lv. 8)
Work Suitability:
• Generating Electricity 1
Possible Drops:
• Normal 
 ◦ Electric Organ
• Boss
 ◦ Ancient Civilization Parts
 ◦ Electric Organ
 ◦ Precious Claw
 ◦ Ring of Lightning Resistance

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This Pal was named by the community during a Name This Pal event.
• Its name may come from the words jolt and hedgehog.
• Its Japanese name may come from パチパチ pachipachi (onomatopoeia for crackling) and ぐりぐり guriguri (onomatopoeia for rubbing or rattling).
• Jolthog strongly resembles a simplified cartoon hedgehog.
• Jolthog was featured in Paldeck No.004 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

sections.heading: Gameplay Notes
sections.text: • While Galeclaw's partner skill "Galeclaw Glider" is in use, "Jolt Bomb" can be thrown while gliding. The skill also isn't disrupted by climbing or swimming, despite Jolthog ceasing to be visible during those animations.
Jolthog Cryst (Japanese: コチグリ Kochiguri) is the Ice element variation of Jolthog.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Jolthog Cryst has a largely similar appearance to its electric counterpart, but has some differences. In place of the yellow quills are instead quills that are blue and shiny resembling ice crystals. Its body is retaliated, being a warm grey color. Its eyes, nose, feet and inside of its ears instead of being black instead all are indigo.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Jolthog Cryst are normally passive. When attacked, this Pal will retaliate.
It doesn't have established habitats in Palpagos Islands, meaning that the player can only encounter Jolthog Cryst when this Pal is being attacked or held captive by a Faction, while it's being attacked by a pack of Direhowl or as an Alpha Pal inside a dungeon.

sections.text: • While Galeclaw's partner skill "Galeclaw Glider" is in use, "Cold Bomb" can be thrown while gliding. The skill also isn't disrupted by climbing or swimming, despite Jolthog Cryst ceasing to be visible during those animations.

Jormuntide (Japanese: レヴィドラ Levidra) is a Water / Dragon element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Jormuntide takes the appearance of an extremely large dragon, with a snake-like body. It has a white under body, with a dark blue upper body, and features yellow plating on its face, and also yellow lining along its body.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal will act aggressively immediately after it catches sight of the player.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Jormuntide can be encountered in the wild exclusively as an Alpha Pal.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Stormbringer Sea Dragon - A ridden skill. Allows travel on water and prevents stamina loss while riding on water. (Requires Jormuntide Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 39)
Work Suitability:
• Watering 4
Possible Drops:
• Pal Fluids

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This Pal was named by the community during a Name This Pal event.
• This Pal appears to be based on a sea serpent with Chinese dragon influences.
• There are two Alpha Jormuntide bosses in the overworld. Defeating one of them renders the other one defeated as well.
• The name "Jormuntide" comes from a being in Norse Mythology known as Jörmungandr, or "The World Serpent". The combination of this and the word "tide", in reference to its status as a Water Pal, gives this Pal its name.
• Its Japanese name may come from Leviathan (A large biblical sea serpent) and dragon.

images.caption: thumb|One of the Alpha Jorumntides sleeping underwater.

sections.text: Jormuntide Ignis (Japanese: アグニドラ Agnidra) is a Dragon / Fire element Pal, and the fire variant of Jormuntide.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Jormuntide takes the appearance of an extremely large dragon, with a serpentine body. It has a white slightly partitioned under body, with a black upper body that appears to have a plate like structure containing red accents along its entire length. It has fin like appendages along its back/spine that have a black main bone and have red flesh. It has two fin like appendages on both sides of it at about 3/4 of its length, These "fins" have a black main bone and red flesh, it has 2 large slits that extend almost all the way across the flesh. It also has a tail with black bones and red flesh It has a large red protrusion from the top of its head and another on the tip of its nose. It has two long whiskers and black protruisions that are tipped with red that extend from the jaw, most likely where the jaw hinges or just above. Its eyes are a bright red.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Normally this Pal acts indifferent towards the player and retaliates when attacked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
• Wild Jormuntide Ignis can be found inside the No. 2 Wildlife Sanctuary, as well as a small portion of Feybreak. 
• Huge Dragon Eggs find around Mount Obsidian have high chance to grant this Pal.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Stormbringer Lava Dragon - Can be ridden. Enhances Fire attacks while mounted. (Requires Jormuntide Ignus Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 43)
Work Suitability:
• Kindling 4
Possible Drops:
• High Quality Pal Oil
• Flame Organ

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its Japanese name may come from Agni (The god of fire in Vedic mythology) and dragon.
• It is one of only two pals with level 4 kindling as of version 0.7.2

Journals in Palworld are lore-rich documents scattered throughout the game world. There are many different types, with each serving a specific purpose and offering valuable insights into the game's lore, environment, and mechanics:

sections.heading: Castaway's Journals
sections.text:
These are scattered throughout the world and chronicle the struggles and observations of an unnamed, shipwrecked traveler who ended up on Palpagos Islands. They offer glimpses into the basic history, dangers, and unique creatures of the land.
• Castaway's Journal - Day XX
• Castaway's Journal - Day 0
• Castaway's Journal - Day 1-1
• Castaway's Journal - Day 1-2
• Castaway's Journal Day 2
• Castaway's Journal - Day 3
• Castaway's Journal - Day 5
• Castaway's Journal - Day 6
• Castaway's Journal - Day 8
• Castaway's Journal - Day 10
• Castaway's Journal - Day 11
• Castaway's Journal - Day 12-1
• Castaway's Journal - Day 12-2
• Castaway's Journal - Day 12-3
• Castaway's Journal - Day 13
• Castaway's Journal - Day 15
• Castaway's Journal - Day 18
• Castaway's Journal - Day 22
• Castaway's Journal - Day 25
• Castaway's Journal - Day 29
• Castaway's Journal - Day 30
• Castaway's Journal - Day 32
• Castaway's Journal - Day 38

sections.heading: Zoe Rayne
sections.text:
These diaries belong to a skilled hunter and researcher, detailing her encounters with rare creatures, her theories about the ecosystem, and even her own personal reflections. They're a great resource for uncovering secrets about the wild Palworld inhabitants.
• Zoe Rayne's Diary - 1
• Zoe Rayne's Diary - 2
• Zoe Rayne's Diary - 3
• Zoe Rayne's Diary - 4
• Zoe Rayne's Diary - 5

sections.heading: Lily Everhart
sections.text:
Lily is a talented botanist and alchemist, leaving behind diaries filled with notes on the diverse flora of Palworld, their medicinal properties, and even recipes for creating powerful potions. These diaries are crucial for aspiring Palworld herbalists and potion masters. 
• Lily Everhart's Diary - 1
• Lily Everhart's Diary - 2
• Lily Everhart's Diary - 3
• Lily Everhart's Diary - 4

sections.heading: Marcus Dryden
sections.text:
This explorer delved deep into the ruins and ancient technology of Palworld, offering insights into the history of the land's previous inhabitants and the advanced technology they left behind. His diaries are invaluable for those seeking to understand the world's mysteries.
• Marcus Dryden's Diary - 1
• Marcus Dryden's Diary - 2
• Marcus Dryden's Diary - 3

sections.heading: Victor Ashford
sections.text:
Victor was a passionate engineer and tinkerer, recording his experiments with Palworld's technology and crafting unique tools and machines. His diaries provide valuable blueprints and inspiration for enterprising players who want to build and create.
• Victor Ashford's Diary - 1
• Victor Ashford's Diary - 2
• Victor Ashford's Diary - 3

sections.heading: Axel Travers
sections.text:
This lone adventurer explored the farthest reaches of Palworld, encountering rare creatures, facing perilous challenges, and documenting his journey in a single diary. It's a testament to the spirit of exploration and a source of inspiration for those seeking their own unique path in the wilderness.
• Axel Travers's Diary - 1

sections.heading: Saya Kurosaki
sections.text:
These notes, belonging to the leader of the Moonflower Clan, who zealously defended their land from outsiders. For countless ages, they have guarded the tower, unaware of its purpose, and waited for the arrival of the Crescent Moon, the herald of change, to lead them and guide them towards the end.
• Saya Kurosaki's Diary - 1
• Saya Kurosaki's Diary - 2
• Saya Kurosaki's Diary - 3
• Saya Kurosaki's Diary - 4
• Saya Kurosaki's Diary - 5

sections.heading: Bjorn Seligsson
sections.text:
Bjorn Seligsson is a resolute warrior driven by duty to defeat "the nuisance" and protect Feybreak. Bound by his ancestors' legacy, he relentlessly pursues strength, despite moments of regret. He remains committed to his mission, fighting until the end.
• Bjorn Seligsson's Diary - 1

Jumping Claw is a Dark active skill in Palworld.
It is exclusive to Loupmoon and cannot be taught to Pals via a skill fruit.
The Katana is a melee weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 44 for 2 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Katana is an excellent choice for an intermediate melee weapon due to its high damage.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Katana differs from the sword in that its only attack is a dash-style slash. It has very low attack speed comparatively.
• The ULTRAKILL update inverted the Katana and the Sword in the tech tree making it more late game.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.4.11
 ◦ Introduced.
Katress (Japanese: クレメーオ Kuremæw) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Katress is a bipedal greyish teal Pal with feline features. It has an exaggeratedly large purple witch-like hat and robe obscuring its mouth. Its ears are on top of its hat being tipped in white with the inner parts being pink. It has a black cloak with hot pink accents. Its paws are white giving the impression of gloves and boots. Its eyes are pink with small eyelashes visible.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal, being a nocturnal species, can only be found at night or as an Alpha Pal in the Sealed Realm of the Invincible. Wild Katress will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon.
In the wild large groups of Katress can sometimes be found forming a circle and performing a ritual reminiscent of ceremonies like Walpurgis Night.
If employed as a Base Pal, Katresses will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate them, as they never sleep. If not tasked with anything, they'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Grimoire Collector - When fighting together, increases dropped items from defeated Neutral Pals.
Work Suitability:
• Medicine Production 2
• Handiwork 2
• Transporting 2
Possible Drops:
• Leather
• Katress Hair
• High Grade Technical Manual

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from cat and enchantress.
• Its Japanese name may come from 暮れ kure (nightfall or darkening) and แมว mæw (cat in Thai).
• Katress was featured in Paldeck No.056 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
•

Katress Cap is a cosmetic armor headpiece in Palworld.
It provides 20 defense and 60 health, and has 300 durability. It also provides Dark Damage Reduction Lv. 1.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: • Requires Katress Cap Schematic 1, which can be purchased for 500 gold from the (Green Coat) Wandering Merchant in Duneshelter.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.

Katress Cap Schematic 1 is a schematic in Palworld.
It's needed to craft Katress Cap.
Katress Hair is a material in Palworld.

sections.heading: Use
sections.text: The Katress hair is used to make Katress Cap using the Katress Cap Schematic 1 purchased from the Wandering Merchant in Duneshelter.
Katress Ignis (Japanese:クレメーナ Kuremēna) is a Dark and Fire elemental Pal. It is a subspecies of Katress.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Katress Ignis is a bipedal, cat-like and witch-like Pal. It appears to wear a wide brimmed crimson witch hat, with loose tufts of crimson fur sticking both up and down in random spots. At the base of the hat, two cat like ears stick up from the top of its head. These ears curl inwards slightly, and are tipped with white. The inside of its ears are a sunny orange colour. Draping downwards from its head, are beige tufts of fur. The top half of its head is a beige colour, meanwhile the lower half of its head is white. It has narrowed eyes, strawberry pink in colour. It has a small, dark button nose. Its mouth is not visible. Is has a crimson red collar around its neck, which hides its mouth. It looks like it has long crimson sleeves, these sleeves tipped with white. It has white paws rather than hands. It has a beige v shape on its chest, a golden seed shaped spot at the bottom of the v. Draping down from the sides are long, dark crimson bits of fur that resemble a cloak. The ends of this cloak-like fur is tipped with a lighter crimson colour. Katress Ignis stands on two legs, its legs beige in colour and looking like it has white socks. Draping down from the middle is crimson fur attached to its cloak like pelt. This middle part is also tipped with a flame-like soft crimson.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal seems unbothered by the player's presence, but will attack when provoked.

sections.heading: Dungeons
sections.text: • Sakurajima island.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Katress Ignis was featured in Paldeck No.091 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
• Katress Ignis is the only pal with Kindling that can also perform Medicine Production.
Kelpsea (Japanese: ケルピス Kelpis) is a Water element pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kelpsea has an appearance that is superficially reminiscent of a seal, with an equine head and some dragon-like features. Its body is light blue, with a cream-colored stomach. It is Identical to Kelpsea Ignis, except for its coloring.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Tends to run away unless attacked. This Pal can be mostly found swimming in shallow waters.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill
• Aqua Spout - While in team, increases attack power of Water pals.
Work Suitability:
• Watering 1
• Farming 1
Possible Drops:
• Raw Kelpsea
• Pal Fluids
Farming Produce:
• Pal Fluids

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Kelpsea was named by the community during the 3rd Name This Pal event.
• Its Japanese may partly come from Kelpie, this being a mythological shapeshifting horse that inhabits lakes from Irish and Scottish folklore.
Kelpsea Ignis (Japanese: マグピス Magpis) is theFire element variation of Kelpsea.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kelpsea Ignis takes an appearance similar to a seal, with an equine head and some dragon-like features. Its body is red, with a cream-colored stomach. It is identical to Kelpsea, except for its coloring.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is usually found in groups around streams of lava, normally exhibiting a pacific behavior towards the player.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Missing Information
