Kerauno is an Electric active skill in Palworld.
It is exclusive to Orserk and cannot be taught to Pals via a skill fruit.

sections.heading: History
sections.text: • Prior to 2.5.0, it would just hit one enemy and deal direct damage. The patch also added the effect of some electric bolts that shoot out from the target and deal damage unfortunate enough to be in their wake, making it an (somewhat unpredictable) AOE attack as well.
Kikit (Japanese: コロマジロ Koromadillo) is a Ground Elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kitkit is a small, armadillo-like Pal. It has a round head and oval-shaped body, with four short and stubby legs. It has white circles for eyes, these white circles having black horizontal lines in them that makes the Pal appear tired and asleep. Its body is mostly a soft beige colour, having a mango orange plate of armour on its head. Its ears curve outwards a bit, these ears tipped with a darker mango orange. On its back, it has two plates of the light orange armour, the plates lined with a darker orange. On the sides of these plates are dark orange diamond shapes. It has a short and stubby mango coloured tail.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is passive until provoked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Sakurajima

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Its name is pronounced “Kick It”.
Killamari (Japanese: ヤミイカ Yamiika) is a Dark and Water element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Killamari resembles a floating periwinkle-coloured squid. It sports short frilly arms around the circumference of its body, and two long tentacles extending from the sides of its body ending in darker purple tips. Its dark purple mantle is shaped like a beanie with two ears sticking out of the sides, similar to a flapjack octopus; the top of the mantle and ears are in a wavy periwinkle pattern. It has big, circular, shiny pink eyes with purple pupils. Underneath the ghost-like body lies a circular series of razor-sharp teeth in place of a cephalopod's beak.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal is a nocturnal species that can only be found inside dungeons. 
If employed as a Base Pal, Killamari will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds as it never sleeps. If not tasked with anything, it'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.
While its partner skill doesn't allow for true flight, it should still be an option to consider for an early-game player who wishes to get a relatively stamina-efficient method of air travel as soon as possible, at least until access to a flying Pal that can actually serve as a mount is gained.

sections.heading: Dungeons
sections.text: • Windswept Hills.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Fried Squid - While in team, can be summoned and used instead of a glider. Can float for long periods of time while gliding. (Requires Killamari's Gloves→ Unlocks at Lv. 9)
Work Suitability:
• Transporting 2
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Venom Gland

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words calamari and kill.
• Its Japanese name may come from 闇 yami (darkness) and イカ ika (squid).

images.caption: The underside of a Killarmari, seen by gliding with it.

Kindling is one of the work suitability skills a pal can have in Palworld.
Pals with this ability are great at powering fire stations. These stations allow the player to cook food or smelt minerals. Pals with Kindling will also light unlit torches inside the base.

Kingly Slam is a Neutral active skill in Palworld.
It is exclusive to Kingpaca and cannot be taught to Pals via a skill fruit.
Kingpaca (Japanese: アルパオー Alpaō) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kingpaca is a giant overweight alpaca, who's fluffy cream-colored wool is covered in a red royal robe with golden buttons and a golden pattern. Atop its head it wears a three-pointed golden crown. It's short legs are tipped with red hooves, the St colour is it's ears and robe.

sections.heading: Location
sections.text:
Kingpaca can be found as an Alpha Pal northwest of the Small Settlement, and is always being escorted by 2 Melpaca. They are usually passive if not attacked, but the whole entourage becomes aggressive once any of them is struck. 
Wild Kingpacas can be found in the No. 2 Wildlife Sanctuary.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Can only be found as an Alpha Pal.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• King of Muscles - While in team, Kingpaca helps carry supplies, increasing the player's max carrying capacity by 100. Can be ridden (Requires Kingpaca Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 22).
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Wool

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words king and alpaca.
• Its Japanese name may come from alpaca and 王 ō (king).
• Kingpaca was featured in Paldeck No.040 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: A player riding on the back of a (named) Alpha Kingpaca.

sections.text: Kingpaca Cryst (Japanese: アオパオー Aopaō) is the Ice element variation of Kingpaca.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kingpaca is a large fluffy alpaca, sporting a blue royal robe adorned with ice buttons and wearing a majestic three-pointed crown made out of ice.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Kingpaca Cryst can only be found as an Alpha Pal inside the caves of the Forgotten Mineshaft, unlike Kingpaca, it is always alone without the company of Melpaca.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Found as an Alpha Pal inside the caves of the Forgotten Mineshaft.

sections.heading: Utility

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:

• Its Japanese name may come from 青 ao (blue) and アルパオー Alpaō (Kingpaca's Japanese name).
Kingpaca Saddle is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 21 Technology item and requires 2 points to unlock after catching a Kingpaca. It is crafted in the Pal Gear Workbench.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used to unlock Kingpaca's Partner Skill, King of Muscles, which allow players to ride it, with increased max carrying capacity.
Kitsun (Japanese: シラヌイ Shiranui) is a Fire element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kitsun is a fairly large canid-like creature with white fur and various light blue markings, such as on the forehead, paws, behind the eyes, the tips of its mane, and most of the enormously thick tail, which is as long as the Pal's own body. Its most notable feature is a luminous, electric blue ring of fire wreathed around the neck, acting like a mane, positioned in front of its actual mane of thick white fur. A wing-like tuft of fur sprouts from the base of each thigh. There are chrome-gold plates and bands placed behind its jaws/cheeks and worn on the lower legs, respectively.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
It is known to be very anxious. While it can be intimidating on the surface and were once considered bad luck, its actually quite timid, and is known to flee into caves when approached. That being said, it is anything but weak, and it will defend itself if harassed into a corner, so players should wake one up at their own risk.
While this Pal can only be found at night, for some reason, they will be more commonly found sleeping than they will be roaming around. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon.
Despite being considered a nocturnal species, if employed as a Base Pal, they will actually sleep during nighttime, and thus will need a bed to accommodate them, perhaps a side effect of not being a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Frostbound mountains at night.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Clear Mind - Can be ridden. Unaffected by the cold or heat while riding this Pal. (Requires Kitsun Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 30)
Work Suitability:
• Kindling 2
Possible Drops: 
• Normal
 ◦ Flame Organ
 ◦ Leather

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• The English name comes from kitsune, the Japanese word for "fox", but with the E at the end removed to possibly emphasize the Pal's "blue sun" motif.
 ◦ Its codename, "AmaterasuWolf", reinforces this sun motif, as Amaterasu is the Shinto deity of the sun, who was famously depicted as a wolf in the cult classic videogame Ōkami, this association of Amaterasu with wolves has to do with wordplay using the Japanese word for wolf (狼 Ōkami) which is homophonous to the Japanese word for great god (大神 Ōkami).
• Its Japanese name, シラヌイ Shiranui (literally "unknown fire" or "phantom fire"), references a type of ghost fire or will-o'-wisp commonly reported off the coasts of the Japanese island of Kyūshū, specifically in the skies above the Ariake and Yatsushiro Seas. In Japanese culture, ghost fire is often depicted as being blue or purple in colour.
• Kitsun was featured in Paldeck No.054 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Kitsun Noct (Japanese: カゲヌイ Kagenui) is a Dark elemental Pal and a sub-species of Kitsun.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Kitsun Noct is a bulky, fox like Pal. On its head, there are two midnight purple marks in the shape of a v. It has two big ears in the shape of triangles that point outwards, the inside of the ears a raw salmon colour. It has narrowed golden eyes, with midnight purple semi circle marks under its eyes. On its cheeks, with has bright yellow fur that sticks outwards. Floating around its neck is a ring of magenta and purple flame. The fur on its head is a coal black colour. It has a dark coal black mane that's tipped with a muted purple colour. It has bulky legs, the legs tipped with a dark royal purple. Around the front legs only are two gold bands. On the upper portion of its hind legs are dark coal black swirls that end with two tufts. These tufts are tipped with a dark violet colour. It has a long tail that starts thin but gradually gets thicker the closer it is to the end. The end of this Pal's tail is a dark violet colour.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Kitsun Noct is a rather timid Pal that flees upon seeing the player. However, it will retaliated when attacked.

Knocklem (Japanese:ヤクモマル Terraknight) is a Ground elemental Pal. It was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Knocklem is a knight golem Pal, described in its Paldeck entry as mechanical in nature. Its appearance resembles a living suit of armor, with large gauntlets and pauldrons and a large red plume on its head. The large floating ring behind its back resembles wings when seen from the front, and during Knocklem's signature attack, is removed and thrown like a boomerang.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Knocklem can be encountered in the wild exclusively as an Alpha Pal.

sections.heading: Active Skills
sections.text: Steel Guardian Mode when activated increases Knocklem's attack and defense

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its English name is a Amalgamation of Knock and Golem.
• Knocklem was featured in Paldeck No.094 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Ladder is a structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It it used to climb a highter place.
Lamball (Japanese: モコロン Mokoron) is a Neutral elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Lamball, as their name suggests, is a spherical pal resembling a lamb. They are bipedal and have short stubby arms and legs and shiny golden eyes. The body and long ears of Lamball are a greyish brown with white wool enveloping their body. On the top of a Lamball's head features two small orange horns. They have two gold gems on their chest similar to buttons which, combined with their wool, give off the appearance of a parka.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Lamballs are curious of the Player when they approach it. If attacked, they will attack back with its signature Roly Poly Active Skill.

Large Feed Bag is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.text:
• Technology Tier 26
• Average Feed Bag
• Three (3) Ancient Technology Points.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Crafting the Large Feed Bag increases the number of different types of food storage slots from 2 to 3.
Useful for having a primary food source you have a lower quantity of, and two backup foods like Baked Berries,
Alternatively, having a high-quantity food in the first slot allows you to use the second, and third slots as additional Backpack space for a food of higher Satiety.

sections.heading: Drawbacks
sections.text:
Things to consider when using multiple food sources at the same time include
• Weight; how much does all food combined add to my total carry weight?
• Priority; Which food do I want my character, and my pals, to consume first. keeping in mind Pals and Player eat automatically when hunger reaches ≤50%, so if you have a high Satiety food in the first slot, your party may consume foods too early to fully appreciate the satiety value of mid to late-game recipe foods.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text: • Baked berries in the first slot mean your party will consume the least valuable food first.

The Large Incubator is a Structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 47 technology item and requires 5 ancient technology points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: The Egg Incubator is used to hatch eggs found through out the world or through breeding at . Eggs come in Common, Rocky, Verdant, Damp, Electric, Dragon, Frozen, Dark and Scorching varieties. Each of these is associated with the matching type of Pal. Eggs of each type can also come in Large and Huge varieties which hatch rarer Pals.

The Large Toolbox is a structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text:
The Large Toolbox - once researched and constructed - improves the Handiwork Speed of ALL handiwork capable Pals within the base it has been built in.
Increasing the Handiwork Speed allows Pals to construct new structures and perform crafting tasks faster.
NOTE: Placing more than one has no additional effect.

sections.heading: Researching and Unlocking
sections.text:
Before the Large Toolbox can be built, it must first be unlocked within the Pal Labor Research Laboratory inside the Handiwork Research Tree under "Large Toolbox Development"
Once researched, it will appear within the Players Technology Tree at Player Level 20 and will cost 2 Technology Points to fully unlock for use.
After both researched and unlocked, it can then be built within a Players Base for the following cost:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The Large Toolbox comes fully equipped with several tools, such as Pliers, Hammers and even Socket Wrenches, yet only Hammers are used by Pals and Players when constructing.

Leezpunk (Japanese: ダリザード Dalizard) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Leezpunk is a bipedal reptilian pal resembling a purple lizard. It has a layer of black skin on its body resembling a hoodie with a v-shaped marking on the front. It has a spiky yellow crest giving the appearance of a mohawk. It has large yellow eyes with small pinprick pupils. It has claws that are red. Leezpunk has a long tail with four spikes running along and has a red underside.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Found naturally in the south-western parts of the map, but also as part of various raids. Wild Leezpunk can be aggressive or may flee away from the player.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Leezpunk seems to be able to order other Pals to work.
Partner Skill:
• Sixth Sense - When activated, utilizes its sixth sense of detect nearby dungeons. (This skill has to be activated manually (60 s cooldown). It will scan an area of 100 m (on lvl 1) around the pal and show dungeons on the map for 60 s. Scanning the whole area takes ~7s).
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 1
• Gathering 1
• Transporting 1
Possible Drops:
• Copper Key
• Silver Key

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from lizard and punk.
• Leezpunk was featured in Paldeck No.024 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: A Leezpunk transporting some wood by carrying it on its shoulder.

Lifmunk (Japanese: クルリス Kururisu) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Lifmunk is a pal taking on the appearance of rodents such as squirrels and chipmunks. Their body primarily consists of a light greenish yellow color. The tail of Lifmunk is large and bushy and is tipped with white. Their ears, whiskers and collar all take on the appearance of green leaves with the right ear having a distinct notch giving the appearance of a bitten leaf. Their shiny eyes are a bright red-orange, and they have a large gem on their forehead to match. Underneath their collar, there can be seen two red berries attached to their chest, possibly resembling cherries.

sections.heading: Behavior

Lifmunk Effigy is a collectible in Palworld.
These curious collectibles, scattered like emeralds across the map, whisper tales of the past and beckon you to uncover their power.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
• Boost Capture Rate: Collect Effigies and offer them at the Statue of Power to increase your ability to capture wild creatures. The more you offer, the stronger your taming prowess!
• Unlock Stat Upgrades: As your Capture Rate levels up, you'll gain access to additional stat upgrades for enhanced monster-snatching skills.

sections.heading: Locations
sections.text: See Map:Palpagos Islands for an interactive map with marked locations.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
• They're particularly easy to find at night when their green glow is easily visible from far away.
• Don't limit your search to obvious spots. Check behind waterfalls, atop rocky peaks, and even within ruins. They can also be found on ground levels, sometimes hidden within crevices.
• Reach level 6 and unlock the Statue of Power to build your own monument to turn these in and increase your capture abilities.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 11 Technology item and requires 1 point to unlock after catching a Lifmunk. It is crafted in the Pal Gear Workbench.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is used to unlock Lifmunk's Partner Skill, Lifmunk Recoil, which allows players to have the pal ride on their heads while firing at enemies.

Lily Everhart is the leader of the Free Pal Alliance and owner of the grass Pal Lyleen. Her tower is located in the Frostbound Mountains in Verdant Brook.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Lily is a fair skinned woman with violet eyes. She is barefoot and has short white hair with light green streaks. Her fingernails are dyed green. She wears a green toga with golden lines. Beneath the toga, she has a black brassiere with golden lines and has a golden belt with blue stones on her waist. On her arms, she has long chiffon sleeves that end with golden flared cuffs. On her forehead, she has a crown that resembles a branch with the left side protruding upwards, adorned with rubies that are meant to be fruits. The leaves on the branch have different colors and are colored white, green and light green. She has a single diamond shaped earring on her left ear. She also wears an anklet on her right leg with a blue line that runs through the middle of it and a metal thigh strap on her left leg.

sections.heading: Biography
sections.text:
Lily Everhart detests humans despite being one herself and holds a narrow view of her fellow humans. She sees them as selfish and savage monsters who only care about themselves, because of how some of them treat the Pals badly. She reveres Pals in a very fanatical religious way, believing humans to be beneath them. To her, Pals are the true upholders of civilization since the ancient days of the island. She views eating Pals as blasphemy against god, for that she doesn’t eat them.
She strongly opposes the use of Pal Spheres, seeing them as tools of enslavement and a major failure in human history. She believes they exploit and enslave Pals, and she can’t see the relationship between humans and Pals as anything more than master and slave when they are used. To her, using Pal Spheres to capture Pals is the greatest shame in human history. She believes that Pals and humans should live and work together as equals.
Since Lily despises the use of Pal Sphere, Lyleen is not bound to one, but shows up when Lily needs her help. Lily adores Lyleen and has an unbreakable friendship with her. She calls Lyleen “queen” as a show of respect and adoration. To her, Lyleen is her only light in a world she finds sad and dark.
The two formed their strong bond after an event in which Lyleen was being hunted and attacked by Rayne Syndicate poachers. During a very foggy day, Lily was taking her usual walk by the berry fields, which is her favorite activity to do, when she suddenly heard the voices of the Rayne Syndicate poachers. She investigated the source of voices and saw them using their Pal Spheres on Lyleen, who resembled a vivid lily flower, in attempt to catch her. Lily decided to protect Lyleen from the poachers and help her, so she attacked the poachers from behind and killed them using her spear, but not before they managed to wound her. She then destroyed all the remaining Pal Spheres they brought with them.
Not wanting to taint Lyleen with her bloodstained hands from her wounds, she retreated back to her base to bandage herself from the shots she received during the fight with the poachers. When she arrived there, she found Lyleen waiting for her. Lyleen reached out, touching Lily’s arm healing her wounds as a thanks for helping her with the poachers. From that day on, Lily and Lyleen become companions who work together.
Because of her beliefs, she established and upholds the five traditional principles of the Free Pal Alliance (FPA). These principles forbid the mistreatment of Pals, which include not eating, overworking, abusing, or using Pals for experiments. They also encourage people to risk their lives to protect Pals.
Lily discovered Wildlife Sanctuaries with rare Pals living there. She found it unusual that none of these Pals inhabited the main island. She theorized that these Pals might need a specific environment to survive, or perhaps their natural predators inhabited the main island, making the sanctuaries a safer place. Another possibility she considered was that these Pals were unable to cross the sea. Although she was uncertain why they only existed there, Lily wanted to protect them, fearing their potential extinction.
However, the FPA duties in guarding the forest kept them occupied. She was especially worried about the Rayne Syndicate poachers, who might establish a hideout on the islands to exploit it and endanger the existence of these rare Pals.
As much as she hates requesting the help of another faction because of their disregard for the FPA ways, she decided to put the Pals' protection over her hate and differences. She decided to approach the Palapagos Islands Defense Force (PIDF) to seek their assistant in protecting the Pals on these islets. She made a contract with the PIDF leader, Marcus Dryden, to protect these islets. As per the contract, Marcus honored the contract between them and stationed the PIDF officers there to protect the Pals on these islets.
The contract with the PIDF made Lilly aware that some of her followers might not be completely faithful to the principles of the FPA, which had been hidden from her. What made her suspicious was how they were able to bring in large amounts of money for the contract, claiming it was from raiding the Rayne Syndicate poachers’ camps after killing them and taking their profits. She found this strange and suspected that they might be involved in other activities.

sections.text:

• Lily's Spear is the highest damaging spear in the game and second highest damaging melee weapon, just behind the Sword.

Locker is an item in Palworld.
It's a Metal Shelf Set item.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: You can store items in Locker. It has 20 slots.

Players can unlock treasure chests by picking the lock rather than using a key. To pick a lock, the player must maneuver a lockpick to find the correct position before turning the lock. If the player turns the lock too much while the pick is in the wrong position, the pick will break. Although the player has an unlimited supply of lockpicks, the game continues in real time, leaving the player exposed to danger while attempting to pick the lock.

Loupmoon (Japanese: ツキカゲ Tsukikage) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Loupmoon bears a slight resemblance to a bipedal honey badger with huge bat-like ears, a collar of white fur enveloping the neck and chest, three red sharp claws on both of its hands and feet, bright blue eyes, and a pair of large black horns on its head that are both shaped like sickles or crescent moons. The head is rather spherical, the blue nose is very small, and the mouth hides a pair of sharp fangs within. The aforementioned ears are flexible and coloured red on the inside. The tail is rather short and bears a white tip. The legs are digitigrade, and have three spur-like protrusions emerging from the ankles. Loupmoon's whole body is a uniform dark blue-to-black in colour. It averages 170-180 cm tall at the head, not including the ears or horns.
Its overall silhouette is similar to that of a fellow Dark-element Pal, Incineram.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Being a nocturnal Pal, Loupmoon can only be found at night or in dungeons. It will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon. This Pal is likely to attack the player when approached.
If employed as a Base Pal, Loupmoon like other nocturnal Pals, won't need sleep and will continue working or roaming around the base through the night.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Spawn during the night.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Claws Glistening in the Dark - When activated, attacks targeted enemy with a powerful Jumping Claw.
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 2
Possible Drops:
• Bone

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Loupmoon may be derived from loup (French for wolf) and moon.
• Its Japanese name may come from 月 tsuki (moon) and 影 kage (shadow).
• Loupmoon could be partly based in the Moon rabbit and a Jackalope.
• Loupmoon was featured in Paldeck No.018 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Loupmoon Cryst (Japanese: ユキカゲ Yukikage) is an Ice elemental Pal and a sub-species of Loupmoon.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Loupmoon Cryst is a tall, bipedal Pal resembling a honey badger. It has two dark blue horns on its head, the horns resembling crescent moons. It has wide, pointed ears, the inside on its ears a muted dark blue colour. Its face is a ice blue colour, looking like a mask compared to the rest of its head, which is a snow white colour. It has narrowed crimson eyes, and a smirk on its face. It has long, fluffy chest fur that is white in colour, the fur reaching mid torso. It has along snow white arms, with tufts of fur on its shoulders and elbows. It has three long, sharp muted dark blue claws on each paw. It has a slim body, and thin legs. Its body is snow white in colour, with a ice blue underbelly. It has three tufts of fur on its lower legs, its feet having three short yet sharp muted dark blue claws. It has a short, fluffy snow white tail.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Loupmoon Cryst is an hostile and aggressive Pal that will attack the player on sight.

Lovander (Japanese: ラブマンダー Lovemander) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Lovander is a rather... provocative Pal in its appearance, to put it nicely. It is a tall, bipedal salamander averaging 3 meters tall, assuming the player avatar is around 180 cm. It is often found in various shades of pink, while the lower jaw, under-neck, and stomach are white in colour. The head features an elongated snout and jaws, which houses a visibly long red tongue. The top of the head and nose has a magenta-coloured stripe that goes between the two long diamond-shaped ears, which leads into a set of long, draping hair or veil of the same colour that reaches down to the waist. The wrist cuffs, two flat ribbon-like tails, heart-shaped puff that covers the chest, and the flat heart piece that covers the inner groin, are also this magenta colour. The inside colour of the tails and heart piece are purple.
The creature as a whole is rather lithe and thin, save for the thighs, which are well-developed for running and pursuing the targets of its affection. Lovander has sharp purple eyes with eyelashes, three fingers on each hand, and three toes on each foot, which appear to be digitigrade.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal is a nocturnal species, meaning it can only be found at night (outside of raids), though unlike Grintale, they can usually be encountered roaming around instead of sleeping. In any case, they are usually found alone or in a pair. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon. Perhaps unsurprisingly, they are fast runners, and are adept at hit-and-run attacks. 
Some of the animations, such as the idle pose with a hand on the hip and weight shifted slightly, are purposely meant to be seductive in nature. Lovander also has a unique expression for when it is petted by the player.
And no, a player's Lovander won't actively chase other Pals around, despite its preview video and what the Paldeck entry states.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Can be found in the deserts at night. Can also be found in the dungeons in Mount Obsidian.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Heart Drain - While fighting together, grants the player and Lovander the life steal effect which absorbs some of the received damage and restores HP.
 ◦ The healing effect is maxed out at 6% of Lovander and the Player's damage converted into health when 4 starred. High damage pellet weapons like Shotguns provide the most healing in one attack.
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 2
• Medicine Production 2
• Transporting 2
• Mining 1
Possible Drops:
• Mushroom - (2-4) 100%
• Cake - 1%
• Suspicious Juice - 1%
• Strange Juice - 1%
• Memory Wiping Medicine - 1%

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Lovander Paldeck Entry No.069, is an adult joke.
• Their name may come from the words love and salamander, or possibly from lavender.
• Lovander is the only non dark pal which is nocturnal; meaning they naturally spawn during the Night, do not need to sleep and will continue to work in Bases day and night
• One of the possible items that Lovander may drop is a cake; this may be a reference to how the word "cake" may be used as slang for "booty", likely referencing the Pal's behavior.
 ◦ Additionally, cake is required to breed Pals, another reference to this Pal's "debauchery".
• Lovander was featured in Paldeck No.049 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Lucky Pals are rare pals that can be found throughout the world. 
They emit a shining or sparkling sound and are surrounded by glowing, star-like particles but they will no longer emit the sounds and particles after they're captured. 
The chance of a Pal being Lucky is currently unknown.

images.caption: The ingame Survival Guide picture of a Lucky Gumoss

sections.heading: Key Differences
sections.text:
Lucky Pals are slightly different from regular Pals: 
• The Lucky Pals icon is displayed next to the Lucky Pal's name in most interfaces. 
• Lucky Pals will always come with the "Lucky" passive trait, which gives +15% Work Speed and +15% Attack.
• They are larger than regular Pals, akin toAlpha Pals.
• Catching or killing a Lucky Pal awards Ancient Civilization Parts.
• They always have one random non-exclusive Active Skill.
• They have a higher level than their non-lucky variants in the area, making them considerably stronger.
• The Lucky Pals icon currently exclusive specifically to wild Pals with the Lucky passive. 
• A Pal that inherits the Lucky passive through Breeding will not be larger, and will not have the Lucky Pals icon.
• A Pal that inherits the Lucky passive and is also an Alpha will look the same as a wild Lucky Pal, being both Lucky and Larger. However, they will have an Alpha Pals icon by it's name instead of the Lucky Pals icon.

sections.text: Lullu (Japanese: フワフラワ Fuwaflower) is a Grass Elemental Pal. It was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Lullu is a plant pal with a petal-like dress that's covered in green, yellow and orange stripes. It has a white petal-like midriff with a plant-like bowtie and wears a puff crown that has a tulip-shape piece at the center with orange and yellow stripes.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn

Work Suitability:

Possible Drops: 
• Normal

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Lullu's name may come from lull and lulu (A common name to mean a few things). It may also come from hula (In Hawaii it means to express see, hear, feel, taste, touch and smell in a form of dancing).
Lumbering is the ability to chop trees.

sections.heading: Partner Skills that affect Lumbering
sections.text:
There are a handful of pals that increase the efficiency for the player when it comes to lumbering.
• Gumoss - While in team, improves efficiency of cutting trees.
• Eikthyrdeer - Can be ridden. Can perform a double jump while mounted. Improves efficiency of cutting trees.
• Eikthyrdeer Terra - Can be ridden. Can perform a double jump while mounted. Improves efficiency of cutting trees.
• Mammorest - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of cutting trees and mining ores while mounted.
• Mammorest Cryst - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of cutting trees and mining ores while mounted.
Lunaris (Japanese: ミステリア Mysteria) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Lunaris is a humanoid Pal, seemingly resembling an anthropomorphic mixture of multiple creatures, such as hares and felines. It has a smooth, silvery-white complexion throughout, with its body being rather small and willowy in proportion to the rest of it, and featuring a small cyan jewel embedded on the upper chest. Its long limbs appear to be sectioned at the shoulder and pelvis joints, with the legs appearing rather tubular in shape and ending in conical points instead of feet, while the arms start out thin but become bulbous upon reaching the hands, which have three fingers each. Lunaris has a fairly round head, the front-center of which is marked with a dark blue circle that touches three certain points: its large cyan-coloured eyes and a forehead-embedded cyan jewel as big as said eyes. From the back of the head are two very large ovaloid-shaped ears (or what can be perceived to be ears) that are as long as the Pal itself, and end in conical tips. The top of the head also features what could be horns, or a second set of ears, which are conical in shape.
Accenting various extremities of the silvery-white Pal are dark blue markings with light blue tips; these are found on the fingers and both pairs of ears. Lunaris seems to average roughly 140-150 cm in height.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Lunaris perpetually levitates above the ground; however, it can't truly fly and seems to suffer similar pathing problems over complex structures and rough terrain on occasion, as if it were a grounded Pal.

sections.heading: Dungeons
sections.text: • This Pal can be encountered as a level 32 Alpha Pal living in the southwestern part of the map, inside the Sealed Realm of the Esoteric.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Antigravity - While in team, Lunaris manipulates gravity, increasing the player's max carrying capacity.
 ◦ 80 weight added at level 1 and does stack with multiple
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
• Transporting 1
• Handiwork 3
Possible Drops:
• Paldium Fragment

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Lunaris was named by the community during the 2nd Name This Pal event.
 ◦ "Luna" is latin for "Moon", which is likely the reason for this Pal's celestial motif.
• Its Japanese name seems to come from the english word Mysterious.
• Within the game files, Lunaris is referred to as "Mutant". There exists data for an unreleased or unfinished Pal dubbed "Dark Mutant", which may or may not be intended to be a Lunaris subspecies, but any speculation regarding any relation between the two pals is unconfirmed and should be considered baseless until further information appears.
• Lunaris was featured in Paldeck No.064 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: Lunaris seen in the title screen.
thumb|A Lunaris sleeping over a Fluffy Pal Bed by levitating over it.

sections.text: Lyleen (Japanese: リリクイン Lilyqueen) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Lyleen is a floral Pal that takes on a humanoid appearance, being primarily green with orange eyes. Leaves atop its head form a hairdo, two of these leaves extend over its shoulders resembling pigtails, while two orange hibiscus flowers adorn the base of each pigtail. A darker green midsection and collar accent the slender torso. 
On the lower body It has a large frilly skirt resembling a ball gown composed of 3 layers, the top layer of the skirt is made up of various leaves tipped with dark green that encircle its waist, and below them, large droopy white petals form the middle layer leaving an opening at the front, this gap allows the tan-colored innermost layer of the skirt to peak in between.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal's non-hostile behavior only becomes aggressive after being provoked by the player.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • No. 3 Wildlife Sanctuary

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:

Work Suitability:

• Gathering 2

• Innovative Technical Manual

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• All wild Lyleen has the Spirit Emperor Passive Skill. However, except the Lyleen hatched through an egg from Mossanda and Petallia.
• Lyleen was featured in Paldeck No.065 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Lyleen Noct (Japanese: ルナクイン Runakuin) is the Dark element variation of Lyleen.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Lyleen Noct's appearance is similar to Lyleen's but with a different coloration. It has pale blue leafy hair adorned with teal hibiscus flowers. Its body is white with a gold-accented midsection and a golden collar. The innermost layer of the skirt is pale blue, large dark blue petals form the middle layer, with the top layer of the skirt having various gold-tipped blue leaves.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Lyleen Noct is non-hostile until the player is within line of sight, at which point she is hyper aggressive. Lyleen is often facing the back of the cave on spawn, will not see player first enter the cavern.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn

sections.text: • At minimum health, a Legendary Sphere has a Lyleen Noct base Catch rate of 7%. This number First Rolls at 20% with Max player level and Max Effigies achieved.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: Likely an abandoned Ammo as the Assault Rifle Ammo is used in all Full-Auto Weapons in Palworld.
Magnum Ammo is a type of ammo in Palworld.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: Likely a Holdover from previous Development Builds where there may have been multiple Handguns planned, but were ultimately scrapped in favor of the more generic Handgun Ammo.
The Makeshift Assault Rifle is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 31 for 2 Technology Points.

sections.heading: History
sections.text: ◦ Introduced.
The Makeshift Handgun is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 25 for 3 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Makeshft Handgun is a pistol designed for close quarters combat. Though it deals much less damage per shot than the Musket, it can fire 6 times before needing to reload, making it a perfect sidearm for early-game players. However, since the Makeshift Handgun and Musket both share the same ammo type, being Coarse Ammo, one must keep an eye out on their ammo counter lest they find themselves without ammunition for either weapon.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Makeshift Handgun's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Fenglope boss.
• Contrary to its in-game description, the Makeshift Handgun can fire all 6 bullets in its magazine before needing to reload.
• The Makeshift Handgun can be purchased from a Wandering Merchant in Fisherman's Point for 16600 Gold Coins.

Mammorest (Japanese: グランモス Granmosu) is a Grass and Ground elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mammorest is a large, quadrupedal proboscis Pal that serves as a reference to a woolly mammoth. Its body is mostly brown, and thick green grass covers the top of its body. It walks on four legs, which each one bearing a stone brace. It has two tusks made out of stone by its face, and there are two spikes by the corners on top of its body.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Mammorest is neutral, and won't attack the player unless they attack first.
While Mammorest has established habitats where it's more common to find, these Pals can be encountered roaming all over Palpagos islands. On certain occasions a pair of wild Mammorest can be seen fighting to the death, if there's no external intervention both Mammorest will likely end up passing away at the end of the conflict from the damage received.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Gaia Crusher - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of cutting trees and mining ores while mounted. (Requires Mammorest Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 28)
Work Suitability:
• Planting 2
• Lumbering 2
• Mining 2
Possible Drops:
• High Quality Pal Oil 
• Leather
• Mammorest Meat

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This Pal's name is a portmanteau of the words Mammoth and Forest.
• Its Japanese name may come from grand and mammoth or moss.
• Mammorest and its subspecies currently boast the highest base HP stat of all Pals in the game.
• Mammorest was featured in Paldeck No.006 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Mammorest Cryst (Japanese: ブリザモス Blizzamosu) is an Ice and Ground elemental variation of Mammorest.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mammorest Cryst appears as a giant, snow-covered mammoth, with icicles acting as tusks. They also have ice in their backs, ankles and toenails. Aside from their white fur, they have a brownish skin under it.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Mammorest Cryst is neutral, and won't attack the player unless they attack first.

• Mammorest and its subspecies currently boast the highest base HP stat of all Pals in the game.
• Mammorest Cryst was featured in Paldeck No.085 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Maraith (Japanese: ソルレイス Solraith) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Maraith is certainly a morbid-looking creature, bearing a black lion-like body with a massive, flared mane and purple pointed feet, which may possibly be meant to be hooves. The sickly pale face of this Pal is featureless and hollow, save for two piercing green eyes emanating from within. Positioned directly behind the mane are a pair of skeletal wings with a membrane made of blue-purple fire, and more of the same ghostly fire is present above its head and on the tip of its tail, which resembles a bony brazier.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal, being a nocturnal species, can only be found at night or in dungeons. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon.
If employed as a Base Pal, Maraiths will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate them, as they never sleep. If not tasked with anything, they'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Messenger of Death - Can be ridden. Applies Dark damage to the player's attacks while mounted. (Requires Maraith Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 23)
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 2
• Mining 1
Possible Drops:
• Bone
• Small Pal Soul

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• The Pal's name might be a combination of "mare", a type of demon said to induce nightmares or sleep paralysis by sitting on the sleeper and applying their weight on them, and "wraith", one of many synonyms for a ghost, spectre, phantom, etc.
• It could be based on a number of possible things, such as the manticores of Persian mythology, the helhest of Danish folklore, or general depictions of death omens and psychopomps.
• Maraith was featured in Paldeck No.072 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Maraith Saddle is a key item in Palworld. 
It is a tier 23 Technology and unlocks Maraith's Partner Skill.
Marcus Dryden, leader of the Palapagos Islands Defense Force (PIDF) and owner of the fire Pal Faleris. His tower is located in the Desiccated Desert in the northeast.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Marcus is a tall, well-built man with light tan skin, brown eyes and brown hair with blonde streaks. He has a two-toned beard that is brown with black streaks.
He wears a white shirt with the sleeves rolled up underneath his black vest, which has a thin chain hanging on it on his right side along with a badge.
His black pants have two chains hanging from both sides of his belt and he wears black chain-embellished shoes. A long scarf with a geometric pattern is draped on the sides under his long, dark military coat with a fur collar. There are two stripes on the sleeves of the coat.
He wears two ring earrings in his left earlobe. All his fingers have rings on them and he wears a single bracelet on his left arm and three on his right arm.

sections.heading: Biography
sections.text:
Marcus Dryden is a proud, arrogant, and intelligent man who is also a master manipulator and schemer. Duneshelter has a severe issue with people using stims and because of how severe it is, Marcus give up on even trying to control it and instead decided to exploit it for his benefit by selling stims. These stims would be sold to those addicts and the Palapagos Islands Defense Force (PIDF) would be sent to arrest and fine them. They confiscated the stims, which then go back to Marcus, who sells them again. He doesn’t allow anyone to sell stims without his permission and anyone who is caught are dealt with.
Despite Marcus exploiting the stims issue to increase his wealth, his intervention benefited the area greatly, allowing the villagers to live more safely. Before his arrival, the place was crawling with criminals, who made the life for the villages a daily struggle between life and death. One of the villagers mentioned that they couldn't go about their daily lives without the constant fear of being mugged or killed until Marcus arrived and got the PIDF involved. The villagers are grateful to him for that. The PIDF are also dispatched to protect settlements from the Rayne Syndicate’s raids on them around the islands, and they investigate their activities, such as the operation they run on their oil rig.
The only one who Marcus genuinely cares about is his partner, Faleris. He has a close relationship with Faleris that is built on mutual trust, as they both view each other as partners and watch out for each other's backs. Marcus enjoys Faleris' company, keeping him out of his Pal Sphere to talk to him.
Marcus and Faleris are not different from each other, as they both view the island as a playground and both like playing in their own way.
Faleris is free to roam around and if he spots an intruder, he will attack, kill, and eat them without Marcus even being around to give the command. Since he is under Marcus’s protection, he does not face any consequences for his actions.
For instance, when Faleris ate a Rayne Syndicate thug early that day, he looked at Marcus expecting a treat, but Marcus had none on him and instead told Faleris he could eat the white sand if he wanted, which he always provides for his partner and which Faleris enjoy greatly.
Marcus does not have issue working with other faction leaders. Lily, the Free Pal Alliance (FPA) leader, after discovering the Wildlife Sanctuary’s islets, made a contract with Marcus so that the PIDF will protect these areas. This contract also made Lilly aware that some of her followers might be involved in suspicious activities she was not aware of, because of how they were able to bring large amounts of money for the contract, claiming it was from raiding Rayne Syndicate poachers’ camps.
Marcus honored the contract with Lily and stationed the PIDF officers there to guard the Wildlife Sanctuary’s islets as per the contract.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Marcus is one of the original five tower bosses that were first released alongside Axel, Zoe, Lily and Victor.
• Marcus and Faleris are the first tower boss with aerial battle.
• Marcus' Faleris is a male.
• Marcus made Faleris the PIDF symbol.
• Marcus seems to trust Faleris greatly, as they both watch out for each other's backs like partners. This trust extends to the point that Marcus would not mind if Faleris were to eat him one day.
• It's unclear if the White Sand Faleris eats is a drug. If it is, then it is unknown whether it has the same effect on Pals that it has on human beings. It is possible that it's not different from when the player use potions on their Pals.
 ◦ It’s presumed the White Sand are crushed Cinnamoth scales. If so, it might just be another source of food or a treat for Faleris, as Marcus hasn’t mentioned any side effects for it other than Faleris enjoying it. However, a side effect is mentioned for humans in Cinnamoth Palpedia entry. This mirrors how animals can eat certain things safely while humans can’t due to health risks.
• In the Japanese version and within the game files, Faleris is called "Horus". Horus is the flacon-headed Egyptian god of vengeance, who avenged the death of his father, Osiris, by killing his uncle, Set.
 ◦ In the English version, it is referenced in-game when Marcus Dryden calls his Faleris "Horus" in their boss intro screen.
• It's possible that Marcus and Faleris are a reference to Gears of War characters Marcus Fenix. Marcus' partner Faleris is a phoenix, but this could be a coincidence.

Mau (Japanese: ニャオテト Nyaotet) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mau appears as a small black cat, with a pair of gold stripes or bands on each ear, lower leg, and its tail. It has a thick gold collar that is hollowed out with several circular holes along the circumference, square-shaped gold markings along its shoulders, and a large bell-like ornament at the end of its tail that is shaped like a sistrum. Its bright green eyes have gold eyeliner, and it has a small gold nose and brows.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal can only be encountered inside dungeons or imprisoned in the encampments of a faction. 
A very timid creature. Mostly likes to run around and play. Most suitable use is for its ability to retrieve gold passively. Recommended to use its ice-type variant as it gives cooling as well as gold.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: In the dungeons in Windswept Hills.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Gold Digger - A passive skill that will cause Mau to dig up coins when assigned to a ranch
Work Suitability:
• Farming 1
Possible Drops:
• Gold Coin

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This Pal was named by the community during a Name This Pal event.
• Mau's name and Egyptian theming references the Egyptian Mau cat breed.
 ◦ Mau is referred to as "Bastet" within the game files. The end of its tail being shaped like a sistrum, an ancient Egyptian musical instrument, reinforces this further, as the Egyptian patron deity of cats is often depicted with one.
 ◦ The name is also an onomatopoeia of cat meowing in several languages.
• Its Japanese name may come from ニャオ Nyao (onomatopoeia for the cry of a cat) and Bastet.
• Mau was featured in Paldeck No.041 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Mau Cryst (Japanese: チルテト Chilltet) is the Ice element variation of Mau.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mau Cryst is a medium sized cat-like Pal. It has small triangle ears, baby blue in colour with two dark blue horizontal stripes on both ears. On the middle of its head is a white, diamond like crystal. It has narrowed golden eyes, its pupils black slits. On the top of the eyes are muted blue lash-like marks, with dark blue smudges above their eyes. Its nose is a dark blue colour, the nose shaped like an upside down triangle. It has a downturned mouth. It has a white, diamond like collar around its neck with holes in it. The rest of its body is a soft, light baby blue colour. It has long, thin legs, and a long, thin tail Around the lower half of its legs are two horizontal dark blue stripes. On its shoulders are three dark blue squares. On the tail tip, it has a white, diamond like charm on the tail. Around the end of the tail are two dark blue horizontal stripes. The charm on the tail is oval shaped, with a hollow middle. In the hollow middle is a circle.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Unlike Mau, this Pal can be found roaming in the wild outside of dungeons. Mau Cryst are normally passive and may run away from the player when approached.

sections.text: The Meat Cleaver is a melee weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 12 for 2 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Meat Cleaver is used for butchering the user's Pals, letting them obtain their drops once again after catching them. Though this action can be quite lucrative, especially if the Pal being butchered is an Alpha and will drop valuable items, Pals that are butchered will die permanently. As such, the user must be very careful when selecting which Pals they want to kill.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• To butcher a Pal, press "4" (default key) to bring up the Pal command menu with the Pal you want to butcher with the Meat Cleaver in hand. On the uppermost tab of the menu it should then have the option to butcher that Pal (this option should replace the "Pet" command that would instead appear without the cleaver).
• Butchering Alpha Pals can be used as a method to obtain legendary-tier Schematics much faster, since the Alpha Pal will drop the same pool of items again after being butchered. For example, if you don't get the Schematic you want after catching an Alpha Pal, you can butcher it for a second chance at getting it. However, since legendary-tier Schematics have low drop rates, chances are you'll have to butcher the Alpha Pal several times before you get it.
• Though the Meat Cleaver can be used as a weapon, it actually deals less damage than a Stone Spear, making its combat capabilities near non-existent.

Medical Supplies is a consumable in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: They can be crafted at Medieval Medicine Workbench and Electric Medicine Workbench. You can also buy them from Wandering Merchant with gold coins.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: They are used to cure Pals of Ulcers and Fractures. To treat a Pal you must feed it to them directly, they cannot be placed in a feed box.

It does not absorb fall damage and starvation damage. When the player is not attacked for [25 seconds?], the shield recharges itself automatically.

Melpaca (Japanese: メルパカ Melpaca) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Melpaca is a tall, alpaca like Pal. It has medium height rabbit like ears that point up. These ears are strawberry pink in colour, with dark blue insides. Its face is strawberry pink in colour. Its nose is a small dot, dark blue in colour. It has black oval shaped eyes with dark red lights in them. It has a long neck, its head, neck and body covered in cloud-like white wool. It has a thin strawberry pink tail, and four long, slender legs. It has white legs, with strawberry pink boot-like parts on its legs. It has dark blue triangles that point upwards on the bottom of its legs, dark blue stripes around its legs. On its legs, it has two strawberry pink tufts on the back of each leg.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Melpaca normally travel in groups of two or maybe three. These Pals are timid, and flee upon seeing the player.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Pacapaca Wool - Can be ridden. (Requires Melpaca's Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 7) Sometimes drops Wool when assigned to Ranch.
Work Suitability:
• Farming 1
Possible Drops:
• Wool
• Leather

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words marshmallow, mellow, and alpaca.
• Melpaca was featured in Paldeck No.025 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

sections.heading: Usage

Memory Wiping Medicine is a Consumables in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
Its obtained by crafting using the Electric Medicine Workbench by using 99 Beautiful Flower, 50 Horn, 50 Bone, and 50 Pal Fluids. The crafting time is also incredibly long, even longer than Cake.
It can be also obtained rarely from defeating Lovander.

sections.heading: Use

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: In v0.1.3.0, this item had a critical bug that caused the Capture Power to reset without returning the Lifmunk Effigy statues used, making the loss permanent.
Menasting (Japanese: デスティング Deathting) is a Dark / Ground element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Menasting is a scorpion-like Pal with a predominately black shell accented by purple segments. Its tail ends in a scythe-shaped stinger and the back of the abdomen is engulfed in blue flames.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Menasting is a hostile pal and will attack the player when spotted.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
• The Menasting Alpha is located in the Desiccated Mineshaft, in the southernmost part of the Desert (493, 79).
• No.2 Wildlife Sanctuary.
• Deserts in Sakurajima at night

sections.heading: Utility

Work Suitability:
• Lumbering 2
• Mining 3
Possible Drops:
• Coal
• Venom Gland

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may be a play on the word menacing, as a scorpion has a stinger, and this Pal is menacing.
• Its Japanese name may come from death and sting.
Menasting Terra (Japanese: ゴルディング Golding) is a Ground Elemental Subspecies Pal. It is a Sub-Species of Menasting.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Menasting Terra is a large, scorpion-like Pal. it has a pair of, long huge claws. The inside of these beige claws is a sandy yellow colour. Around the base of the claws is a gold band. It has a stubby build with sharp, claw like legs. it has around four beige legs tipped with a rusty gold colour. It a four segment beige tail that hangs over its head. It has a long rusty gold stinger on the end of its tail, the tail looking deathly sharp. It has narrowed bright red eyes, and no visible mouth. Running down its back is what looks to be golden fire. On its head, its has a rusted gold crest.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is hostile and will attack the player character on sight.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Menasting Terra can be encountered in the wild exclusively as an Alpha Pal in the northen desert in Sakurajima island.
The Meowmere is a strong melee weapon in palworld that can also fire projectiles to attack enemies. It’s a technology tier 30 unlock and requires 2 ancient technology points to unlock.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Meowmere is a legendary rarity melee weapon that can be unlocked when the player reaches level 30. When used in battle, it fires a bouncing projectile that can be used in place of a basic ranged weapon such as a bow. However, more advanced ranged weapons such as the compound bow and Assault Rifle may surpass the ranged damage of the Meowmere. The Meowmere can also be used to attack trees and ores, though it is considerably less efficient than Pickaxes and axes. The Meowmere is a highly recommended melee weapon early game as it provides both melee and ranged advantages.

sections.heading: Crafting & Availability
sections.text:
The Meowmere can be crafted at most weapon workstations, including:
• High quality workbench
• Weapon workbench
• Production assembly line
• Production assembly line II
• Weapon assembly line
• Weapon assembly line II

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Meowmere is similar to the legendary Meowmere, which is a potential drop from the moon lord raid boss added in the tides of terraria update to the game.
• the Meowmere plays a special sound effect when used

images.caption: The legendary Meowmere, which is a very similar weapon to the Meowmere
Tides of terraria update when the Meowmere was released

Metal Structure Set is a set of structures in Palworld.
It's a tier 30 technology item and requires 4 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Like the other foundation sets, once unlocked, one can then build 8 basic structures, this time for metal. These include walls, foundation, roofs, stairs, and a door.
Like other structure sets, all structures need a form of contact with the ground. Walls and doors can be built when vertically connecting to a roof or foundation. If this foundation is broken, all walls connected to it will break if there is no additional gripping point above or below them (and drop their ingredients), even if they are touching another wall.

The Mill is a production structure in Palworld.
Requiring Tier 15, This structure costs Two (2) Technology Points to unlock.
A Mill can be used to process harvested or purchased Wheat into Flour. The process requires a watering pal to take place. The higher tier Work Suitability in Watering the pal has, the faster Wheat is processed into Flour.
It is possible to toggle Pal transport, and a pal can now take the finished Flour and store it in a container for later use. Preferred storage being Cooled by a Pal, to prevent the Flour from expiring before getting used.
Mimog (Japanese: ミミドッグ Mimidog) is a Neutral elemental Pal. It was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mimog is a mimic-like pal that has a black treasure chest with light blue markings and a pair of golden ears. Also on the inside is pitch black with the exception of a pair of glowing blue eyes and a tongue that's sticking out from the lid.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Is static when found in the wild. When attacked or attempted capture, will run off very quickly and disappear.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
Almost all around Palpagos Islands. Mainly central of the islands.
Tip: When attempting to capture a wild Mimog you may surround it using the build item "Wooden Defensive Wall," as it can be built anywhere with nothing but wood, to trap the creature before making the attempt. Even on failure the Mimog will remain inside it's pen that you've made and you can safely whittle down it's HP before trying again.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Unlock - When near a treasure chest, Mimog can open the chest without using a key.
The type of treasure chest that can be opened depends on the skill level.
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops: 
• Normal
 ◦ Gold Coin
 ◦ Dog Coin
 ◦ Training Manual (XL)

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Mimog's name is a combination of mimic (A type of D&D enemy that resembles a voracious chest) and dog,
• Normal Mimogs grant the same amount of XP as an Alpha/Lucky pal upon defeat.
• Mimog was featured in Paldeck No.098 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: A player attempting to catch a disguised Mimog.

sections.text: Mind Control Meds is a Consumables in Palworld.

sections.heading: Usage

Mine is a defense structure in Palworld.
is a trait of a Pal that allows it to mine various resources.

sections.heading: Facilities that use Mining
sections.text:
Base structures
• • • • • • Field resource nodes/deposits
• Lv. 1
 ◦ Stone
 ◦ Paldium Fragment
• Lv. 2
 ◦ Ore
 ◦ Sulfur
• Lv. 3
 ◦ Coal
 ◦ Pure Quartz
The Stone Pit will only give stone. The resource nodes drop their respective item when mined. A stone deposit also has a chance of dropping Paldium Fragments when mined. Resource nodes will get depleted after mining some amount from it.
Some areas in the world have groups of 5 to 10 ore deposits. This is a nice location to mine large amounts of ore.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.text:
Mining can be done in multiple ways:
• Letting Pals with mining skills work at the Stone Pit
• Causing a certain amount damage to rocks.
 ◦ Punching rocks with your fist or a pickaxe
Any damage done to a rock can cause rocks to drop it's mineral. Pals with mining skills assigned to the base will target rocks with their attacks, causing them to drop it's material. These items can be picked up by Pals with a transporting skill.
Tip: Mammorest's Earth Impact attack causes large area effect damage to rocks. This is especially effective when you are surrounded by ore deposits. Obtaining the Mammorest Saddle and rotating between different Mammorest Pals in your party lets you quickly mine multiple ores.
You can also find and pick up stones found on the ground.

sections.heading: Types of Pickaxes
sections.text:
• Stone Pickaxe
• Metal Pickaxe
• Refined Metal Pickaxe

sections.heading: Partner Skills that affect Mining
sections.text:
Some Pals have partner skills that will aid the player in their mining endeavors. Such as increase the yield of ores, make them less heavy or track them down.
• Rushoar - Can be ridden. Increases efficiency of destroying boulders while mounted.
• Fuddler - When activated, generates vibrations to detect nearby ore.
• Surfent Terra - Can be ridden. While in team, Surfent Terra helps carry supplies, decreasing the weight of ores in the player's inventory.
• Digtoise - When activated, performs Shell Spin, follows the player while spinning, and mines ores efficiently.
• Reptyro - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of mining ores while mounted.
• Reptyro Cryst - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of mining ores while mounted.
• Mammorest - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of cutting trees and mining ores while mounted.
• Mammorest Cryst - Can be ridden. Improves efficiency of cutting trees and mining ores while mounted.
• Astegon - Can be ridden as a flying mount. Increases the damage dealt to ores, and you can obtain more Ore while mounted.
• Kikit - While in team, Kikit helps carry supplies, decreasing the weight of Crude Oil in the player's inventory.
• Xenovader - While in the team, increases damage dealt to ores, and it's easier to obtain Meteorite Fragment.
• Xenogard - Can be ridden. While in the team, it's easier to obtain Meteorite Fragment and Pure Quartz.
• Smokie - When activated, uses its sharp sense of smell to detect nearby Chromite. While fighting together, a large amount of Chromite is obtained.

The Moonflower Clan is a ninja clan that resides on the island of Sakurajima, zealously defending their home from outsiders. Saya, the leader of the clan, was waiting for the arrival of the Crescent Moon, Selyne the herald of change the one who will bring a new dawn for the land. She accepts the change that is coming and sees the Crescent Moon as a sign of a new beginning and freedom from darkness who is guiding them towards the end.

sections.heading: Members
sections.text:
• Saya Kurosaki - Leader 
• Moonflower Genin
• Moonflower Jonin
Moonflower Genins are a type of enemy affiliated with the Moonflowers.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Moonflower Genins are found exclusively in Sakurajima, appearing in small groups that patrol random points on the island. Armed with katanas and fast on their feet, the Genins will relentlessly pursue you once they spot you, and they're able to attack by dashing forward and performing a wide swipe with their weapons. Despite being melee attackers with no long-ranged attacks, the Genins' dashing ability makes it dangerous to fight them at close range, so it's best to try picking them off from a distance. Notably, once a Genin performs his attack, it takes him a moment to regain his stance and start chasing you again, which leaves you a small window to land some hits on the Genin if you manage to dodge his attack.
Moonflower Jonins are a type of enemy affiliated with the Moonflowers.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Moonflower Genins are found exclusively in Sakurajima, occasionally appearing alongside a small group of Moonflower Genins as they patrol random points on the island. Much like their weaker counterparts, Moonflower Jonins wield katanas and are capable of performing a dash attack that ends with the Jonin swinging his katana in a wide arc. Generally, Jonins can be dealt with by keeping a fair distance from them, using ranged attacks to pick them off without being caught in their dash attacks, and dealing as much damage as possible to them during the brief moments where they leave themselves vulnerable after attacking.

Moonlight Beam is a Neutral-elemental Active Skill.
It is exclusive to Selyne and cannot be taught to Pals via a skill fruit.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text: Selyne's exclusive skill. Stores energy in the moon on its back, then fires a crescent-shaped beam forward.

sections.heading: Learnset
sections.text: List of pals that learn it through level up and which level.
Mossanda (Japanese: ササゾー Sasazō) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mossanda is a large, bulky, panda-like Pal. It has round, short black ears that sit on the top of its head. Its head is covered by a sage green mask, that ends with leafy triangles. On the top of its head is a dark moss green leaf that points upwards. It has a white snout, its nose being black and a bit oval shaped. Around its eyes are black circles. Its eye sclera is white, and its eyes are circle shaped. Its eyes are a dark grey colour. It has long black arms, with three white fingers on each hand. On the back of both hands are seed shaped lime green marks. The upper torso of its body is white in colour, with two downturned lime green leaves that resembles a bow. The lower half of its body is black in colour, with two white lines on the feet, making it look like paws.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Frequently sighted in the wild roaming in pairs or alone, it has an aggressive temperament, attacking players that enter its proximity.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Grenadier Panda - Can be ridden. (Requires Mossanda's Grenade Launcher→ Unlocks at Lv. 24)
• Can rapidly fire a grenade launcher while mounted.
Work Suitability:
• Planting 2
• Handiwork 2
• Lumbering 2
• Transporting 3
Possible Drops:
• Mushroom
• Leather
• Tomato Seeds

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Their name may come from the words moss and panda.
• Sasazou may come from 笹 sasa (bamboo grass) and 増 zou (increase).
• Mossanda was featured in Paldeck No.030 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: Fight vs Mossanda "Guardian of the Forest"
A Mossanda enjoying the Hot Spring.

sections.text: Mossanda Lux (Japanese: ライゾー Raizō) is the Electric element variation of Mossanda.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mossanda is a large, bulky, panda-like Pal. It has round, short black ears that sit on the top of its head. Its head is covered by a cheese orange mask, that ends with leafy triangles. The bottom of the leafy-like mask ends with a darker orange colour. On the top of its head is a electric yellow thunderbolt-like mohawk that points upwards. It has a white snout, its nose being black and a bit oval shaped. Around its eyes are black circles, with blunt tops that makes it look angry. Its eye sclera is white, and its eyes are circle shaped. Its eyes are a dark grey colour. It has long black arms, with three white fingers on each hand. On the back of both hands are seed shaped lemon yellow marks. The upper torso of its body is white in colour, with two downturned cheesy orange leaves that resembles a bow. On top of this 'bow' is golden thunder bolts on both sides. The lower half of its body is black in colour, with two white lines on the feet, making it look like paws.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal is hostile.
Likes to charge its attacker then bursting electricity in it vicinity. Highly advised to recall Pals during its choreographed lighting strike attack as it can splash all 3 strike in one location instantly killing Pals After the strike, resummon and continue. Also, because of its massive size it will walk through every open fire within your base.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
• Two islands northeast of spawn island just northeast of the Natural Bridge fast travel point (Alpha). 
• In the dungeons in Mount Obsidian.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Grenadier Panda- Can be ridden. (Requires Mossanda Lux's Grenade Launcher→ Unlocks at Lv. 25) 
• Can rapidly fire a grenade launcher while mounted. 
Work Suitability:
• Generating Electricity 2
• Handiwork 2
• Lumbering 2
• Transporting 3
Possible Drops:
• Mushroom
• Electric Organ

Mount Obsidian is a region in Palworld.
It makes up most of the volcanic island in the southwestern part of the Palpagos Islands. The area is made up of a lot of steep cliffs and lava, making it sparse of both pals and other beings.

Mounted Crossbow is a defense structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Setup
sections.text: To have a functional Mounted Crossbow, you need at least a Handiwork Pal, and a storage container with Arrows in it that a Pal can path to from the Mounted Crossbow. Then you'll need to manually assign a Handiwork Pal to the Mounted Crossbow, automatic work mode will not work, and Players cannot use it.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: The assigned Pal will collect Arrows from nearby storage to load the Mounted Crossbow. While the Pal is using the crossbow, they will shoot wild Pals that are both within the Palbox base radius, and in front of the crossbow.
Mounted Machine Gun is a defense structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Setup
sections.text: To have a functional Mounted Machine Gun, you need at least a Handiwork Pal, and a storage container with Rifle Ammo in it that a Pal can path to from the Mounted Machine Gun. Then you'll need to manually assign a Handiwork Pal to the Mounted Machine Gun, automatic work mode will not work, and Players cannot use it.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: The assigned Pal will collect Rifle Ammo from nearby storage to load the Mounted Machine Gun. While the Pal is using the gun, they will shoot wild Pals that are both within the Palbox base radius, and in front of the gun.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: The mounted machine gun appears to be a M2 Browning despite having the label M5 Machine Gun as imprinted under the serial number on the right hand side.

Mozzarina (Japanese: ミルカルビ Milkalbi) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Mozzarina is a large, cow like Pal. It has a circle shaped head and body. Its head is mostly white, with flappy ears that point downwards. The inside of the ears are a raw salmon pink colour. It has closed eyes, and a relaxed smile on its face. This smile has two, long sharp fangs on the sides of its mouth. On the top of its head are two horns that point upwards, the horns a dark beige colour. The horns have two thin, horizontal black stripes on them. On the right side of its head is a black patch, the right ear black in colour. Its legs are short and stubby, its hooves being a navy blue, almost black colour. It has a large black spot on its back, a black spot on its sides, and a black spot on the right front leg. Its udders are just visible underneath its body, the udders a raw salmon colour.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: In the wild this Pal is normally non-hostile unless provoked.

The Musket is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 21 for 3 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Musket is an early-game gun that has good range and deals great damage per shot. It can deal with most enemies around the user's level with relative ease, especially if the user has good aim and consistently lands headshots. However, the Musket can only fire once before needing to be reloaded, and its long reload time means that using it in close quarters combat generally isn't the best idea.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Musket is the first gun players can unlock.
• Due to its single-round capacity and long reload time, it is highly recommended to bring a backup weapon such as a Crossbow in the event that you are unable to reload your Musket.
• The Musket's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Quivern boss.
• The Musket's reload time can leave you vulnerable, so use cover to your advantage.
• The Musket fires Rifle Ammo.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Musket Schematic 3 possible drop from defeating or capturing Quivern Alpha, and is required to craft the Uncommon variant of the Musket.

Musket Schematic 3 possible drop from defeating or capturing Quivern Alpha, and is required to craft the Epic variant of the Musket.
Musket Schematic 4 is a possible drop from defeating or capturing Quivern Alpha, or a Gold Chest on the Oil Rig, and is required to craft the Legendary variant of the Musket.

Nitemary (Japanese: ナイトメアリ Nitemary) is a Dark elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Nitemary is a tall, bipedal rabbit like Pal. It has two long black ears that sit atop of its head, the ears pointing upwards like rabbit ears. It has a light blue-white head, with golden eyes. The fur around its eyes makes it look like its wearing eyeliner. It has slitted black pupils, and a small black nose. Flowing from its head is long, muted cyan hair-like fur, with a cyan curl that falls on its forehead. The long flowing hair fades to a purple colour. Around its neck, it has spiked black fur that resembles a collar. It has black arms, with raw salmon pink lines that resemble paw lines. Holding in its left hand, is a floating ball of fire that is its soul. The fire is both cyan and purple. It looks to wear a dress made of its own fur, the dress beige in colour and tipped with black triangles that point upwards. It has small, black goat-like feet, and a light cyan tail that's shaped like a circle with two tufts of fur on it.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is passive until provoked.
