sections.text: Nitewing (Japanese: ホークウィン Hawkwin) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Nitewing has 3 colors in its body those being: dark grey, red and white. The main part of its body is the dark grey you will find it on the top of its wings, on the top of the head, its flank (the lower side of its body) and the beginning of his tail. The white feathers can be found on the ends of the wings, the belly, breast and throat and the middle part of the feathers on its head. The red feathers can be found on the end of the tail, its beak and at the ends of the longer feathers on its face.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Wild Nitewing can be often seen soaring the skies never descending to the ground, except at night for sleeps. It typically ignores the player unless provoked, at which point it becomes aggressive, though Alpha Nitewing can become aggressive when approached.
Additionally, Nitewing can appear shortly after the demise of a Pal or human to scavenge the remains.

Nox (Japanese: ルナティ Lunati) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Nox resembles an ambiguously vulpine-, canine-, or feline-like creature with pale lavender-coloured fur and large red eyes. Its cheeks and mouth area, as well as the fuzzy collar around the neck, are white in colour, as is the tip of its tail and paws. It wears a dark purple cape on its back with a lavender trim, secured on the front by a large purple gem on its chest. The inside of the ears are also a dark purple.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal, being a nocturnal species, can only be found at night but is rare to find. It will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon. It is generally solitary and may run away from the player until attacked. There is actually a small chance of encountering Nox soundly slumbering in crevices or caves during the daytime.
If employed as a Base Pal, Nox will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate it, as it never sleeps. If not tasked with anything, this Pal will simply wander about the base playfully. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Kuudere - When fighting together, applies Dark damage to the player's attacks.
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Leather
• Small Pal Soul

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• The term "Kuudere", used for the name of their partner skill, refers to a common character archetype in Japanese media. A Kuudere is a character who appears to not have emotions. They are often stoic and expressionless, and remain calm in stressful situations.
• The name Nox is the Latin word for "Night"
 ◦ Its Japanese name, Lunati, possibly comes from the English "lunatic" or "lunacy", which in Latin literally meant "moonstruck", but carried the meaning of "demented, madness, of unsound mind" since the 4th-5th centuries, due to the medieval belief that certain phases of the moon induced epilepsy, madness, and various psychological disorders in people.
• Nox was featured in Paldeck No.079 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Nyafia (Japanese:ニャニミテ Nyanimite) is a Dark Elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Nyafia is a tall, bipedal cat-like Pal. It has a mauve coloured head, with two long ears that point upwards. The inside of the ears is a magenta colour. It has loose, spiky magenta fur that falls over its left eye, concealing it. On its cheeks, it has the long tufts of fur. For its visible eye, its eye is wide and gold in colour. It has a thin, cat like smile on its face. The Pal appears to wear a dress, the dress long sleeved and black in colour. The dress has a magenta coloured fur collar, that flows back. Around the sleeves of the dress, it has spiky magenta fur. The paws in the sleeves are mauve in colour with three long, white and sharp claws on each one. It has long, slender mauve coloured legs that are tipped with black. It has a long, mauve coloured tail with three thick black lines on it. The tail is tipped with spiky magenta fur.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal becomes hostile when the player is spotted

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Spawns in various locations around Feybreak island. Nyafia are hostile and will attack the player once spotted.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Nyafia could possibly be A combination of "Nya", the sound A cat makes in Japanese and "Mafia", a term used for a Criminal Organization.
Oil Rig Stronghold is a location in Palworld.

sections.heading: Location
sections.text:
The Rayne Syndicate Oil Rig is located on the southeast of Marsh Island's waypoint.
It can be reached from Marsh Island waypoint located at 430, -345 by a flying Pal, but there is a chance it may get downed, so you should also bring a swimming Pal in case that happens while getting to the Oil Rig located at 538, -421. 
It's recommended to be at least level 50 before attempting to get to the Oil Rig because the enemies will be attacking using their turrets and firing lasers from the towers on it before you reach it. You will also be facing high level Rayne Syndicate and their Pals on it. 
Once you get on it, you will have to deal not only with Rayne Syndicate and their Pals but also the turrets. You can disable the turrets and laser towers by using the console on them.

sections.text:
Syndicate Thugs can drop Crude Oil.

• The Old Bow's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Kingpaca boss.

sections.text: ◦ Introduced.

Old TV is decorative furniture in Palworld.
It's a technology tier 41 item and part of the Amusement Furniture Set.
Omascul (Japanese: オーメン Ōmen) is a Dark elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Omascul is a medium-sized, owl-like Pal. With the exception of a white heart-shaped mask on its face and a bright red diamong on its chest, its fur- or feather-pattern appears to be in the shape of a hooded black cape. Its head has two ear-like points, and two triangular markings sit above and below its yellow eyes. It has a long neck and body atop two long gray feet, and a pronged tail tipped in gray.
It doesn't appear to have arms, but four spindly black arms sprout from its back whenever it prepares to attack. As it attacks, it splays its claws and flips up its "mask" to reveal a bright red mouth and a row of sharp teeth.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is passive until provoked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Missing Information.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Masquerade Dance
While in team, the Pals in the team gain more experience points.
Work Suitability: Gathering Lv. 1
Gathering is used to harvest crops at farms.
Possible Drops:
• Dark Fragment

Ore is a material in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
You can get Ore from:
• Ore rocks
• • • Some Pals can also drop Ore when captured or defeated.
 ◦ Digtoise
 ◦ Gorirat Terra
 ◦ Knocklem

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The ore as seen in game is apparently Copper, as can be seen when aiming a Pal Sphere with a Pal within at an Ore node.

Ore Mining Site is a production structure in Palworld.
It's built at one's base to let captured Pals with mining skills generate Ore.
You have to defeat the boss at the Free Pal Alliance Tower to gain the ability to unlock this building.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • As a response to player feedback about bases having to be in specific locations, the Ore mining sites were added, so players can locate their bases wherever they want.

Ore Mining Site II is a production structure in Palworld.
It's built at one's base to let captured Pals with mining skills generate Ore. It is significantly faster than the normal Ore Mining Site. It is very helpful if you need a large amount of metal, and is relatively cheap to make (but requires more space in your base and an ancient tech point). I suggest using Astegon, Anubis, or Knocklem to mine with it. This can assist with making refined ingots, cutting down on needing to farm things except for coal and quartz until you unlock their sites. I recommend Faleris or Jormuntide Ignis, or maybe even Blazamut/Blazamut Ryu to smelt all the ore.
Orserk (Japanese: ボルゼクス Boruzekusu) is a Dragon / Electric element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Orserk is a bipedal dragon pal with black scales marked with yellow zig-zag patterns on its forearms, waist, and back of neck. A pair of yellowish scythe-like horns protrude from its head starting at its eyes.It has a white, seemilngly tuffted, chest and a white underside excluding its waist line. Its legs are yellow and look boot-like in apperance and has 3 claws on each foot with 3 yellow spike-like protrusions on the back of each of its legs. Each arm has three fingers and three claws aswell and a pair of wings that seem to be made of electricity. Its tail also has 2 parts that seem to be made of electricity.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is usually aggressive towards the player.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Rarely found in No.3 Wildlife Sanctuary at all times of day.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Ferocious Thunder Dragon - While fighting together, Water Pals drop more items when defeated.
Work Suitability:
• Generating Electricity 4
• Handiwork 2
• Transporting 3
Possible Drops:
• Electric Organ - (1-2) 100%
Possible alpha/boss drops:
• Ancient Civilization Parts - (4-6) 100%
• Electric Organ - (1-2) 100%
• Precious Dragon Stone - (4-6) 100%
• Ring of Lightning Resistance - 1%
• •

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Orserk's name may come from Orca and Berserk.
• Its Japanese name may come from Bolt and the letter X, maybe referencing that its electric wings form a cross shape similar to an X.
• All wild Orserk has the Lord of Lightning Passive Skill. However, except the Orserk hatched through a egg from Relaxaurus and Grizzbolt.
• Orserk was featured in Paldeck No.080 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
• When walking Orserk will stand upright but when running it will run on all fours.

The Palpagos Island Defense Force (PIDF) acts as the self-proclaimed law enforcement of Palpagos Islands, aiming to maintain order and safety. The PIDF represents a complex and morally ambiguous faction. The enigmatic Colonel Marcus leads them, accompanied by his powerful Fire-element Pal, Faleris. While they might claim to uphold the law, their methods and motives are often questionable. Consider their actions and reputation carefully before choosing how to interact with them during your adventures.

sections.heading: Members
sections.text:
• Marcus Dryden - Leader
• PIDF Guard
• PIDF Infantry
• PIDF Elite
• PIDF Agent
• Reliable PIDF Officer (Small Settlement Village)
• PIDF Slacker (Fisherman's Point Village)
• Sensitive-Nosed PIDF Officer (Fisherman's Point Village)
• Burly PIDF Officer (Dessicated Desert)
• PIDF Grunt (Duneshelter Village)
• PIDF Goody Two-Shoes (Duneshelter Village)
• PIDF Bonfire-Lover (Duneshelter Village)
• Cautious PIDF Officer (Marsh Island)

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: • PIDF enemies that spawn when the player breaks the law provide less XP than they should at their level. Therefore, it is not advised to break the law for the sake of level-grinding.
PIDF Guards are a type of enemy affiliated with the PIDF.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
PIDF Guards can usually be found in various small villages as well as on the Wildlife Sanctuaries. They will initially pay you no mind when you encounter them, but will become hostile towards you if you commit a crime in their presence, however the PIDF Guards on the Wildlife Sanctuaries will immediately start attacking you once they notice you, as entering the Sanctuaries is considered trespassing. PIDF Guards may also sometimes spawn alongside the PIDF Infantry that show up to attack you after you get caught committing a crime.
PIDF Guards are armed with Handguns, which makes them somewhat dangerous to go up against unprepared, although they're not as powerful as some of the other types of PIDF personnel you may encounter in the game. Should you decide to fight a PIDF Guard, it's recommended to have weapons and Pals strong enough to take him down quickly before he can do too much damage to you.
PIDF Infantry are a type of enemy affiliated with the PIDF.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
PIDF Infantry can be found on the Wildlife Sanctuaries, where they stand guard over the islands and attack you on sight if they spot you. Otherwise, they usually only appear after you commit a crime in front of a non-hostile NPC, spawning at your location in groups with the intent to bring you down. They're armed with Assault Rifles, making them rather dangerous to take on unless you have superior firepower to defeat them with.
Even if you can take them on, it's generally not worth the effort anyway as they give out an unusually low amount of XP, only half as much as they should for enemies of their levels.
Pal Arena is a feature for Palworld where you'll be using three Pals while fighting against another person or NPC-controlled tamer.

sections.heading: Pal Beds
sections.text: Pal beds are needed to keep a working Pal happy and raise its SAN and health as it sleeps. 1 bed of any kind is required for each pal stationed at your base.

sections.heading: = Straw Pal Bed =
sections.text:
A straw bed for Pals. Pals can rest on it when injured, or to sleep at night. It isn't soft but it's better than nothing. Unlocks at level 3.
• 10 Wood
• 5 Fiber

sections.heading: = Fluffy Pal Bed =
sections.text:
A fluffy bed that Pals can get a good rest on. Pals can rest on it when injured, or to sleep at night. Pals love sleeping on fluffy beds like this. Pals sleep more soundly, improving the amount of HP and SAN recovered while sleeping. Unlocks at level 24.
• 10 Cloth
• 30 Wood
• 5 Nail
• 10 Fiber

sections.heading: = Large Pal Bed =
sections.text:
Fluffy bed to give Pals a good rest. Pals can rest when injured or sleep at night. Even large Pals can sleep soundly on a bed like this. Unlocks at level 36.
• 10 High Quality Cloth
• 60 Wood
• 20 Nail
• 30 Fiber

images.caption: Crafting image for Large Pal Bed

Pal Dressing Facility is an item in Palworld.
It's a tier 1 technology item and is automatically unlocked, where one can add or remove Pal Skins regarding their Pals.

Pal Eggs are various eggs that can either be obtained through Breeding or found randomly hidden in the world. Each element has a corresponding egg. Eggs can be hatched by using an Egg Incubator.
Pal Eggs have a small chance to hatch as Alpha Pals, and their Pal Egg size will be "huge" if they do.

images.caption: Various different kinds of Pal Eggs.

sections.heading: Generation
sections.text:
All eggs have the nature of the Pal within determined when the egg was generated. Then the game takes that and looks on the following chart to decide what the egg looks like.
How it chooses a species/sub/species of Pal differs somewhat based on where the egg came from. Breeding just gives you whatever the result of breeding is. Eggs you pick up seem to choose the species of Pal based on the map location where you pick it up. For example: the Mount Obsidian area mostly gives you Fire types.

sections.heading: Common Egg
sections.text: Within Common Eggs you'll find Neutral Pals.

rows.t: A gentle spirit throbs within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.heading: Scorching Egg

rows.t: A blazing spirit burns within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.heading: Damp Egg

rows.t: A tranquil spirit sways within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.heading: Verdant Egg

rows.t: A stronge life force dwells within.
A verdant spirit grows within-as if a monster is about to born.

sections.heading: Electric Egg
sections.text: Within Electric Eggs you'll find Electric Pals.

rows.t: A raging spirit grows within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.heading: Frozen Egg
sections.text: Within Frozen Eggs you'll find Ice Pals.

rows.t: A frigid spirit stirs within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.heading: Rocky Egg
sections.text: Within Rocky Eggs you'll find Ground Pals.

rows.t: An earthly spirit resounds within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.heading: Dark Egg

rows.t: A shadowy spirit cries within—as if a monster is about to be born.

sections.text:
Pal Essence Condenser is a structure in Palworld.
This is a tier 14 Ancient technology item that requires 2 Ancient Technology Points to unlock.
When placed, interacting with the machine allows the player to sacrifice a set number of Pals of the same species to improve a singular "base Pal". This base Pal will show blue stars to indicate that its stats have been boosted by the process. The Pal Essence Condenser will increase the base Pal's max Health, Attack, and Defense, and increase the Partner Skill level by 1, up to a maximum of 5 levels. At max stars, the Pal's Work Suitability levels will also increase by 1.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: A Pal can be condensed a total of four times, represented by four stars, with each consecutive tier doubling (except for the 2nd which quadruples) in cost:

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Pal Essence Condenser will not transfer the Active or Passive Skills of those sacrificed over to the "base Pal".
• Once sacrificed, the Pals are unrecoverable, so make sure you select the right ones to sacrifice!

images.caption: The pop-up window for the Pal Essence Condenser.

sections.text: Pal Expedition Station is a structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 15 technology item and costs 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
A Pal Expedition Station is needed to send Pals in the Palbox on Expeditions. It is also one of the largest structures that can be built in a base, and only 1 can be built per base.
Each expedition has a minimum firepower threshold for the expedition to be guaranteed success. Every individual Pal contributes firepower, and the higher a Pal's level is, the more firepower that Pal contributes. Pals that have been upgraded via souls and/or condensing contribute additional firepower. Up to 100 Pals may be deployed on an expedition. 
Each expedition takes at least 1 hour of time to complete once it is launched. If the expedition's firepower requirement is not met, the odds of the expedition being successful when time is up is reduced. If the expedition's firepower requirement is met, the odds of success will be 100%. Any extra Pals or firepower contributed to the expedition reduce the amount of time it takes for the expedition to complete. Some expeditions also require a certain amount of Pals with a certain type before the expedition can be launched.
More expeditions become available after defeating the tower bosses; once normally, and once on hard mode.

The Pal Genetic Research Unit (PGRU) is a faction dedicated to researching and developing Pals. Commander Victor Ashford, an ambitious and ruthless man, leads the group and seeks to create the ultimate Pal through genetic manipulation and selective breeding. Recently, they found success in the form of a Dark-elemental Pal called Shadowbeak, which Victor uses to fight in battle.

sections.heading: Members
sections.text:
• Victor Ashford - Leader 
• Pal Genetic Research Unit Executioner (Flamethrower)
• PAL Genetic Research Unit Executioner (Laser Rifle)
Pal Genetic Research Unit Executioners are a type of enemy affiliated with the Pal Genetic Research Unit.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: The Executioners are powerful late-game enemies only found in Astral Mountains, appearing in small or medium-sized groups that patrol various parts of the frozen wasteland. They carry Laser Rifles that let them shoot powerful energy blasts at you, dealing heavy damage if they land a hit on you. Ideally, much like most of the other ranged enemies in the game, Executioners should be fought only if you have a weapon or Pal strong enough to take them down fairly quickly, plus some strong armor to help you withstand the punishment from their attacks. Unlike the members of other factions, they don't pick fights with nearby Pals, presumably to keep them alive for research, so you can't wait for them to be attacked by a wild Pal and finish them off when at low health.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.2.0.6
 ◦ Pal Genetic Research Unit Executioners can now drop Circuit Board.
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Pal Labor Research Laboratory (PLRL) is a structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
The PLRL can be unlocked at Player Level 20 for the cost of 2 Technology Points.
Once unlocked, the structure will be available within the 2nd page of the Pal Tab.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
A Pal Labor Research Laboratory is needed for various Researches in order to improve various passive effects to improve Production and Quality of Life at a player's Base.
A Pal ability level must be equal to or greater than the research level, for exemple a pal with Handiwork Lv.2 cannot perform Handiwork Lv.3 or above Research, but a Pal with Kindling Lv.3 can do a Lv.1, Lv.2 and Lv.3 Kindling Research.
Researching is considered by the game as the "lowest priority" in the base and so pals will perform other more important tasks first. For exemple, if you have a Watering research ongoing but there is water related tasks inside your base (IE: Watering crops or using the crusher), your Pals will do the other tasks before and will only do the research if they have nothing else to do.
If you plan to idle for a time inside your base, it might be a good idea to have a Pal with a suitable ability level available (within your Party or Base) and summon it or pick up and throw the pal while aiming at the base of the research station, so they will work on research.

Pal Merchants are friendly NPCs found in villages and Small Settlement areas throughout the world of Palworld. They offer a variety Pals in exchange of Gold Coins for players starting out in the game.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
• Pal Merchants are easily recognizable by their light blue uniforms and friendly demeanor.
• They typically stand behind stalls or counters showcasing their wares.

sections.heading: Services
sections.text:
• Pal Trading: The core function of Pal Merchants is facilitating trades involving Pals.
 ◦ Buying: Spot a rare or coveted Pal you crave? Pal Merchants might have them in stock, ready to add to your team for a price. This allows you to expand your collection and find specific Pals you desire.
 ◦ Selling: You can offer up unwanted or duplicate Pals to Pal Merchants in exchange for gold coins. This lets you declutter your collection and earn some cash.
• Unique Availability: Each Pal Merchant offers different Pals, depending on their location and potential specializations. Exploring diverse settlements can unlock access to a wider variety of creatures you can acquire.

sections.heading: Locations
sections.text: Pal Merchants can be found in most villages and Small Settlement areas throughout the world.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
• Pal Merchants offer different Pals depending on their level so it's worth checking out different merchants to find the best deals.
• Don't forget that you can still capture Pal Merchants themselves. Place them in your base, and they'll transform into permanent vendors, always accessible for future Pal trading.

A Pal Sphere is the lowest tier of Pal Spheres available to newcomers on Palpagos Islands.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 2 technology item and requires 1 point to unlock. It can be crafted at Primitive Workbench, Sphere Workbench, Sphere Assembly Line, or Sphere Assembly Line II.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It is the most basic of the Pal Spheres, which are items used to capture and store Pals. It is recommended to use on low level Pals.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
• Using a Pal Sphere from behind will increase the chances of a successful capture.
• Vixy has a chance to dig up Pal Spheres from the ranch.

images.caption: A Pal Sphere successfully catching a Foxparks
A Pal Sphere on a conveyor belt
A player preparing to throw a Pal Sphere

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.2.0.6
 ◦ Icon got updated.
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.
Pal Spheres are items used to capture Pals in Palworld.

sections.heading: Obtaining
sections.text: Pal spheres can be found in the wild, dropped from enemies, dug up from the Ranch, or crafted.

sections.heading: Finding Pal spheres in the wild
sections.text: Pal spheres spawn in random locations on the ground or in chests. The type of sphere will depend on the location: higher level areas will give better spheres.

sections.heading: Pal spheres from enemies
sections.text: Some hostile NPCs will drop pal spheres. These NPCs may show up during Raids.

sections.heading: Pal spheres from the Ranch
sections.text: If the player assigns a Pal like Vixy to the Ranch, it will dig up items such as gold coins and Pal Spheres.

sections.heading: Crafting pal spheres
sections.text: To craft a pal sphere, it must first be unlocked in the Technology window. Different pal spheres require different buildings to craft them in.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
There are different types of pal spheres with different strengths. To use a pal sphere, the player must have them in their inventory. Throwing pal spheres does not consume stamina.
Higher level pals are more difficult to catch, and thus are more easily caught with a better type of sphere. You can catch Pals and Alpha bosses and dungeon bosses with it. Although it is considered inhumane, you can also catch humans with it.
If you aim at a Pal from behind, you will get a back bonus and get a higher catch chance.
Pal spheres cannot be thrown while gliding, but can be while flying or mounted, crouched or sliding, falling, or aiming with a loaded bow.

sections.heading: Types
sections.text: The Radar Sphere is currently unobtainable through normal gameplay, but may become part of the game in a future update.

sections.heading: Bugs
sections.text: There's a bug where standing on a Pal sphere after it reaches 100% causes the player to be shot up into the sky. It can give a nice view of the surrounding area, but be careful not to die from fall damage.

All Pals in this game have the exact same base stats at level 0:
• HP: 500
• Attack: 100
• Defense: 50
Every regular Pal starts with those stats. As they level-up (including level 1) they gain additional stats based on their species-specific scaling value and their individual scaling factor (IV).
They also gain 5 HP per level.

sections.heading: Individual Values
sections.text:
Each individual pal also has a random scaling factor assigned to it that increases the species-specific scaling value. This IV stat accounts for all variance between individuals within the same species.
This bonus only applies to the level-up stat gains for the Pal and does not increase the base stats.
Each of the three main stats (HP, Attack, Defense) have a different IV. This means you can have a Pal that has great HP scaling but terrible Attack Scaling, or a Pal that is exceptionally lucky with both great HP and great Attack scaling.

sections.heading: Key findings
sections.text:
• The IV is only a positive bonus. You can never have less than the base stats + minimum stat gain mentioned above. That means the lowest possible IV bonus is 0% (or 1x).
• Consistent testing proves that the maximum range of this bonus is 23%, with the minimum being 17%. Essentially, the difference between 0 IV and 100% is 20% with a margin of error of 3%

Pal subspecies, often referred to as Fusion Pals, are alternative versions of existing Pals in Palworld. These subspecies differ from their base counterparts in both appearance and elemental typing. For example, a Fire-type Pal may have a subspecies variant with Plant or Ice typing.

sections.heading: Breeding
sections.text: Subspecies can be acquired through intentional breeding. By breeding a base Pal with a specific partner, an egg containing the subspecies may hatch. This method provides a structured way to obtain these unique Pals. Here is the list of all special combinations: click here

sections.heading: Wild Encounters
sections.text:
Most subspecies can also be found in the wild. They appear in distinct areas of the map, often far from the areas where their base forms are found.
• They can spawn as regular encounters.
• They may appear as dungeon or Field Bosses.

sections.heading: Characteristics
sections.text:
• Elemental Diversity: Subspecies often have elements different from their original forms.
• Design and Themes: While retaining the core design of their base counterparts, subspecies showcase distinct thematic changes, often inspired by their new elemental affinity.
• Palpedia Completion: Subspecies have unique entries in the Palpedia, identified with a "B" suffix (e.g., 5B). This ensures collectors can track and differentiate them from their base forms.
• Naming Conventions: Many subspecies have Latin, Greek, or culturally inspired suffixes appended to their names, denoting their elemental divergence:
 ◦ Aqua: Water-type subspecies.
 ◦ Botan: Grass-type subspecies.
 ◦ Cryst: Ice-type subspecies.
 ◦ Gild: Coated in gold.
 ◦ Ignis: Fire-type subspecies.
 ◦ Libero: Retains the base form’s element.
 ◦ Lux: Electric-type subspecies.
 ◦ Noct: Dark-type subspecies.
 ◦ Primo: Neutral-type subspecies.
 ◦ Ryu: Dragon-type subspecies.
 ◦ Terra: Ground-type subspecies.

Pal Surgery Table is a structure in Palworld.
This is a tier 36 Technology item that requires 2 Technology Points to unlock.
When placed, interacting with the table allows the player to adjust the gender and add skills to the Pals in the player's party for a cost.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: A Pal can have their Passive Skills changed or change its gender. Below is the cost of adding passive skills as determined by their rarity.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• To obtain more Passive Skills to add to your Pals, you have to do a multitude of activities. One such activity is doing the Pal Arena.
• To change the gender of your pal you require a Pal Reverser that can be obtained doing the Pal Arena.

Trust is a new feature added in the Tides of Terraria update. It allows a Pal to grow stronger if high enough trust is formed between the player and the pals. 
Pals will gradually increase trust as they're in the party or can be boosted with a certain consumable.
A pal's base growth increases with the amount of trust level it has.

Palbox is a structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It's a tier 2 technology item and requires 1 point to unlock.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text:
When placed it provides you with a Base. This Base serves as a fast travel point, a respawn point, and a place you can have Pals work. You can do missions to upgrade the Base Level. For every level it's upgraded you will unlock an extra Working Pal slot. At level 10 you are allowed to place another Base. Additionally at level 15 you are permitted a 3rd base.
The Palbox has a large cube hitbox around it to prevent objects being placed/built within it as this is where one spawns when fast traveling/spawning in at the base. Note this box also extends up as well, so trying to build over it typically requires a large clearance space.
When placed, a large, circular outline can be seen to denote the range of the Palbox's influence. This outline is visible even before building the palbox, making it helpful to see if it needs to be moved or not first.

sections.heading: Palbox Management Menu
sections.text:
Caught Pals will end up in this menu. New Pals are put into the first available slot. This list can be sorted in the top right corner. This list can be seen in all Palboxes in the world.
Hovering over a Pal and clicking 'F' will show more information about the Pal, such as Work Suitability, health, Sanity, Skills, and Partner Skills (and their required gear). You can also change a Pal's name here.
Right-clicking a pal will move it to one of the Palbox's active slots. A Pal can only work at one Palbox at a time. The Pal will spawn in the hitbox behind the Palbox. Once it is in a Palbox's active slot, it will no longer be visible in the Pal list in other Palboxes. 
When Pals run out of health, they will need to be revived in the Palbox. This can be done simply by placing them into this menu. There will be a timer of 10 minutes counting down until they have been revived. Pals can also be healed by placing them here. Pals won't go hungry too, but their hunger won't be replenished either.

sections.heading: The Base
sections.text:
Pals in active slots will begin to fill out tasks around the Palbox. They can work at buildings that are within range of the Palbox.
If you look at a pal in your base, you can lift them up by pressing 'V'. While carrying a pal, you can throw them back down using 'V'. If the pal is thrown at a structure and matches its work suitability (eg. Throwing a Pal with Kindling at a Campfire), it will be bound to this structure and only focus on this task (except when eating or sleeping).
Looking at a pal in your base, you can also press '4' to open a radial menu to see more details about this Pal. Here, you can feed or pet the pal, view more details about them, add them to your party, or return them to the Box (Palbox Management Menu).

sections.heading: Upgrading the base
sections.text: Upgrading your base can make it so that you can have more bases and more pals working. Note these values are based on default/normal game settings and can be altered via the world settings.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
• Raids occur around the Palbox.
• Pals can be commanded to target enemies in the base (using Alarm Bell).
• Pals likely don't work as much when you're away from the base (or have to be loaded in first when the game starts).
• You can only respawn at palboxes (or specific areas on pre-discovered islands).

Overview=
Map=
The Palpagos Islands is the setting in which the game of Palworld takes place in.

sections.heading: Exploration
sections.text:
Palworld is an open-world survival game. The world is full of dangers such as food shortages, harsh weather, and illegal poachers.
The player can fly, swim, and explore all kinds of places on lands, the sea, and in the sky by riding Pals.
The map features a selection of huge islands offering multiple biomes, from the frozen wastelands to the desert sands, all connected by giant oceans, filled with treasure and exotic pals. 
Players can transform their gathered Pals into mounts, enhancing their exploration and transportation capabilities. Every Pal offers a unique mount experience, enabling players to traverse the game world in various ways – be it riding, swimming, flying, or gliding. This swift and secure mode of travel not only increases speed, but also ensures safety.

sections.heading: Biomes
sections.text:
The whole archipelago is divided into many named regions, with each region offering various Pals to encounter and points of interest to explore. Among them the 6 major biomes are Grass, Forest, Volcano, Desert, Snow and Sakurajima; each one corresponds to a specific Factions. Each biome is further divided into distinct regions, each has its own set of Incidents, offering a chance to encounter a fascinating array of Pals.

sections.heading: Points of Interest
sections.text: Recurring points of interest appear in the Palpagos Islands. They show up both in the game's compass on the HUD and in the game's map.

sections.heading: Fast Travel
sections.text: Fast Travel are shown both on map and compass when the area is discovered. They can be used to fast travel between other statues, bases and towers.

sections.heading: Towers
sections.text: Faction Towers are shown both on map and compass when the area is discovered. These are where the faction bosses dwell.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The name of the archipelago is probably a pun on the Galápagos Islands.

Pals are the eponymous creatures of the game Palworld. Pals help the player, willingly or unwillingly, in the farm, breeding, fighting, or working in factories, including gun factories, as shown in the trailer. Pals can also be sold or slaughtered for food; however, said action is forbidden by law.

sections.heading: Interacting with Pals
sections.text:
Pals can be interacted with in multiple ways:
• Petting: You can pet Pals to make them happy.
• Feeding: You can feed Pals manually, or they will eat from a Feed Box.
• Picking up: You can pick up Pals to carry them around or throw them at a building to have them work on it. Once you pick them up, their model ragdolls.
• Partner Skills: You can use partner skills your Pal has. Some are passive, some are weapons, and most are unlocked through crafting something at the Pal Gear Workbench. There's also Rideable Pals.
• Naming: At a Pal's summary page, players have the option to give their Pal a name, which are limited to 24 characters.

sections.heading: Fighting
sections.text:
You can fight Pals by using weapons and other Pals. Some wild Pals are aggressive and attack when approached.
Some Pals can also wield a weapon of their choice.

sections.heading: = Capturing =
sections.text: You can use Pal Spheres to capture wild Pals. This is recommended, as you get much more XP from capturing rather than killing, and you also get all drops that normally comes by defeating the pal. You are granted an EXP bonus on each capture for the first 12 times you catch a specific pal. The higher tier of Pal Sphere, the higher the chance of catching a Pal.

sections.heading: = Killing =
sections.text: The developers have said that killing Pals will be illegal within the world. Most likely, if the player character kills a Pal and is witnessed by an NPC, a bounty will be placed on the player character, who then will be pursued as a criminal. But there are no consequences if the player character manages to kill a Pal without being witnessed.

sections.heading: Breeding
sections.text: When Pals breed, the children can inherit the parents' characteristics or create completely new ones. Mixing rare species of Pals creates stronger Pals.

sections.heading: Wilderness Activities
sections.text: Pals can be used in the wilderness for certain activities. They can break rocks, cut down trees, and be used to run/swim/fly around the map.

sections.heading: Base Activities
sections.text: Pals can participate in several activities done on bases, depending on their form and abilities.

sections.heading: Work Traits
sections.text:
There are 12 different Work Traits a Pal can have. After placing a building there will be a text indicator above it to let you know what suitability is needed. Some buildings require a specific suitability to work, while others suggest a suitability to make things go faster.
• Kindling: Pals will use fire to provide heat to heaters, furnaces and cooking food. And will warm you while riding on them.
• Planting: Pals will plant seeds at plantations.
• Handiwork: Pals will help with construction and crafting at crafting stations.
• Lumbering: Pals will chop down trees and work at Logging Sites.
• Medicine Production: Pals will produce medicine at any medicine bench.
• Transporting: Pals will transport items to any storage item.
• Watering: Pals will water plantations and power Crushers and Mills. And will also extinguish structures on fire.
• Generating Electricity: Pals will generate electricity at a Power Generator.
• Gathering: Pals will harvest grown plantations.
• Mining: Pals will mine ore, stone, sulfur, and coal, as well as work at Stone Pits.
• Cooling: Pals will provide cooling to coolers and refrigerators. And will cool you while riding on them.
• Farming: Pals will produce items at a Ranch.

sections.heading: SAN
sections.text: Short for Sanity. Over time, a Pal's SAN decreases while working, which will eventually cause them to not be as productive/slack off. Pals will take breaks by themselves. At night, if a Pal bed is available, most Pals will go to sleep. However, Dark-element Pals will continue to work through the night. Certain things, like Hot Springs or particular medicines and foods, can help get their SAN back up quicker.

sections.heading: Other
sections.text:
There are also other activities a Pal can do at the base. 
• Mass production: Pals can be put to work in factories to mass-produce gear and items.
• Breeding: Pals can be assigned to Breeding Farm to breed.
• Defense: Pals can be used to defend the base from enemy raids.
• Rescuing: Pals will rescue incapacitated Pals in base.

sections.heading: Pal Variations
sections.text: Besides regular Pals, Alpha Pals, Legendary Pals, and Lucky Pals exist.

sections.heading: Alpha Pals
sections.text: Alpha Pals, also known as bosses, are huge Pal variants with titles. You can capture them.

sections.heading: Lucky Pals
sections.text: Lucky Pals are shining variants of Pals. They are larger variants of a pal and are heard from a decent distance when one spawns in. Usually recognized by a "sparkly" icon next to their name as well. When caught, they will always give you ancient civilization items and will come with the "Lucky" trait, which increases ATK by 15% and Workforce by 15%.

sections.heading: Legendary Pals
sections.text: Legendary pals are an especially rare type of Pal. They are only found as Alpha Pals, and come with the “Legend” trait, which increases ATK and DEF by 20% and Movement Speed by 15%.

sections.heading: Subspecies Pals
sections.text: Some Pals can be created by breeding two specific species together. You can also breed pals the same species to reproduce them or pass down the passive traits to the offspring.

sections.heading: Palpedia
sections.text:
The Palpedia is an encyclopedia of all the Pals in Palworld. When you first encounter a Pal it is logged in your Palpedia. It will appear as a greyed-out image of the Pal. You will also be able to view their Habitat range. If you capture a pal it will show up in the Palpedia with full color and list possible drops, their partner skill, work traits, food level, and your capture bonus.
There are currently 187 Pals in Palworld.

sections.text: Palumba (Japanese: ココヤンバ Cocoyamba) is a Grass elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Palumba is a tall, avian pal that bears a striking resemblance to a palm tree. Its head, neck, and wing-like arms are covered in long, green leaves. Its head is topped with a leaf "crown" similar to the top of the aforementioned tree, surrounding a large, coral-orange flower. Its face has yellow, smug eyes with coral-orange lids, and a yellow, bird-of-prey beak twisted into an open smile. Its body is covered in brown, jagged bark, and ends in a small, green, leafy tail. Its legs are similarly bird-like, ending in talons that are tipped with bright white claws.

sections.text: • None so far.
Palworld is a single-player and multiplayer survival game with monster-taming elements made for Windows, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and Xbox One. It is developed and published by Pocketpair.

sections.heading: Description
sections.text:
Palworld is a brand-new, multiplayer, open-world survival crafting game where you can befriend and collect mysterious creatures called "Pal" in a vast world! Make your Pals fight, build, farm, and work in factories. In the first three days of its early access debut, Palworld sold 5 million copies. The game reached nearly 1.5 million concurrent players on Steam. 
• Survival
 ◦ This world is full of dangers such as food shortages, harsh weather, and illegal poachers. You must be prepared to do anything if you want to survive. You may even need to consume Pals sometimes...
• Riding and exploration
 ◦ Fly, swim, dig holes... you can explore all kinds of places on lands, the sea, and in the sky by riding Pals.
• Building
 ◦ Do you want to build a pyramid? You can have a large number of Pals work on the construction. Don't worry; labor laws won't be applied to Pals.
• Life
 ◦ Make your life more comfortable by collecting a variety of valuable Pals to light fires, generate power, and mine minerals.
• Farming
 ◦ Different Pals are good at different things such as sowing, watering, harvesting, or else. Create attractive farms and orchards with your Pals. There are also Pals that can duplicate themselves when they're planted.
• Production and Automation
 ◦ It is essential for automation to let Pals do the manual work. Build a workbench or assembly line for production and pals can be assigned to complete tasks based on their abilities and type. 
• Dungeon Exploration
 ◦ With Pals, you'll be safe in dangerous areas. If you are in danger, you use Pals to cover your escape! They'll give their lives to protect you.
• Breeding and Heredity
 ◦ When you breed Pals, they inherit the parents' characteristics. Mix rare species Pals to raise the strongest Pals!
• Poaching and Crime
 ◦ Endangered species Pals live in the hunting prohibited area. If you sneak in and catch them, you'll get tons of money! It's only illegal if you get caught.
• Multiplayer
 ◦ This game supports multiplayer. Invite your friends to go on an adventure together. Of course, you can battle and trade with other players!
from the Steam store page

sections.heading: Game support
sections.text: The Palworld Wiki is focused on creating the definitive guide to Palworld. But while we make an effort to be complete, which may include game bugs and fixes for them, we are not an official game support channel. Please direct support requests to the developer's Discord.

sections.heading: Reception
sections.text: When the game was first announced, fans are dubbing it "Pokemon with Guns".

sections.heading: Legal issues
sections.text: Pocketpair received a lawsuit from Nintendo and The Pokemon Company, for copying its patents. Pocketpair stated that Palworld is inspired by ARK: Survival Evolved, and not Pokemon.

sections.heading: Minimum
sections.text:
• OS: Windows 10 or later (64-Bit)
• Processor: i5-3570K 3.4 GHz 4 Core
• Memory: 16 GB RAM (Playable on 12GB)
• Graphics: GeForce GTX 1050 2GB
• DirectX: Version 11
• Network: Broadband Internet connection
• Storage: 40 GB available space, SSD required

sections.heading: References to Parbobox
sections.text:
When palbox is full, any caught pals give the notification that the ParboBox is full, the caught pal is dropped as a retrievable, and marked with the death box beam and an X on the map.
Parbo is worshipped as a deity, although information is scarce about the secretive cult that calls him their god.

sections.heading: Update
sections.text: At some point prior to v0.2.4.0 The title of Palbox has been fixed and now it correctly displays "Palbox is full" when no more spots are available.

Partner Skills are the innate abilities each species of pals can have.
They can be upgraded by using the Pal Essence Condenser.

sections.heading: Mount Speed upgrades
sections.text:
These are the only Pals that you can ride that gain a mount speed boost by upgrading their Partner Skills.
All mounts that do get a speed increase from upgrading their Partner Skill gain the exact same amount of speed.

sections.heading: Player Attack Boost While Mounted (+element type swap)
sections.text:
Increases the player's attack by X% and additionally changes the player's damage type to .
• Frostallion and Frostallion Noct both also swap the player's damage type to Ice and Dark (respectively), without an attack bonus.

sections.heading: Player Attack Boost + Element Swap for non-mounts
sections.text:
Similar to the above, the non-mount Pals that grant the player an type swap to their attacks also give an attack boost to the player's attacks as well when the Pal is active.
• Verdash grants an element swap to Grass but with a move speed bonus to itself and to the player (20|22|26|32|40%) instead of an attack increase.

sections.heading: Increased Drop-Rates for specific Elements
sections.text: The following Pals need to be active for the drop bonus to be active.

sections.heading: Mounted Element Boost
sections.text:
This bonus is ambiguously explained by the in-game description. This bonus applies to both the mounted Pal and the player. 
If the player has an attack that matches the element type bonus it will get boosted too. The important part though is that the Pal itself receives this bonus as well.
Meaning a mounted pal with this bonus will do 50% up to 100% more damage with their attacks that match the element bonus type. This makes using these Pals as combat mounts (with manual ability usage) especially powerful - better than letting them fight automatically.
Frostallion and Frostallion Noct both also swap the player's damage type to ice and dark (respectively), just without the attack increase that typically comes along with that. This means the player's attacks would fully benefit from the elemental damage boost of 50%-100%. This makes Frostallion good for both mounted player damage and mounted pal damage.

sections.heading: Party Buffs
sections.text:
These bonuses apply to any matching Pal (that matches the "Condition") in your party. The buffer only needs to be in your party to apply these bonuses to your active Pal. The bonuses stack additively if you have multiple buffers.
Notice Beegarde and Swee increase both attack and defense, the others only increase attack.
These bonuses stack additively with themself and with other sources of attack/defense bonuses. For example, if you were to make a party with 1 Sweepa and 4 Lv.5 Swee buffing it, each granting a +24% attack/defense bonus, you would have a total bonus of +96% attack and +96% defense to your Sweepa. This is a remarkably high stat bonus.
Making a full team of buffers combined with one strong active Pal is the way to make the absolute strongest single Pal you can. Combine that with the 'Mounted Element Boost' above (for an additional 100% damage increase) to deal the highest possible Pal damage. 4 Foxicle + 1 Frostallion (mounted) is probably the highest Pal damage possible outside of special moves like the Penguin launcher (but hey, you can combine the Penguin with 4 Kelpsea to make that even better too!).
Gobfin and Gobfin Ignis buff the player's attack and these do stack as well.

Passive Skills are attributes or natures that Pals can have. They can give various buffs/debuffs to the Player or Pal.
They are obtained randomly in the wild and can also be passed down through breeding. 
A Pal can have up to four Passive Skills at a time, but it is not possible to have multiple copies of the same Passive Skill on one Pal. However, multiple Passive Skills that carry boosts or downgrades to the same stat will stack additively with each other.
While most of the passive skills do exactly what they say they do, the ones affecting sale price have somewhat misleading descriptions: both price when selling and buying is affected, with Noble and Fine Furs affecting it favorably for the player (increasing the value when selling, and decreasing the value when purchasing), and Shabby affecting it unfavorably for the player (decreasing the value when selling, and increasing the value when purchasing).

sections.heading: Special Cases
sections.text: These are some special cases of Passive Skills you can only obtain by capturing specific Pals in the wild. They can however be bred down once captured, so they're not permanently exclusive for these Pals.

sections.heading: Lucky
sections.text: The Lucky Passive Skill can only be obtained by capturing a Lucky Pal.

sections.heading: Pal-Specific Passives
sections.text:
There are certain Pals which will always have a common Passive Skill after being captured.

• Hooligan - Gorirat, Gorirat Terra.
• Abnormal - Lovander.
• Glutton - Relaxaurus.
• Hard Skin - Digtoise, Warsect, Warsect Terra, Astegon.
• Flame Emperor - Blazamut.
• Spirit Emperor - Lyleen.
• Lord of Lightning - Orserk.
• Legend - Paladius, Necromus, Frostallion, Frostallion Noct, Jetragon.
• Otherwordly Cells - Xenovader, Xenogard, Xenolord.

sections.heading: Boss-Specific Passives
sections.text:
There are certain Boss Specific skills that can only be obtained by catching the Alpha Pals designed as bosses in the overworld, or Raid Bosses summoned at a Summoning Altar.
• Eternal Flame - Defeat the Raid Boss Blazamut Ryu, and hatch the egg received.
• Earth Emperor - Capture the Alpha Anubis.
• Lord of the Sea - Capture the Alpha Jormuntide.
• Celestial Emperor - Capture the Alpha Paladius.
• Lord of the Underworld - Capture the Alpha Necromus or Frostallion Noct.
• Ice Emperor - Capture the Alpha Frostallion.
• Divine Dragon - Capture the Alpha Jetragon.
• Siren of the Void - Defeat the Raid Boss Bellanoir Libero, and hatch the egg received.
• Invader - Defeat the Raid Boss Xenolord, and hatch the egg received.

Pengullet (Japanese: ぺンタマ Pentama) is a Water / Ice element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Pengullet resembles a small, round, blue penguin with a simple, smooth body shape. It has a red teardrop-shaped marking on its chest, adding a distinct feature to its otherwise plain appearance. Its eyes are minimalistic, featuring a black-to-blue gradient that gives the impression of tears welling up. A small tuft of feathers sits atop its head, which, according to its Paldeck entry, is implied to be the only feathers it has.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Pengullet can be found roaming near bodies of water. Normally, Pengullet are non-hostile towards the player, only retaliating when attacked.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Pengullet name is derived from the words penguin and bullet. The Japanese name for the Pal, Pentama, has a similar etymology, probably deriving from the English word Penguin and the Japanese word tama (bullet or shot).
 ◦ Pengullet's name may also be a reference to the American magician, Penn Jillette.
 ◦ Pengullet's name being a combination of 'Penguin' and 'Bullet' could be a reference to Pengullet's partner skill, where Pengullet is shot out of a rocket launcher.
• Pentama may be derived from penguin and 玉 tama (ball).
• Its Partner Skill having it being used as an explosive projectile may be a reference to the Prinnies of the Disgaea series.
• Pengullet was featured in Paldeck No.022 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Penking (Japanese: キャプペン Cappen) is a Water / Ice element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Penking resembles a large blue penguin with notable regal and pirate-like features. It wears a dark blue bicorn hat with blue trim and a long red tail extending from the back of it. Unlike a typical penguin, Penking has a fluffy, white-tipped tail and a duck-like bill. Around its neck is a white frill similar to that of Fuack, adding to its distinguished appearance. Penking's body is adorned with a dark blue overcoat featuring gold shoulder pads, giving it a royal and commanding look. On its stomach, there are three large red gems arranged vertically, with the central gem being the largest. These intricate details, combined with its pirate-inspired design, give Penking a unique and imposing presence.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Penking is found in the No. 1 Wildlife Sanctuary and as a Level 15 Alpha Pal in the Sealed Realm of the Frozen Wings. This Pal can become aggressive upon noticing the player's presence.

[[Image:Logo_Pocket_Pair_1.png|thumb|250px|link=]]
Pocketpair, Inc. is a Japanese video game developer based in Kyoto, Japan. Besides Palworld, they are best known for developing the popular open-world survival crafting game Craftopia.

sections.heading: Summary
sections.text:
• Founded: 2016
• CEO: Takuro Mizobe
• Notable games: Craftopia, Palworld, Overdungeon, Never Grave: The Witch and The Curse, AI: Art Impostor
• Website: Click Here
• Twitter: Click Here
• YouTube: Click Here

sections.heading: Popular games
sections.text:
Pocketpair, Inc. has quickly carved a niche in the gaming industry with their innovative and diverse portfolio. Here's a closer look at some of their most notable titles:
• Craftopia (2020): The crown jewel of Pocketpair's library, Craftopia took the world by storm with its charming visuals, addictive gameplay, and unique blend of sandbox, survival, and crafting elements. Players gather resources, build magnificent structures, battle fantastical creatures, and explore a vast, vibrant world brimming with possibilities. With over a million copies sold on Steam, Craftopia's success established Pocketpair as a developer to watch.
• Palworld (2024): This controversial title caused a stir upon release, drawing comparisons to Pokémon while injecting its own unique blend of monster taming, resource management, and survival mechanics. Players capture and train "Pals," adorable yet weaponized creatures they can use for combat, farming, and even transportation. With its dark humor and ethical themes, Palworld sparked heated discussions and divided opinions, but its innovative approach and engaging gameplay garnered attention and a dedicated fanbase.
• Overdungeon (2023): For roguelite enthusiasts, Overdungeon delivers pixel-art charm with strategic depth and intense action. Players delve into procedurally generated dungeons, battling enemies, acquiring loot, and unlocking new character builds for endless replayability. Overdungeon's fast-paced combat, diverse weapons, and unique mechanics like "dungeon crafting" offer a satisfying challenge for roguelite veterans and newcomers alike.
• Never Grave: The Witch and The Curse (2022): A departure from Pocketpair's usual offerings, Never Grave takes players on a hand-drawn, story-driven adventure as the witch "Nyne." Explore a fantastical world filled with puzzles, secrets, and platforming challenges, unraveling Nyne's past and confronting her inner demons. Never Grave's captivating narrative, beautiful visuals, and unique mechanics offer a distinct experience for those seeking a story-rich adventure.
• AI: Art Impostor (2023): This experimental title merges creativity and technology, challenging players to collaborate with an AI to paint masterpieces and discern genuine artwork from forgeries. With its unique concept, blend of artistic expression and strategic deduction, AI: Art Impostor pushes boundaries and offers a thought-provoking gameplay experience.

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.text: These are just a few of the notable games from Pocketpair's portfolio. Each title showcases their willingness to experiment and explore new genres, mechanics, and themes. With their innovative approach, commitment to quality, and focus on employee well-being, Pocketpair is a studio to keep an eye on for exciting and groundbreaking games in the future.

Poison Blast is a Dark-Element Active Skills that can be learned by different Pals.

sections.heading: Learnable By
sections.text:
• Daedream: Available at Level 7
• Vaelet: Available at Level 35

sections.heading: Skill Detail
sections.text:
• Element: Dark
• Cost: 30 Power
• Effect: Hurls poisonous sludge at an enemy, inflicting Poison status.

sections.heading: Poison Status
sections.text:
• Deals damage over time (DoT) for several turns.
• Can stack with other sources of Poison for increased DoT.
• Some enemies may be immune or resistant to Poison.

sections.heading: Using Poison Blast Effectively
sections.text:
• Against Poison-susceptible enemies: Can be a strong DoT source, chipping away at their health steadily.
• For additional damage: Useful when combined with other attacks, allowing you to stack damage types.
• For strategic control: Can help weaken enemies before making a final attack or capturing them.

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.text:
• Requires the Pal to be close to the enemy to use.
• May not be effective against all enemies, especially those resistant to Poison.
• Can be countered by certain enemy skills or items that remove status effects.

sections.heading: Overall
sections.text: Poison Blast is a versatile skill that can be useful in various situations, especially against enemies vulnerable to Poison. However, consider its limitations and strategize accordingly to maximize its effectiveness.

sections.heading: Addition Information
sections.text:
• You can obtain the Dark Skill Fruit (teaches Poison Blast) from certain merchants or trees in the Palworld world.
• The skill's effectiveness can be enhanced by certain items or abilities that boost Dark-element attacks or DoT damage.
•

Predator Pals are Pals that you cannot capture with Pal Spheres. They spawn randomly in specific locations throughout the Palpagos Islands.
Defeating a predator pal is the only way to obtain Predator Cores.
Typically, predator pals are stronger than their normal counterparts. They are visibly discernable by a red glowing aura surrounding the Pal.
Prior to the Tides of Terraria Update, defeating this pal was the only way to obtain Giant Pal Soul. Since then, the devs have added other ways to obtain giant pal souls.

Prixter (Japanese: ブッサソリ Bussasori) is a Dark and Ground Elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Prixter is a tall, bipedal, scorpion-like Pal. It appears to wear mauve coloured armour on its head, the armour concealing most of its face. The middle part points downwards, with two parts on the sides that point downwards as well. The armour has a outline of a seed shape on it, this outline a lighter mauve colour. The upper half of this Pal's head is a black colour, with the lower half, presumably the lower jaw, is a white colour. It has narrowed yellow eyes, with black slitted pupils. Right underneath its head is more armour, black and lined with the dark mauve colour the head piece is. It has a thin and long torso, the torso black in colour with two stripes on it. Its shoulders are made of two large mauve segments, with spikes that curve inwards atop of them. The arms themselves are each made of three mauve coloured circular segments, before ending with claws. These claws have a mauve coloured top with the seed like outline shape on the sides of them. The end of these claws have a spike that point upwards. The bottom half of these claws is black. It has a long, seven segment tail that ends with a large, potent stinger. Each tail segment is mauve in colour, with the seed-shaped outline on each one. The tail is notably larger than the tail segments, but is still circular shaped. The tail has the seed-shaped outline on it as well. The tail ends with a grey stinger that points upwards. Its legs are sharp and pointed like knifes, mauve in colour and tipped with a s silver colour.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal will attack the player character upon sight, regardless of what has happened so far.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Missing Information.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Prixter's design may pull from the Aqrabuamelu (Better known as a scorpion man).
 ◦ It is called Scorpion Man internally further proving this fact.

Palworld has a number of ways to craft gear at your bases via a variety of workbenches. 
While work can be completed at workbenches and assembly lines by both the player and handiwork capable pals, other production sites (like furnaces) can only be handled by pals and only if they meet the requirements for it (such as kindling being required for furnaces). A pal on one's party can also be assigned to do the work while at one's base. 
Pal crafting speed is determined by their Handiwork level-or the level of skill associated with the specific type of crafting. Player work speed can be upgraded in player stats. 
Note that in build mode, this category does not include sphere assembly, which is considered a pal-based structure. However, these can also be crafted by the player and/or pals.

images.caption: Pals assembling automatic rifles in a factory

Quivern (Japanese: フェスキー Fesukii) is a Dragon element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Quivern is a big, fluffy, dragon-shaped Pal. It has white fur all over its body. The wings are angel-like, with blue-toned feathers. Two feathers resembling antennae rise from the top of its head.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Alpha Quivern has an aggressive temperament, attacking players on sight. 
Wild Quivern sometimes spawn on No.2 Wildlife Sanctuary, and can also be found around central Sakurajima. They are curious of the player, peacefully approaching for a moment if spotted before returning to idle. They are passive, only resorting to violence after being attacked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text:
• No. 2 Wildlife Sanctuary 
• Sakurajima

sections.heading: Dungeons
sections.text: • Can be found as a level 23 alpha Quivern at the Sealed Realm Of The Winged Tyrant fast travel waypoint.

• Transporting 3
• Gathering 2
• Mining 2

Possible Alpha Drops:

• High Quality Pal Oil - 100%
• Precious Plume - (2-4) 100%
• Ring of Dragon Resistance +1 - 3%

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This Pal was named by Discord user @smackerooon as a part of the Pal Naming thread on the official Pocketpair Discord.
• Despite it having four legs, its name is based on the word Wyvern, which usually is used for dragons with two wings and two legs.
• Sailor Quivern was one of the three skins obtained from Palworld Twitch Drops. The other two are Golden Relaxaurus and Summer Chillet.
• Quivern was featured in Paldeck No.055 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

sections.text: Quivern Botan (Japanese: フォレスキー Foresukii) is an Dragon and Grass elemental Pal. It is a subspecies of Quivern.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Quivern Botan is a big, fluffy, dragon-shaped Pal. Other than a white abdomen, it has green fur all over its body. The wings are angel-like, with green-toned feathers. Two feathers resembling antennae rise from the top of its head.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Quivern Botan are found in grassy areas around central Sakurajima and are curious of the player, peacefully approaching for a moment if spotted before returning to idle. They are pacific, only resorting to violence after being attacked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Sakurajima

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:

Work Suitability:
• Handiwork 1
• Transporting 3
• Gathering 2
• Mining 2
• Planting 2

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• First revealed in a trailer for Pal Arena.
• Quivern Botan was featured in Paldeck No.095 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Quivern Saddle is a key item in Palworld.

Radar Sphere is an unreleased item in Palworld.
Its unique ability is that it is able to catch other players' Pals, hinting at a future PvP update. It appears to have the same capture power as the Giga Sphere.
Ragnahawk (Japanese: イグニクス Ignix) is a Fire element pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Ragnahawk is an orange feathered hawk with black and yellow markings on its wings, wears a black mask with yellow markings and orange ponytail on the back of its head.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Ragnahawk will tend to be aggressive towards the player upon sight, attacking unprovoked.

sections.heading: Utility

• Can be ridden as a flying mount. (Requires Ragnahawk Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 37)
• Applies Fire damage to the player's attacks while mounted.
Work Suitability:
• Kindling 3
• Transporting 3
Possible Drops:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from the words Ragnarök and hawk.
• Its Japanese name may come from Ignis and Phoenix. 
• Ragnahawk was featured in Paldeck No.074 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Ragnahawk Saddle is a key item in Palworld. 
It is a tier 37 Technology and unlocks Ragnahawk's Partner Skill.

Raid Bosses are powerful pals that can be summoned by using a Summoning Altar with the corresponding slab. Like overworld bosses, raid bosses have higher stats than normal, but unlike them, raid bosses cannot be caught.
Raid bosses will drop an Egg upon defeat that will hatch into a non-boss version of the pal.

Ranch is a structure and mechanic in Palworld.
This building allows multiple Pals in at once to graze and relax. Alongside allowing the Pals to relax, they also drop items on the floor of the ranch to be collected.
It's a tier 5 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Ranch building works automatically once any compatible Pals are deployed at Base. Once a Pal walks into the ranch and starts performing labor, they will have the status "Grazing..." until they leave the Ranch. Each Ranch may house up to 4 Pals.

sections.heading: Strategies
sections.text: Sanity does not deplete when at the ranch, so you don't need any sanity-based passive skills. Work speed passive skills do not affect how soon nor how fast they "produce" their drops. The only thing it affects is how fast their "drop found" animation goes, which save minuscule to no time at all.

sections.heading: Best Passive Skills for Ranch Pals
sections.text: • You do not need movement speed if you put a feed box close to the ranch. You could still have movement speed if this pal will be used for any combat while at base, so it can swiftly fight and then come back to the ranch. However, if production speed matters more, then it would be best to prevent combat using the Monitoring Stand.

sections.heading: Drops
sections.text: Different Pals drop different things while grazing the ranch. Note that as of v0.1.4.1, there are no pals with a farming level over 1 without the use of the Pal Essence Condenser.

• Lvl 4: 1-4 High Quality Pal Oil or 10-40 Gold Coins

Rayhound (Japanese: イヌズマ Inuzuma) is a Electric element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Rayhound is a tall, canine like Pal. It has a thin build and long snout, its nose pitch black and shaped like an upside down triangle. It has narrowed eyes, its eyes a sunny orange-yellow colour. It has pointed ears that point upwards, the ears golden and the inside of the ears being a mud brown colour. The inside shape of the ears resemble a squished, jagged, upside down heart. Around its eyes are mud-gold spots, with golden spikes sticking out from its cheeks. Flowing from the back of its head is long, flowing mud brown fur. The rest of its body is mud brown in colour, with a white chest. Its chest has two bits of fur that points upwards. It has thin legs, with golden tufts near the bottom of its paws that points up. On its front legs, it has long, lightning-like spikes that point upwards diagonally. Across the sides of its body, it has golden lightning bolt stripes. On its rear, it has three mud brown fur bits that stick up. Its tail is incredibly thin, and ends with a large, three pointed bolt.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal will attack the player on sight.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Jumping Force - Can be ridden. Can double jump while mounted. (Requires Rayhound Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 26)
Work Suitability:
• Generating Electricity 2
Possible Drops:
• Electric Organ

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from lightning ray and hound.
• Its Japanese name may come from 犬 inu (dog) and 稲妻 inazuma (lightning).
• Rayhound was featured in Paldeck No.052 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Rayhound Saddle is a key item in Palworld.

The Rayne Syndicate is a criminal organization notorious for its involvement in illegal Pal poaching and exploitation. The determined and ruthless Zoe Rayne leads the group with her Electric-elemental Pal, Grizzbolt. They operate from their heavily fortified Rayne Syndicate Tower, located west of the central area of the map.

sections.heading: Members
sections.text:
The Rayne Syndicate members can be found scattered throughout Palpagos Islands. They can be found at Syndicate camps and dungeons as well as the Syndicate Oil Rig.
• Zoe Rayne
 ◦ The current leader of the Rayne Syndicate. Her tower is located in the starting region, Windswept Hills.
• Syndicate Thug
 ◦ Armed with a wooden club or a handgun. Can spawn during a raid and can be found out in the open or inside dungeons.
• Syndicate Grenadier
 ◦ Armed with grenades. Can be found out in the open, inside dungeons, at the Syndicate Oil Rig, and can spawn during a raid.
• Syndicate Cleaner
 ◦ Armed with flamethrower. His range on the flamethrower is formidable and can be quite difficult to deal with. Can be found out in the open, at the Syndicate Oil Rig, and can spawn during a raid.
• Syndicate Gunner
 ◦ Armed with assault rifle. Can be found out in the open, inside dungeons, at the Syndicate Oil Rig, and can spawn during a raid.
• Syndicate Hunter
 ◦ Armed with Pump-action Shotgun. Can be found out in the open, inside dungeons, at the Syndicate Oil Rig, and can spawn during a raid.
• Syndicate Crusher
 ◦ Armed with Minigun. Can cause a lot of damage to the player. He also has a sweeping spin attack with his minigun if the player gets up close to him. Can be found out in the open, at Syndicate Oil Rig and can spawn during raids .
• Syndicate Elite
 ◦ Armed with Rocket Launcher. The lock on the rocket launcher makes the Syndicate Elite spot the player easily. The player has to be careful as rocket launchers can stun and knock out Pals, which can knock the player flying Pal out of the sky while they are mounted. Can be found in high level areas such as the Dessicated Desert and the Syndicate Oil Rig.
• Syndicate Interceptor 
 ◦ Armed with Missile Launcher. Can be found in high level areas as well as at the Syndicate Oil Rig.
• Syndicate Veteran
 ◦ Armed with Grenade Launcher. Can be found in high level areas as well as at the Syndicate Oil Rig.
• Syndicate Laser Rifleman
• Syndicate Crossbowman
• Syndicate Swordsman

sections.heading: Drops
sections.text:
The Rayne Syndicate can drop different items depending on the level and the spawn location. Such as if the player is being raided, they can drop training manuals. They can drop almost any type of ammo, the most common being Coarse Ammo. The Syndicate members at the Syndicate Oil Rig can drop Crude Oil.
Since the Rayne Syndicate are neutral element damage. It's advised to bring a long a Katress or a Blazehowl Noct to have the Partner Skills effect active to get double the loot.

Reindrix (Japanese: ツララジカ Tsurarajika) is an Ice element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Reindrix is a large, majestic Pal that stands proudly. On the top of its head, is has tall ice-like antlers that curve inwards, with two spikes that curl in the opposite direction. In the middle of these antlers, are two curled spikes that curl inwards. Sticking out from the bottom of the antlers, are two wavy horns that stick out to the sides. There are singular curled horns near the end of these middle antlers. Underneath the bottom antlers are two ice-like parts that slightly curl upwards. On the top of Reindrix's head is a majestic ice-like plate that has three spikes that point upwards. This ice-like plate resembles a fork. Growing out from under this plate are two long tufts of fur that stop at the shoulders. These long fur parts are a light baby blue colour with a dark blue pattern on them. The tufts end with a sea blue colour, having a sea blue zigzag that sits above it. Above the zigzag are sea blue triangles. Reindrix has a narrowed head, with deep red eyes and a muted blue nose. The top half of its head is a sea foam green-blue colour. The bottom of its head and its neck are a snow white colour. Around the bottom of its neck is a collar of long, fluffy fur. This fur is a light baby blue colour that ends with a dark blue colour. It has loose fur growing on its back that drapes down. This loose fur is a light baby blue colour with the same pattern as the loose fur growing on its head. It has thin light blue legs, with sharp and jagged ice growing around its hooves. Its hooves are a sky blue colour. It has a rather long tail, being light blue in colour and tipped with a dark blue.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Reindrix are an aggressive Pal and will attack the player, regardless of what has happened so far.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Cool Body - Can be ridden. Keeps the rider cool in hot environments. (Requires Reindrix Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 29)
Work Suitability:
• Lumbering 2
• Cooling 2
Possible Drops:
• Reindrix Venison
• Leather
• Horn
• Ice Organ

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name may come from Reindeer and the suffix -rix.
• Its Japanese name may come from 氷柱 tsurara (icicle) and 鹿 shika (deer).
• Reindrix was featured in Paldeck No.069 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Reindrix Saddle is a key item in Palworld.
