Relaxaurus (Japanese: ペコドン Pekodon) is a Dragon & Water element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Relaxaurus is a large saurian pal resembling a long-necked dinosaur. Its body is rotund and blue with a cream underside. Relaxaurus always is seen displaying a vacant expression having simple dots as eyes and a toothless mouth ending with a short beak-like snout. Its arms and legs appear short and stubby while its neck and curled tail are long in comparison. The backside of Relaxaurus is full of darker blue pill-shaped markings running along from its neck to its tail.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Relaxaurus is an aggressive Pal, and will attempt to attack the player when they are nearby. They usually spawn alone or in pairs.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Hungry Missile
• Can be ridden.
• Can rapidly fire a missile launcher while mounted. (Requires Relaxaurus Missile Launcher→ Unlocks at Lv. 44)
Work Suitability:
• Watering 2
• Transporting 1
Possible Drops:
• High Quality Pal Oil
• Ruby

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Relaxaurus appears to serve as the mascot for Palworld being used as as the icon for Palworld's official accounts.
• Its Japanese name may come from ペコペコ pekopeko (onomatopoeic word for hunger) and the suffix -odon which is used in the scientific name of multiple large animals (especially associated with non-avian dinosaurs like the ones Relaxaurus is probably based on).
• All wild and hatched Relaxaurus has the Glutton Passive Skill.
• Despite its english name contains the word "Relax", it is, ironically, anything but.
• Golden Relaxaurus was one of the three skins obtained from Palworld Twitch Drops. The other two are Sailor Quivern and Summer Chillet.
• Relaxaurus was featured in Paldeck No.003 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

sections.heading: Gameplay Notes
sections.text:
• Despite not being mentioned in the description "Hungry Missile", Relaxaurus moves very quickly and without consuming stamina while submerged in water.
• The missiles of "Hungry Missile" have a noticeable homing behavior when fired, able to turn at least 90 degrees from the original firing direction if there are targets present.

images.file: Palworld Twitch main Chillet Quivern Relaxaurus.png

images.caption: thumb|A Relaxaurus transporting a piece of Stone.

sections.text: Relaxaurus Lux (Japanese: パリピドン Paripidon) is the Electric element variation of Relaxaurus.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Relaxaurus Lux has an appearance that is largely similar to its regular counterpart with some differences. Its body is a bright yellow instead of blue. Its back patterns are black instead of blue. Most notable difference that Relaxaurus Lux has from a regular Relaxaurus is that it sports a large plasma crest on its head resembling a mohawk.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal doesn't have an established habitat in the wild, as such it is only encountered through certain situations:
• Breeding and hatching eggs.
• During a Pal raid on a player base of sufficiently high level.
• When encountering a swarm.
• As an Alpha Pal in dungeons.

sections.heading: Utility

• Generating Electricity 3
Possible Drops:
• High Quality Pal Oil
• Electric Organ
• Sapphire

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its Japanese name may come from パリピ paripi (Wasei-eigo for "party people") and ペコドン Pekodon (Relaxaurus's Japanese name).
• Relaxaurus Lux was featured in Paldeck No.086 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: A Relaxaurus Lux hatching from an egg in what seems like an early version of the breeding system, from the 2022 Palworld trailer.

Ribbuny (Japanese: ヒメウサ Himeusa) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Ribbuny is a small, bipedal rabbit Pal with short, stubby leg and arm paws. It has a dark magenta bow on the top of its head, long pink rabbit ears sticking upwards. It has a darker pink colour on the inside of its ears. It is mostly light pink in colour, with a pale pink heart shaped mask on its face. It has oval shaped violet eyes, a small black nose and a sweet smile on its face. Its torso is a pale pink colour, while its paws and tail are a darker shade of pink. It has a long, flowy tail that is rather cloud like flowing behind it. On its neck is a small light pink bow, with two ribbons flowing behind it. The ribbons are pale pink in colour, and are tipped with a darker pink colour. These ribbons end with more of a heart shape.

sections.heading: Behavior

Ribbuny Botan (Japanese: ヨモギウサ Yomogiusa) is a Grass elemental Pal and a sub-species of Ribbuny.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Ribbunny Botan is a small, bipedal rabbit Pal with short, stubby leg and arm paws. It has a yellow bow on the top of its head, long gren rabbit ears sticking upwards. It has a yellow colour on the inside of its ears. It is mostly moss green in colour, with a white heart shaped mask on its face. It has oval shaped magenta eyes, a small black nose and a sweet smile on its face. Its torso is a white colour, while its paws and tail are a moss green colour. It has a long, flowy tail that is rather cloud like flowing behind it. On its neck is a small light white bow, with two ribbons flowing behind it. The ribbons are white in colour, and are tipped with a yellow colour. These ribbons end with more of a heart shape.

Ring of Mercy is an accessory in Palworld that prevents knocking down a Pal during combat, allowing an easier capture.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It is a tier 19 ancient technology item and requires 2 points to unlock. It can also be bought for $30,000 from some merchants.

sections.heading: Use
sections.text: Used to catch pals(and maybe humans) easier. While fighting an enemy wearing The Ring Of Mercy, that enemy's health will not drop below 1 unless an unmounted pal fights the enemy, a third party kills it, or it dies to status effects, such as burn and poison. It is recommended to use a mount or gun/bow with no status chance, such as fire element pals or dark element skills with poison chance. Do not use poison or fire weapons.

Robinquill (Japanese: フェザーロ Featherro) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Robinquill is a human-like Pal with a slender black body, and appears to be wearing a crested helmet and floor-length trousers, both of which are a bright spring or aquamarine green in colour. The trousers turn into a light beige as they reach the feet, and the round helmet bears a row of spikes running down the vertical middle of the head, which are further decorated with beige and orange bird feathers. It wears a collared, four-pointed, bright orange cape around its neck and shoulders; this cape is also divided down the middle.
It is similar to humans in height and stature, at about 175-180 cm (not counting the head crest). The orange eyes are the only facial feature visible on its model. Each hand ends in a thumb and two fingers.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal is usually found patrolling forested areas. Players intruding on its territory will quickly find that this guy isn't much for warm welcomes, as it is highly territorial, and will be quick to challenge said invaders with their bows that they pull out of hammerspace the moment it spots someone.
They are not great at close-range combat, and prefer sniping from afar.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Hawkeye - While fighting together, allows you to deal more damage to weak points.
Work Suitability:
• Planting 1
• Handiwork 2
• Lumbering 1
• Medicine Production 1
• Transporting 2
• Gathering 2
Possible Drops:
• Wheat Seeds
• Arrow

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• The name may come from the word quill and the character Robin Hood.
• Its Japanese name may come from Feather and Arrow.
• Unlike Bushi's sword, which is visibly stowed at its side when not in use, there is no indication of where Robinquill stashes its bow when it is not using it.
• Robinquill was featured in Paldeck No.020 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: Robinquill shooting their bow at a player.

A row of long white feathers with a red tip crown the top of its head. Notably, it boasts a wide red cape draped across its back.

Rolling is a mechanic in Palworld that lets players dodge attacks.
Rolling can be used to interrupt other actions: trying to roll while grappled will cause the grappling gun to unhook; rolling while crouched will cause the player to roll and then stop crouching.
It's not possible to roll while sliding[verify] or gliding.

Rooby (Japanese: ヒノコジカ Hinokojika) is a Fire element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Rooby resembles a small red deer with a plume of fire on its forehead that burns brightly. Its body is covered in sleek, cream-colored fur on the legs, underbelly, and face, while the rest of its body is a vibrant red. The fur around the front of its neck is shaped like a bowtie, giving it a distinct and elegant appearance. Rooby's ears are large and pointed, standing upright, and its bright blue eyes are sharp and expressive. Its most prominent feature is its very large, bushy tail, which is about the length of its body and has a smooth, rounded shape, adding to its striking silhouette. Its legs are slender but sturdy, with red markings just above its hooves, complementing its fiery theme.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wild Rooby will be passive towards the player and may flee. Like most other Pals, it will become aggressive when attacked.

It has long dark brown legs that feature black hooves. Its grouchy looking eyes are a blue color. Rushoar's back has various tan markings and a dark brown row of spikes on its head.

Sakurajima is a region in Palworld. It's an island located on the top left of the Palpagos Islands that's most well-known for its sprawling cherry blossom forest and Japanese-style architecture, although it has a few other distinct sections as well. These include a barren rocky field on the northern side of the island, a huge sandbar on the south-western side, and a swamp with giant mushrooms growing everywhere on the south-eastern side.
The Moonflower Clan faction is located here.

sections.heading: Available Boss Pals
sections.text: The pals found here can be level 45 and up.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The Japanese phrase Sakurajima literally translates to Cherry Blossom Island in English.

Sanity is a mechanic in Palworld to tell the player about the status of their Pals.

sections.heading: About
sections.text: Every captured Pal has a sanity level (SAN). This can be found in the Party menu, the , or next to a Pal's health bar when looking at them.

sections.heading: Causes
sections.text:
Multiple things can cause a Pal's sanity level to drop:
• When Pals are forced to work (by a , for example)
• When Pals are hungry (or starving)
• When Pals have no place to sleep

sections.heading: Low Sanity
sections.text: When a pal has low sanity, they will dislike doing work in your base. This is displayed as slacking off, or sleeping in the middle of the day. When the sanity level is low for longer amounts of time, it can cause the Pal to get the "Depression" debuff.

sections.heading: Raising Sanity
sections.text:
When Pals are satisfied with their working conditions, they will slowly regain sanity. 
Good working conditions include: 
• The Pal has access to enough food (Food that increases sanity will help manage their sanity levels better!)
• The Pal has a place to sleep 
• The Pal is not worked too hard. 
Having a or in your base can also let the Pal regain sanity quicker. (So long as they have access to it)
Pals with a Farming skill can regain sanity if they are grazing in the Ranch.
You can also manually raise a Pal's sanity with food using the Command menu.

sections.heading: Acquisition

Saya Kurosaki is the leader of The Moonflowers and owner of Selyne. Her tower is located in Sakurajima.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Saya is a fair skinned woman with purple eyes and has a beauty mark beneath the left side of her lip. Her hair is dark blue with light blue streaks and styled in a half topknot. On the right side, she has two flowers in her hair, while on the left, she has a golden crescent moon hairpin with black swirl patterns on it and pink kumihimo cords dangling from it. She wears a crescent moon earring on her right ear and a hagoromo floats around her head. Her fingernails are dyed purple.
She wears a black Kimono with long sleeves that have golden edges and patterns on them. The obi is purple with golden metal around it and from the back, it has a golden decorative piece with a sakura flower and pink kumihimo cords. Under the kimono, she is wearing black halter top with golden moon accessory on it. There is a waist chain under the obi and she wears long stockings.

Schematics are blueprints in Palworld that allow the player to craft upgraded items while in one's inventory. Schematics offer the ability to craft enhanced versions of gear, or entirely new items.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: Schematics can be found inside of dungeons, bought from the Wandering Merchant, drops from locked chests, or drops from bosses. Certain ones are dropped by specific bosses.

sections.heading: Dungeon Chest Loot
sections.text: • Note: Grass Dungeon refers to Hillside Cavern, Isolated Island Cavern and Ravine Grotto.

rows.t: Cold Resistant Pal Metal Armour Schematic 1
Cold Resistant Pal Metal Armour Schematic 2

sections.heading: Boss-specific Drops
sections.text: This is an incomplete list of specific schematics and where to find them. It is not guaranteed that you will obtain Boss Schematics first try as they have 3% drop rate, you will likely need to grind these bosses.

rows.t: Heat Resistant Pal Metal Armor Schematic 4
Cold Resistant Pal Metal Armor Schematic 4

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• Schematics can be used to unlock items before the player normally is able to. 
 ◦ An example of this is obtaining the Heat Resistant Pal Metal Armor 1 Schematic before having the required level to research the normal Heat Resistant Pal Metal Armor .
• A great place to farm Schematics is Icy Weasel Hill, due to the abundance of locked chests and high-level dungeons. 
 ◦ In the west of the Icy Weasel Hill fast-travel point, there will nearly always be a level 45 dungeon, which can contain end-game Legendary Schematics.

Sea Breeze Archipelago is a region in Palworld.
Located to the southwest of Windswept Hills, the region consists of a rocky beach island that's home to aquatic Pals that live along the shore. Further inland from the shore, Sea Breeze Archipelago contains plenty of flat plains and small hills, some of which contain light forestation.

Selyne (Japanese: セレムーン Selemoon) is a Dark and Neutral elemental Pal. It was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Selyne is a tall, humanoid Pal resembling the moon. On the top of Selyne's head, it has a golden crescent moon shape. It has no visible mouth or nose, the only visible thing on its face being its crimson eyes. It looks to wear a silver headgear that covers majority of its head, its face being a darker shade of grey. On its chest, it has a golden button with two golden ribbons that extend downwards. On its chest, it looks to have a darker grey line that stops at the waist. It has silver spikes that replace its shoulders, and long, thin arms. It has three fingered hands on both arms. The Pal looks to be wearing a silver dress that ends seperate spiked ends. Underneath the silver dress is an extension, having a longer, darker grey section underneath it. At the end of each spike on the dress sits a diamond-shaped gold spike, with golden circles in between the diamond-shapes. Behind Selyne is a large crescent moon that points to the left. This moon has a sky blue tip, with a gradient of purple and pink everywhere else on the moon. Small white dots are scattered on the moon, like stars. Attached to the side of the moon, there are two diamond shaped golden pieces with gold dots in between the diamond shapes.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Selyne is aggressive towards the player and will attack on sight.

sections.heading: Meteorite Event
sections.text: • When the meteorite event spawns in Sakurajima there is a chance for Selyne to spawn with it.

sections.text:
Partner Skill:

Enhances Neutral and Dark attacks while mounted.

• Transporting 3
Possible Drops: 
• Normal
 ◦ Cloth
 ◦ Sapphire
 ◦ Precious Claw

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Her english name is a alternative spelling of Selene. Meaning "Moon" in Greek. Its also the name of The Greek Goddess Of The Moon.
• No other Pal has the same combination of elements as Selyne. Additionally, it is the only Neutral-elemental Pal that has another element.
• Selyne was featured in Paldeck No.0100 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: Selyne sits on its own moon while working at a crafting bench.
The player sits on its moon while riding it.

The Semi-Auto Rifle is obtained at Tier 41 Technology and costs 3 Points to unlock 
The Semi-Auto Rifle fires Rifle Ammo.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Semi-Auto Rifle Is like the Single-Shot Rifle but with a 8 round capacity and a slightly higher Damage. It has the same durability as the Single-Shot Rifle and similar range and reload speed.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The Semi-Auto Rifle resembles the M1 Garand. The M1 Garand was the standard service rifle throughout all of World War 2 and the Korean War.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: The Semi-Auto Shotgun is available to unlock at level 47, and you are able to get it through crafting at a Weapon Assembly Line. There are also 4 schematics available that you can find throughout the world that offer different rarities of the weapon.

Settlements are small towns or villages in Palworld, which function as an area to interact with passive NPCs. 
Currently, the main purpose of these settlements is to trade with merchants such as the Wandering Merchant and the Pal Merchant, as most of them are either abandoned or underdeveloped. Alternatively, some NPCs may also give advice on where to find nearby points of interest (such as the location of Lifmunk Effigies or Alpha Pals). Lastly, some NPCs may challenge you to battle with their own Pals, or provide you with small side quests (these functions are currently not in the game, but are planned to be included at some point in the future).

Shroomer (Japanese: キノッポ Kinoppo) is a Grass Elemental Pal. It was first shown at the ID@Xbox Showcase.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Shroomer is a bipedal green dinosaur pal wearing a spotty mushroom cap on its head.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Shroomer is passive and will flee upon seeing the player, but will retaliate if attacked. It may suddenly stop attacking to stare at the player, which is an accurate portrayal of its Paldeck entry.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Shroomer spawns in the northwest region of The Palpagos Islands, in an area known as the Mushroom Wetlands (Sakurajima,) during the day. You will need a flying mount to reach the island, and it’s not a recommended area for low-level players.

Work Suitability:

• Handiwork 1
• Lumbering 2
• Gathering 2
Possible Drops:
• Mushroom
• Red Berries

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Shroomer's name is a portmanteau of shroom and boomer (meaning large or notable of its kind). Shroomer as a slang means "A person who consumes hallucinogenic mushrooms for their effects".
• Shroomer was featured in Paldeck No.092 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

images.caption: Its first showcase with three other Pals.

sections.text: Shroomer Noct (Japanese: べノッポ Venoppo) is a Grass and Dark Elemental Pal. It is a Sub-Species of Shroomer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Shroomer Noct is a tall, bipedal mushroom-like Pal. On the top of its head, it has a wide brimmed dark purple mushroom with beige spots. Underneath, the mushroom is the same beige colour as the spots. It has a tall neck, small head and small body. Its eyes are small black circles with small white dots on the inside. Its body is a soft lavender colour, with a beige underbelly that goes from the bottom jaw to the stomach. It has small, oval shaped arms and legs. It has a short tail, the tail being the same dark purple mushroom with beige underneath. However, the tail mushroom doesn't appear to have spots.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is passive and will not attack unless attacked first.

Sibelyx (Japanese: シルキーナ Silkyna) is an Ice element pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Sibelyx resembles a humanoid white moth with wing-like upper limbs that resemble sleeves and end in a serrated border. Its body is hourglass-shaped and reminiscent of a dress. A white ruff covers its neck and crowning its head there's a wide-brim hat with a pair of feathery antennae on top. Striped markings are found all over its body except for its face and ruff.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal can be found as an Alpha Pal in the Sealed Realm of the Pristine and may start attacking upon noticing the player. Non-alphas are passive and will run away unless the player attacks.

The Single-Shot Rifle is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 36 for 3 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Like the Musket, the Single-Shot Rifle is a powerful weapon with good range and high damage per shot, though it can only fire once before needing to be reloaded. However, it has much higher durability than the Musket, allowing it to be used for much longer without needing to be repaired. Additionally, the Single-Shot Rifle's reload time is far shorter than the Musket's, making it a good choice for both close-range and long-range combat.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• The Single-Shot Rifle's legendary-tier Schematic can be obtained from the Alpha Verdash boss.
• Though it fires much faster than the Musket, it is still recommended to bring a backup weapon in the event you are unable to reload your Single-Shot Rifle.
• Maximize damage by aiming for critical hits on your targets.
• The Single-Shot Rifle's reload time can leave you vulnerable, so use cover to your advantage.
• Upgrade crafting skills will allow you to craft higher-quality ammo for increased damage.
• The Single-Shot Rifle fires Rifle Ammo.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The Single-Shot Rifle appears to be based on the Spencer Carbine, which was used by Union Cavalry forces during the American Civil War. However, the Spencer Carbine was capable of firing up to 7 rounds in one full magazine. Rather, the gun seems to function similar to the Sharps Carbine, another Civil War-era cavalry carbine, that so happens to bear a striking resemblance to the Spencer.

Skill Fruits are fruits you can obtain from strange looking trees that grow all over Palworld. They can be used to teach various Skills to your pals.
Skill Fruits do not have to be given to Pal's with the same typing, so a fire Pal can learn a water move or vice versa. 
As of Patch v0.1.3.0, giving a Human a skill will cause its AI to not work and it will not attack as long as the skill is equipped. 
There are currently 71 unique skill fruits in the game.

images.caption: A Skill Fruit tree in the wild.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The designs of the Skill Fruit and their ability to give powers to Pals could be a reference to the Devil Fruits from the manga and anime One Piece.

sections.heading: Active Skills
sections.text:
Active Skills are attacks Pals use in battle. Each pal can have up to 3 active skills at a time. Skills are unlocked as the Pal levels up. Skills can also be learned by feeding Pals Skill Fruit found hanging from trees in the world. There is no limit to how many Active Skills a Pal may learn, except that they may only learn each one once.
Each Pal can have up to 3 Active Skills enabled at a time. You can swap a Pal's learned Active Skills by viewing them via the Party menu or the Palbox.
When riding a Pal, a player can use the Pal's Active Skills manually.
Each skill has a cooldown time (CT) before it may be used again.

Sleeping is a mechanic in Palworld to skip the night or regain health.

sections.heading: Player
sections.text: When the player sleeps, their health will quickly regenerate, even when starving. Additionally, if it's night time, sleeping will skip the night and wake up a bit after the start of the new day.

sections.heading: Pals
sections.text:
A lot of Pals sleep at night. There are some exceptions, which are often Pals of the Dark element. These Pals will continue working at the base throughout the night, and won't require a bed to accommodate them. However, do note that this means they won't get any of the benefits of sleeping, as well as having a higher chance of their sanity dropping if left unchecked.
Sleeping will help restore a Pal's health. Pals with low health may sleep to heal. As a result, pals that are starving (and low on health) will sleep too. Starving Pals won't lose health while sleeping. Pals at the base will wake up if there is a raid.
Sleeping Pals are easier to catch than when they are awake. You can get an additional "Back bonus" if you aim at them from behind. Sleeping pals will wake up when attacked; this includes being hit by a pal sphere.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Sootseer is a large, ghost-like Pal. It has a round head and body, and doesn't appear to have legs. On its head is a round, white skull with two horns that point up, and two blunt teeth that sit at the bottom. Its eyes are hollow black circles with line green circles inside of them. The rest of its head is ablaze with a lilac coloured flame. It has two arms, the arms long and thin. Its hands are circular, with three diamond shaped claws that point downward on each hand. On the back of the hands, the hands are ablaze with the same lilac flame that the head is. Its arms are a dark purple to black colour. Its torso is a pitch black colour that later turns into the same dark purple to black colour the arms are. Its body ends with a swirl, with no legs in sight.

Sphere Modules are crafted equipment items that modify how Pal Spheres function when capturing Pals. 
They are crafted at the Sphere Workbench, Sphere Assembly Line, Sphere Assembly Line II, or Advanced Sphere Assembly Line, and are equipped in a dedicated slot in the Player's Inventory.
While not required to capture Pals, doing so can significantly alter capture behavior, such as increasing capture power, extending throw range, or adding homing and curve effects.

rows.t: Equipping it causes the sphere to spin with a curved trajectory. It becomes easier to catch Pals off guard, and increases capture power.
Equipping it makes the sphere heavier, reducing its range but increasing its capture power.
Equipping it makes the sphere home in on Pals, further increasing capture power.
Equipping it adds a slider spin to the sphere. The sphere curves at a sharp angle, making it easier to catch Pals off guard and increasing capture power.
Equipping it increases the sphere's range, allowing it to sharply capture Pals and enhancing capture power.
Equipping it significantly increases the sphere's range, allowing it to sharply capture Pals and enhancing capture power.

Stamina is a mechanic in Palworld and represents how much energy your character (or mounted Pal) has left.

sections.heading: About
sections.text:
Stamina can be found on the player's character, or any Pals when mounting them. You consume stamina when running, rolling, climbing, gliding, swimming, flying, jumping, and attacking.
Mount stamina is consumed and regenerated separately from character stamina. Swimming pals do not consume stamina while swimming. Since most flying pals can 'stand' on water, they don't lose stamina on water. Rather, they regenerate it.
Stamina is slowly regenerated when not consuming any stamina, and on the ground (not falling). Pals will still regenerate stamina while you are dismounted, but only if they are active and deployed. 
Your maximum stamina can be increased in the inventory by using points when you level up.
Being weighed down, crouching and sliding, and throwing Pal Spheres do not consume stamina. Using a Grappling Gun also doesn't, which means it can be used to avoid fall damage when you've run out of stamina.

sections.heading: Consequences
sections.text:
If you deplete your stamina, you won't be able to use any stamina-consuming actions anymore, until the entire bar has been refilled. An exception is swimming: you will only be able to stay afloat while the bar is recharging but won't be able to jump or climb out of the water.
If you run out of stamina, you often will take (indirect) damage as a result:
• While swimming: you will start using your health bar as stamina bar, resulting in damage and potentially death.
• While climbing: you will fall down, potentially leading to fall damage.
• While gliding: you will fall down, potentially leading to fall damage or drowning damage.
• While flying (on a Pal): you will fall down. You and your pal won't take fall damage, but you may unintentionally end up in a battle.
 ◦ Since most flying pals can stand (fly) on water, this won't be a drowning risk when using them.
• While attacking: running out of stamina may mean you can't return fire while recharging.
• While jumping: Pals with double jump may not be able to do their second jump when out of stamina, which can cause you to miss your (double) jump.

Statue of Power is a structure in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
It is a tier 6 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.
Otherwise, there are several of the statues scattered around the map in certain locations. These spawn locations do not change, but the easiest way to gain access to one is for players to simply build one of their own.
Naturally-spawning Statues of Power perform identically to built ones, but cannot be deconstructed by players.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
The Statue of Power is used to permanently enhance Player and Pal stats.
For players, capture power is enhanced using Lifmunk Effigy. The first enhancement costs 1 effigy and each subsequent enhancement costs 4 (3 for the first three levels) more than the last (1, 4, 7, 11, 15, 19, 23, 27, 31, 35, 39, 43, 47, 51). The capture power can only be enhanced 14 times (as of v0.4.11), which means to fully enhance their capture rate the player must gather a total of 353 effigies.
For Pals, one can permanently increase an individual Pal's Max Health, Attack, Defense, or Work Speed. Unlike player enhancements, these cost Small Pal Soul, Medium Pal Soul, and Large Pal Soul. It's important to note that Pal Enhancement is a one-time process, so choosing wisely which stat to boost for each Pal is important.

sections.heading: Interaction
sections.text:
• Find a Power Statue. These can either be built or can be found in various locations throughout the game world. If needed, see Map.
• Interact with the statue and select the "Enhance Pal" option.
• Select the Pal to be enhanced.
• Choose which stat to be boosted (Max Health, Attack, Defense, or Work Speed).
• Pay the Pal Soul cost to enhance the stat. The cost will increase depending on how much the stat is boosted.

sections.heading: Boost Stats
sections.text: • In small pal souls (4 Small Pal Soul = 2 Medium Pal Soul = 1 Large Pal Soul) as converted in the Crusher.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
• Consider the Pals natural strengths and weaknesses when choosing which stat to boost. For example, if you have a Pal that is naturally strong but has low defense, you might want to boost its defense. However if you have excess souls, you don't have to stick with just one stat; you can raise all of them.
• Pal Souls can be obtained by defeating and capturing Pals. You can also find them in treasure chests.
• You can reset a Pal's enhancement by spending Gold Coins at the Statue of Power. This will allow you to choose a different stat to boost or start over from scratch. Pal souls used are returned when you do this.
• To upgrade each stat to it's maximum costs 32 Small Pal Soul (or equivalent in larger souls [see: Crusher]) for a total of 128 to enhance every stat to the max.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The statue's resemblance to Anubis and its requirement of souls to enhance pals is probably a reference to the Egyptian deity Anubis being known as a god of funerals and death.

images.caption: The enhance menu, about to deposit one Small Pal Soul into a Foxparks' Work Speed

sections.text: Status conditions are various conditions that Pals can suffer from, whether it is a work-related or a combat-related condition.

sections.heading: Combat-related conditions
sections.text: Certain conditions can be inflicted in combat to players, human enemies, and Pals by various weapons or skills. While a Pal is afflicted by a condition, it becomes easier to catch. With the exception of Poison, which can stack independently of any other condition. Inflicting a different condition on a target with an already existing condition will replace the former condition with the latter condition (i.e. A Blind Pal hit with a Water-elemental skill may lose its Blind condition and gain the Soak condition).

sections.heading: Blind
sections.text: Most Dark-elemental skills have a chance to blind targets. While blinded, a player's vision will be greatly dimmed, and Pals' attack tracking will aim above the target's position, rather than at the target's position. Blind is represented visually by a dark cloud over the victim's eyes. Dark-elemental Pals are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Burn
sections.text: All Fire-elemental skills, all non-unique Dragon-elemental skills, a few Dark-elemental skills, all Implosion-related skills, and sources of fire like campfires, flamethrowers, and lava can set a target aflame. While on fire, the victim takes damage over time, and Grass-elemental attacks deal half damage. Additionally, the temperature for the victim increases, which may cause temperature damage due to heat, if the victim does not have the heat resistance for higher temperatures. Fire-elemental Pals are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Electrify
sections.text: All Electric-elemental skills and electric weapons (such as the and Shock Grenade) can electrify targets, rendering it immobile for a short period of time then causing electricity to course around it for a short period of time. During these times, Water-elemental attacks deal double damage. Electric-elemental Pals and Ground-elemental Pals are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Ivy-Covered
sections.text: All non-unique Grass-elemental skills can Ivy-Cover targets, entangling them with vines. While Ivy-Covered, the victim's movement is slowed, and Fire-elemental attacks deal double damage. Additionally, an entangled player cannot perform any action that requires stamina. Fire-elemental Pals and Grass-elemental Pals are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Freeze
sections.text: All Ice-elemental skills can freeze a target solid, completely immobilizing them for a short period of time. During this time, the temperature for the victim decreases, which may cause temperature damage due to cold, if the victim does not have the cold resistance for lower temperatures. Ice-elemental Pals are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Muddy
sections.text: All Ground-elemental skills can cover a victim in mud for a short time. During this time, the victim's movements are slowed, and Electric-elemental attacks deal half damage.

sections.heading: Poison
sections.text: Certain Dark-elemental skills such as Poison Blast and Poison Fog and the poison weapons can inflict poison on the victim. Poison deals damage over time and is the only combat condition that can occur concurrently with other combat conditions.

sections.heading: Soak
sections.text: All Water-elemental skills and being submerged in water can cause the target to become Soaked in water for a short period of time. During this time, the victim's movements are slowed; Electric-elemental attacks deal double damage; and Fire-elemental attacks deal half damage. Water-elemental Pals are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Stun
sections.text: Certain skills such as Stone Cannon and Penking's Emperor Slide, small Pals being trampled by larger ones, and shots from the can stun a target, causing it to ragdoll and be immobile for a short period of time. This is visually represented by a ring of stars revolving around the top of the victim. Players are immune to this condition.

sections.heading: Injury
sections.text:
These are conditions that the player's pals will develop while being low on Sanity and working at a base. These are temporary and just need the pals to rest up, either in bed or by being sent to the . They are usually indicated by certain health thresholds. It signifies a deployed pal's health, and will appear when they get hurt (either through a fight or environmental means). Different injury-afflictions will appear based on the severity of the injury. When a pal heals back to full health, "Injury" will disappear. Pals inside of a Palbox will heal quicker than deployed Pals. Here's a rundown of all the injuries and their severity: 
• Minor Injury: A minor injury is a relatively small health concern for your Pal. It won't prevent them from battling or working, but it will slowly drain their health over time. If left untreated, a minor injury can worsen to a major injury, which is much more serious. The simplest way to heal a minor injury is to let them rest. Their health will slowly regenerate over time.
• Major Injury: Pals with Major Injury are badly hurt, and it will take longer for them to heal. Once they heal back to a certain percentage of their max health, the Major Injury affliction is replaced by Minor Injury.
• Incapacitated: Incapacitated Pals have lost all health, and lost consciousness as a result. They will not regenerate their health back over time, and the only way to heal them is to revive them in a Palbox. Incapacitated Pals act identically to corpses left behind by slain enemies. When a Pal has been incapacitated, other Pals with the Handiwork Work Suitability will attempt to rescue them (if they're not too busy getting killed themselves), but by far the easiest way to help Incapacitated Pals is by moving them from the "Pals at the base" section to one of the Boxes in the Palbox. By default, it will take 10 minutes for them to revive, and longer to regenerate back to full health.

sections.heading: Deficit of SAN
sections.text:
Sanity (SAN) is one of a pal's three bars, next to hunger and health. When a deployed Pal has a good level of SAN, it will display "In good condition!" in the Base Info HUD. If their SAN lowers (caused by things such as a lack of a pal bed or from being overworked), they will become less and less motivated to work, and will outright refuse to do it more and more frequently. Once a stressed pal reaches a certain threshold, they will become "Extremely unhealthy due to neglect", and will look sombre, unless another animation (such as relaxing in a ) overrides their facial expression. Here are all of the illnesses Pals can suffer: 
• Sick/Cold: Work Speed of the Pal is decreased by 10. Low Grade Medical Supplies is required to remove this status condition.
• Sprain: Movement Speed of the Pal is decreased by 10. Low Grade Medical Supplies is required to remove this status condition.
• Overfull: Hunger depletion amount of the Pal is decreased by 100. Low Grade Medical Supplies required to remove this status condition.
• Ulcer: Work Speed and Movement Speed of the Pal is decreased by 20 and 10 respectively. Medical Supplies is required to remove this status condition.
• Fracture: Work Speed and Movement Speed of the Pal is decreased by 10 and 20 respectively. Medical Supplies is required to remove this status condition.
• Weakened: Work Speed and Movement Speed of the Pal is decreased by 20 and 30 respectively. High Grade Medical Supplies is required to remove this status condition.
• Depressed: Work Speed and Movement Speed of the Pal is decreased by 30 and 20 respectively. High Grade Medical Supplies is required to remove this status condition.

sections.heading: Deficit of SAT
sections.text:
Satiety (SAT), also known as Hunger, is a stat that reflects how full your Pal is after eating. It's essentially a measure of their fullness meter. Each Pal has a satiety gauge displayed on their information panel. This gauge fills up when they eat food and depletes over time as they perform actions. Different foods offer varying degrees of satiety. Simple berries might only fill a small portion of the gauge, while a hearty meal can provide significant satiety. You can check the Nutrition information of food items to see their satiety value. Players also have a SAT meter, and are subject to the same effects. Here's a breakdown of what might be going on:
• Hungry: When a deployed Pal hasn't eaten in a while, the "Base Info" HUD will display this, and a floating bread symbol will appear above them. Pals in the party will automatically eat from the if the player has one with food or from a food slot while the player has a Small Feed Bag or subsequent items. Pals working at the base will eat automatically from a Feed Box. However, sometimes they can get stuck in structures or spots of the map, making them unable to eat. Returning them to the Palbox before immediately deploying them will unstick them and allow them to eat again.
• Starving: If the satiety gauge is fully depleted, the Pal will begin starving. While starving, the Pal will rapidly lose health over time and will not regenerate health, but this effect cannot incapacitate them. Feeding a Starving Pal will remove this status.

Stone is a basic resource or material that you can find throughout the world. It is used to craft many early-game items, such as the stone pickaxe and workbench. You can gather stone by hitting rocks with your bare hands or using a pickaxe.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text:
• Stone is abundantly scattered throughout the Palworld map. Look for rocky areas, cliffs, and mountains - these are prime spots to gather some stone.
• Stone Pit: Build this structure at your base and assign Pals with the "Mining" trait to automate stone collection, freeing you for other tasks.

sections.heading: Use
sections.text:
• Crafting: Stone forms the backbone of crafting numerous early-game items that ensure your survival and advancement.
• Buildings: Construct various structures for your base, like houses, storage facilities, and workshops.
• Decorations: Add some personality to your base with stone-based decorations.
• Trading: Sell excess stone to merchants for Gold Coins.

Stone Structure Set is a set of structures in Palworld.
It's a tier 18 technology item and requires 3 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Like the Wooden Structure Set, once unlocked, one can then build 8 basic structures, this time for stone. These include walls, foundation, roofs, stairs, and a door.
Like other structure sets, all structures need a form of contact with the ground. Walls and doors can be built when vertically connecting to a roof or foundation. If this foundation is broken, all walls connected to it will break if there is no additional gripping point above or below them (and drop their ingredients), even if they are touching another wall.

sections.heading: General
sections.text:
Each storage slot is capped at 9999. For example: A Wooden Chest has a storage capacity of 99990 items but has 10 unique item slots.
• The most cost-efficient storing method is the Metal Chest with 24 unique item slots.
• Large Containers can be stacked, providing compact high capacity storage.
• The best storage container is the Advanced Chest as it can be stacked and has 54 unique item slots, same as the Guild Chest, with the exception that the Guild Chest is a shared container.
• The Feybreak Update added a building called the Item Retrieval Machine that can be unlocked at level 49 to easily find items within the base.

sections.heading: List of Storage Containers
sections.text: • The Guild Chest is a shared container, all Guild Chests have the same content.

The Stun Baton is a melee weapon in Palworld.
Unlike most of the other melee weapons in the game, the Stun Baton is more of a utility than a tool for direct combat, as it deals low damage but is able to sometimes shock Pals or enemies that are struck with the baton. This makes the Stun Baton most useful for Pal capturing, as the electric shocks delivered by the baton make Pals more susceptible to being captured.

Surfent (Japanese: シーペント Seapent) is a Water element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Surfent is a turquoise-coloured serpentine Pal resembling a prehistoric marine reptile, such as a Pliosaurid or Tylosaur, although the overall silhouette of its body shape can also be interpreted to vaguely resemble a Viking longship. It has a very triangular head with two horns jutting out of the far corners of the skull, a cream-coloured face and stomach, four fins that it uses to swim with, and various navy blue accents found on the tips of the fins, the tail, and a spot on the forehead. The eyes are a bright orange and are very sharp acute triangles in shape. There are six orange triangle-shaped plates or spines running along the back, spaced between both pairs of fins, which are capable of moving up and down as the creature swims. The long tail is tipped orange following a navy blue stripe.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
A Pal that becomes aggressive upon seeing the player; it can be seen swimming along shorelines in groups.
While it can be commanded to travel on land, it is very sluggish whilst doing so.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Swift Swimmer
• Can be ridden to travel on water. While mounted, prevents stamina depletion while moving over water. (Requires Surfent Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 10)
Work Suitability:
• Watering 2
Possible Drops:
• Pal Fluids

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its Japanese name may come from sea and serpent.
• Surfent was featured in Paldeck No.047 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Surfent Saddle is a Key Item in Palworld. 
It is a tier 10 Technology and unlocks Surfent's Partner Skill.
Surfent Terra (Japanese: スナペント Sunapent) is the Ground element variation of Surfent.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Surfent Terra largely resembles is regular counterpart but has notable differences. Unlike a regular Surfent's turquoise body with navy accents, Surfent Terra's body is a navy color with orange accents. It has thick shiny golden spikes on its back. The tips of its fins are colored tan. The most notable difference Surfent Terra has from its regular counterpart is having a golden horn on the top of its head, similar to a narwhal's tusk.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Surfent Terra mostly roam in numerous groups and can be found scavenging corpses left on the desert areas it inhabits.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Sand Swimmer - Can be ridden. While in team, Surfent Terra helps carry supplies, decreasing the weight of ores in the player's inventory. (Requires Aquadon Terra Saddle→ Unlocks at Lv. 24)
Work Suitability:
• Gathering 1
Possible Drops:
• Pal Fluids
His ability reduces 10% off the weight of ore, coal, pure quartz and sulfur (not stone or ingots). After condensing for the first star level, the ability improves to an 11% reduction in weight. 4 star ability reduces weight by 14%.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Its Japanese name may come from 砂 suna (sand) and serpent.
Surfent Terra Saddle is a key item in Palworld. It allows players to ride Surfent Terra and have direct control over its attacks.

It has long pointy appendages on its forehead and back, that shift from red to dark red and then yellow towards the tip.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal doesn't have an established habitat in the wild, as such, it is only encountered through certain situations, such as when encountering a swarm, as an Alpha Pal in dungeons and during a Flying Wild Pal raid on a player base of sufficiently high level.

Water Suzaku Saddle�

Work Suitability:

Possible Drops:

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• This pal's design originates from the Vermilion Bird of Chinese mythology, which explains the element of the original form's appearance, and name.
• Its Japanese name may originate from Shiva (one of the main deities of Hinduism). Shiva is attributed with a third eye, the crescent moon as a crown, a blue throat, and the holy river Ganga flowing from Shiva's matted hair, among other attributes, some of these traits can parallel Suzaku Aqua's design and concept, which may be the reasoning behind its given Japanese name.

Its body is much more fuzzy to where it has a section of fluff that resemble a bushy mustache. Its eyes compared to Swee appear slightly different, having a more mellow or tired look to them. sections.text: Syndicate Cleaners are a type of enemy affiliated with the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Syndicate Cleaners are found in Syndicate outposts or sometimes out in the open with a few weaker Syndicate members. They wield a flamethrower that lets them spray huge streams of fire over a frighteningly long distance, making them very dangerous to fight at close range, plus the added threat of being burned if you get hit by the flames of a Cleaner's attack. Due to the nature of this attack, it's absolutely vital to wear heat-resistant armor when dealing with Syndicate Cleaners to reduce the risk of getting burned if you happen to get hit by their fire.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • The Syndicate Cleaners' name may be a reference to the saying "fire is the ultimate cleanser".
Syndicate Crushers are a type of enemy affiliated with the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Syndicate Crushers are usually found in Syndicate Outposts and in the northest part of Dessicated Desert, and sometimes in high-level dungeons. They are unique from the rest of the Rayne Syndicate members in that they are much bigger, being roughly twice as tall as an average NPC. Because of their towering stature, Syndicate Crushers have copiously high health and are able to wield huge Miniguns not unlike the one Grizzbolt can use, allowing them to pepper you with a barrage of bullets fired at a ridiculously fast rate. Needless to say, Syndicate Crushers are extremely dangerous enemies to face due to the sheer power of their attacks, and should ideally be avoided altogether unless you have some very powerful Pals in your party to be able to counter them.
Syndicate Grenadiers are a type of enemy affiliated with the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Syndicate Grenadiers are typically found in Syndicate Outposts, where they congregate in sizeable groups. They're armed with Frag Grenades and are able to attack by throwing the grenades at you, generating big explosions once the grenades hit the ground. This can make fighting Syndicate Grenadiers challenging if you're facing a whole group of them at once, but thankfully they do have one major weakness: they're utterly defenseless in close quarters as they have no close-ranged attacks to use against you. Therefore, the best way to defeat a Syndicate Grenadier is to get close to him and keep attacking him with a melee weapon until he dies; although if you have ranged weapons, those can help too.
Syndicate Gunners are a type of enemy affiliated with the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Syndicate Gunners are found in Syndicate Outposts, and in Syndicate scouting parties that try to invade your base during a Raid. Essentially a step up from a Syndicate Thug, Syndicate Gunners are typically armed with more powerful guns such as Assault Rifles. It's advisable to try attacking Syndicate Gunners from a distance to avoid getting mowed down by their gunfire.
Syndicate Hunters are a type of enemy affiliated with the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Syndicate Grenadiers are usually found in Syndicate Outposts, or out in the open alongside several weaker enemies such as Syndicate Thugs. Much like a Syndicate Gunner, Syndicate Hunters rely on their firearms to dispose of their targets; however, Hunters use even more advanced weapons such as the Pump-Action Shotgun, making them very dangerous to engage if you're underprepared. Trying to fight a Syndicate Hunter without some decent firepower of your own or a party of powerful Pals is not recommended, as the Hunter's attacks will wear you down very quickly if you don't have the means to take him down before he can land too many hits on you.
Syndicate Thugs are a type of enemy affiliated with the Rayne Syndicate.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Syndicate Thugs are among the first types of enemies to be encountered in the game; they may be encountered in Syndicate outposts or show up in Syndicate scouting parties that try to invade your base during a Raid. Sometimes one or two lone Syndicate Thugs can also be found out in the open or inside dungeons. Syndicate Thugs are usually armed with a Wooden Club or a Handgun, making them fairly easy to take down provided your Pals and equipment are strong enough to fend them off.
Interestingly, they can be captured with a pal sphere, and may be even be deployed in the same manner as any other pal, to fight alongside the player or to work at a base.

images.caption: A Syndicate Thug at the crafting station.

Tanzee (Japanese: エテッパ Eteppa) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Tanzee is a small, monkey-like being with a bright green body and cream-colored accents on its face, belly, and feet. Its large, round eyes sparkle with a cheerful expression, and it has a wide, happy smile that adds to its adorable charm. The creature’s head is adorned with a unique tuft of leaf-like shapes, giving it a plant-inspired appearance. Its ears are large and round, with vibrant yellow interiors that contrast with its green body. A long, thin tail extends from its back, ending in a leaf-shaped tip, further enhancing its nature-themed design. Its short limbs, with small arms and feet, make the creature look compact and playful, embodying a mix of natural and animal-like characteristics that make it both cute and whimsical.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Usually flees if the player is within the vicinity and will attack violently if struck.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Tanzee's name may be a combination of tanbark or -tan (Japanese honorific for babies and small children) and chimpanzee.
 ◦ Eteppa may be a combination of 猿公 etekō (monkey) and -ppa (verb suffix for doing something constantly).
• Its Paldeck entry is possibly a reference to the Pokémon Gun meme, or the Monkey with a gun meme.
• Tanzee was featured in Paldeck No.028 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.
Tanzee's Assault Rifle is a key item in Palworld.

sections.heading: Acquisition
sections.text: It is a tier 12 technology item and costs 1 point to unlock. It can be crafted at a Pal Gear Workbench.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: It unlocks Tanzee's Partner Skill, Cheery Rifle.
Tarantriss (Japanese: モモンチュラ Momontula) is a Dark elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Tarantriss is a spider-like Pal, with its lower body being just like a spider's and the upper half being a little bit more human-like. Tarantriss has four pointed legs, each leg pointed like a knife. Each leg is a pale mauve colour and are tipped with black, with magenta puff balls at the top of the legs. In the spider abdomen of Tarantriss, the lower half is black. The upper half is also black, separated from the bottom half with a line of magenta fluff. On the top of the abdomen are three horizontal magenta stripes. These stripes have circles on them. Tarantriss' torso is separated from the lower half of the body with a circle of spiky magenta fluff. At the bottom of the torso, there is a small black triangle that points upwards. It looks like Tarantriss is wearing a white tank top with two golden horizontal diamond shapes on the chest. Tarantriss' arms are black, with thick mauve cuffs around the wrists with dark mauve horizontal stripes around them. It has three small black claws on each hand. On the top of the chest, there is a upside down black triangle that makes it look like a v-neck. Around the neck is a ring of spiked magenta fluff. Tarantriss' head is black, with yellow goggle like parts over its eyes. Tarantriss' eyes are black slits, with white lights. Its head is covered with spiky mauve fluff. On the top of Tarantriss' head is two mauve coloured horns, the horns tipped with black. Separating the mauve colour from the black colour is a magenta zigzag. On its forehead are three magenta ovals. At the back of its head are black spikes.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: In the wild, ignores the player unless attacked. In dungeons, they are hostile and attack on sight.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Tarantriss can be found typically near the Scorched Hill teleportation point. The boss version can be found northeast of the Feybreak Shipwreck teleportation point at coordinates -1341, 1429. Tarantriss can also be found in the desert region to the northeast at night

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: Tarantriss' Web 
• Can be ridden.
• Can double jump while mounted.
• Additionally, can shoot spider silk to grapple and move at fast speed.
Work Suitability:
• Handiwork Lv. 2 (Lv. 3 at max rank)
• Medicine Production Lv. 2 (Lv. 3 at max rank)
• Transporting Lv. 2 (Lv. 3 at max rank)
• Gathering Lv. 2 (Lv. 3 at max rank)
Possible Drops: 
• Fiber
• Venom Glands

sections.heading: Active Skills
sections.text: Webstrike Impact: Tarantriss' exclusive skill. Spits out a ball of thread, entangling the target it hits. Then rushes at full speed to the trapped target and delivers three slashing strikes to finish it off.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Tarantriss's Name is possibly A combination of Tarantula and Mistress.

sections.heading: Gameplay Notes
sections.text:
• You can fly very far when you use its ability and jump. Its very fun! (Works only on flat land or going down hills however).
• Tarantriss gets an additional double jump when it's web swing connects to a surface. With practice and careful aim, it is possible to use this to climb cliffs and even air walk, not touching the ground until the mount runs out of stamina.
• When using Webstrike impact, the aim of the dash can only be changed in the time between when the projectile fires and the charge begins. After tarantriss begins to dash, it's direction cannot be changed. The aim of it's dash also seems to get easily "distracted" by other nearby enemies, making it easy to miss with the skill.
Teafant (Japanese: チョロゾウ Chorozou) is a Water element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Teafant is a small pal that resembles an elephant. Its cream-colored body gives off the appearance of a teapot or watering can with its trunk acting as the spout. It has shiny teal eyes. The top of its head has a light blue floral marking and a tuft of hair. Teafant has a long thin tail with a light blue tufted end. It has floppy ears shaped like butterfly wings which are tipped in a light blue.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: By default wild Teafant are non-hostile towards the player unless attacked, in which case all nearby Teafant will become aggressive as well.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Missing Information.

sections.heading: Dungeons
sections.text: • Missing Information.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill: 
• Soothing Shower - When activated, spouts mysterious water that soothes wounds and restores the player's HP.
Work Suitability:
• Watering 1
Possible Drops:
• Normal 
 ◦ Pal Fluids
• Boss
 ◦ Ancient Civilization Parts
 ◦ Pal Fluids
 ◦ Precious Entrails
 ◦ Ring of Water Resistance

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Teafant was named by the community during the 3rd Name This Pal event.
• Its name may come from the words tea and elephant.
• Its Japanese name may come from ちょろちょろ chorochoro (onomatopoeia for trickling water) and 象 zou (elephant).
• Teafant is called 'Ganesha' within the game files; Ganesha being the name of a highly revered elephant-headed deity in the Hindu religion!
• Teafant's ability to heal is incredibly rare, being the only early game Pal that has the power to restore the player's HP, this is likely inspired by the Teapot design and the medicinal effects herbal tea is considered to have.

images.caption: Teafant and Kelpsea watering crops in an early unreleased version of the game, from a Palworld trailer released in 2021.
thumb|Two wild Teafants sitting on their hind legs.

Technology is a gameplay mechanic in Palworld.
It's made up of blueprints split between technology and Ancient Technology. Blueprints are unlocked via meeting tiers and using Technology Points and Ancient Technology Points.
The player gains 6 Technology Points every time they level up.
The player also gain 1 Technology Points every time a fast travel or a Tower Fast Travel point is unlocked and 5 ancient technology points for defeating a tower boss.
It is also possible to receive Technical Manuals from various sources that grant Technology Points, or Ancient Technology Points.
As of terraria update (Early Access 0.6.0), the maximum level is 65 so the number of points gained for leveling up is 6x64 (since character start as level 1) = 384 Points.
There is also 75 fast travel points and 7 Tower Fast Travel Points making the total of technology points earned:
384 + 75 + 7 = 466 without farming for Technical Manuals.

The Three Shot Bow is a ranged weapon in Palworld. It is unlocked at level 10 for 2 Technology Points.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Like the Old Bow, the Three Shot Bow is a ranged weapon that fires Arrows. However, it comes with the benefit of being able to shoot three Arrows at once, dealing three times the damage. Additionally, the Three Shot Bow only consumes one Arrow while firing, making it a very ammo-efficient weapon.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: • The Three-Shot Bow's spread mechanic means that it generally isn't the best weapon for longer ranges unless the user is aiming at a large target.

sections.heading: History
sections.text:
• Early Access 0.1.2.0
 ◦ Introduced.

Tocotoco (Japanese: クルットリ Kuruttori) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Tocotoco broadly resembles a toucan, featuring vibrant green plumage on its head and neck area, with orange lines reminiscent of a beard. Its lower body is covered with black feathers, and its chest by what appears similar to a mask. Additionally, its wings boast a combination of orange and white feathers, while a striking mohawk made up of yellow and red plumes crowns its head.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal may look harmless but it has a propensity for exploding. Once a player gets spotted by Tocotoco it will start approaching to detonate upon proximity. When executing its Implode attack, Tocotoco accelerates, while emitting a pulsating glow.
Tocotoco are usually found in groups of 2 or 3 that will attack players together. Moreover, this Pal invades bases en masse, referred to in-game as a "Tocotoco Implode Unit." Tocotoco can also be seen scavenging corpses sometimes.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Usually found in the deserts, and in the dungeons in Mount Obsidian.

 ◦ Gunpowder
 ◦ Tocotoco Feather

Tombat (Japanese: ニャンバット Nyanbat) is a Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Tombat appears as a cross between a cat and a bat, taking the form of a black and white cat, with purple and yellow wings that seem to end in ribbons, red eyes, and three large white claws on each hand and foot. It also features a red fur piece on its chest and a small yellow diamond on its forehead. Three purple will-o'-wisps always follow it around; one over each shoulder, and one attached to the end of its tail.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
This Pal, being a nocturnal species, can only be found at night or in dungeons. They will still be able to spawn for a while after the break of dawn, and won't completely disappear until the sun is actually visibly rising in the horizon.
If a player goes near a Tombat, it will immediately attack the player, as it is aggressive.
If employed as a Base Pal, Tombats will still continue working through the night, and won't need beds to accommodate them, as they never sleep. If not tasked with anything, they'll simply wander about the base. These traits are shared with all nocturnal Pals.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Spawn during the night.

sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Ultrasonic Sensor - When activated, uses ultrasonic waves to detect the location of nearby Pals.
Work Suitability:

• Mining 2
• Transporting 2
Possible Drops:
• Leather
• Small Pal Soul

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Its name seems to come from the words tomcat and bat.
• Tombat may have also been inspired by the Cat-sìth from Celtic myth.

Towers are various structures found throughout the Palpagos Islands.

images.caption: Concept art of the inside of a tower, featuring the arena where the residing boss is met

sections.heading: General
sections.text:
There are currently seven towers present in the game, one for each of the leaders of the factions that can be encountered in the game.
Mechanically, towers are similar to certain dungeons that start with "Sealed Realm of the ..." in their names, but instead of fighting a specific Alpha Pal, the player is tasked with fighting a tamed Pal, alongside its master. There is a time limit of ten minutes imposed onto the player; if said timer runs out before the tower boss is defeated, the game automatically kills the player on the spot, and he/she is booted out. The map will then open, prompting a choice of respawn location. The player's belongings will be dropped back at the tower's entrance for their owner to pick up.
Defeating the boss living in their respective tower will prompt the unlocking of a fast travel point found on the tower's roof.
Tower bosses can be re-challenged at any time, simply by re-entering their respective tower.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
• There was a glitch that allowed the player to catch the bosses (Fixed in Early Access 0.1.5.0). The player must first attain a "wanted" status, before entering the boss tower. Once the PIDF guards spawn in and attack, the player can then "backshot" the boss for the capture bonus, allowing the player to catch the boss. This allows the player to use the boss on their team and be put to work in a base.
• According to the journal entries, Zoe's Grizzbolt is canonically female.

is the ability to transport items from gathering stations and items scattered about in the base to storage units.
Regardless of skill level, all transporters from 1 to 5 will only carry single pieces off the ground when farmed from natural nodes like Stone, Trees, Ore or even the Ranch, as said items drop in stacks of 1. The skill levels however do affect how much they carry at a time from tasks such as the Logging Site, Stone Pit, Plantations or any large stack you drop;
• Level 1 = 2 items
• Level 2 = 5 items
• Level 3 = 10 items
• Level 4 = 20 items
• Level 5 = 40 items*
• Only for Knocklem, Wumpo, and Wumpo Botan as they are the only Pals with level 4 transporting that can reach level 5 through the Condenser. However, it is worth noting that even though they have the highest transporting skill they will always prioritize their other jobs first. For example, with level 3 Lumbering, they will prioritize cutting trees or working the Logging Site instead of transporting, making it redundant to use them in this role unless no trees remain and no Logging Site exists.
Work Speed buffs do not affect this in any way. It does not improve how many items they carry, or how fast they travel while carrying them. As such, recommended buffs are Movement Speed traits to increase the speed at which they move items, and/or Food and Sanity drain traits to help them work for longer periods before taking breaks.
Recommended Pal = Helzephyr - Found around the Verdant Grove and Northwestern shore of the Eastern Wild Island at night.
Level 3 transporting is its only job and can be upgraded to Level 4 through the Condenser for 20 items carry capacity. It is a fast Pal, travelling between node and chest at a quick pace, has a medium hitbox to find the "sweetspot" between nodes and closely placed chests, and it doesnt sleep, working day and night potentially collecting items missed during the day while other pals sleep.
Worksites that are considered 'tasks' rather than natural spawned nodes make use of transporting;
• Logging Site
• All Mining Sites
• Plantations
• Large stacks dropped by player

Treasure chests can be found all over Palpagos Islands and within its numerous Dungeons. Most chests are accessible without any special requirements, but the more valuable chests need a Copper Key, Silver Key and Gold Key depending on the area level.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Treasure chests are common throughout Palpagos Island and respawn daily.

sections.heading: Chest Farming Locations
sections.text: The best locations in Palworld for frequent chest farming are the Dessicated Desert and Astral Mountains. These spots are great for obtaining valuable items from early to late game.

sections.heading: Unlocking Methods
sections.text:
Some chests don't require keys to be opened, but some are locked and require specific keys to open, which are: 
• Copper Key
• Silver Key
• Gold Key
These keys can also be found in other chests.
Pals like Mimog and it's Partner Skill can unlock treasure chests. Sometimes, Mimog may replace a treasure chest.
Lockpicking can also be used for unlocking treasure chest as well.

sections.heading: Types
sections.text: Treasure chests can be split into six Grades.

rows.t: Can be found in all regions, these chests don’t need a key to be unlocked. Their contents vary based on the location and can be recognized by their yellow glow.
Only found on the low level starter islands, these chests needs a Copper Key to be unlocked. It always includes a schematic for low level gear.
Found in all regions, these chests don’t need a key to be unlocked. Their contents vary by location, but outside of dungeons, they typically contain a larger amount of Gold Coin.
Located in the Forest, Desert, and Volcano regions, these chests needs a Silver Key to be unlocked. It consistently holds a schematic for gears appropriate to the level.
Found only in dungeons located in the Desert, Volcano, and Snowy regions, these chests don’t need a key to be unlocken. It occasionally appears in the final room of a dungeon.
Found only in the Astral Mountains, these chests needs a Gold Key to be unlocked. It consistently holds a schematic for either the Refined Metal Armors, the Assault Rifle, or the Pump-Action Shotgun.

Univolt (Japanese: ライコーン Raikorn) is a Electric element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Univolt is a unicorn-like Pal. On its head, it has a sharp, jagged navy purple-blue horn that points upwards. Covering the top of its head is what looks like a golden helmet with a golden lightning bolt shaped spike sticking out of it. Running down its neck are jagged golden spikes that resemble lightning bolts. It has ruby red eyes that appear narrowed. Its body is a teal-turquoise colour. On its chest, it has a spiky golden spot with three spikes that point upwards. It has a golden lightning bolt tail that stick upwards, with many spikes surrounding the tail. It has a golden lightning bolt pattern running down its back. Its legs are rather muscular, with golden tufts around the hooves, with are a dark teal colour. Around where the leg joints are, Univolt has tufts of teal-ish fur.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal may become aggressive towards the player upon sight.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Univolt can be found as a level 31 Alpha Pal in the Seabreeze Archipelago.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text:
Partner Skill:
• Swift Deity - Can be ridden. Applies Electric damage to the player's attacks while mounted. (Requires Univolt Saddle → Unlocks at Lv. 14)
Work Suitability:
• Generating Electricity 2
• Lumbering 1
Possible Drops: 
• Leather
• Electric Organ
• Horn

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Univolt's name is a portmanteau of unicorn, which references Univolt's equine shape and its horn, and volt referencing its type.
• It was named by the community during a Name This Pal event.
• Its Japanese name may come from 雷光 raikou (lightning) and unicorn.
• Univolt was featured in Paldeck No.002 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

There are a number of utility items in Palworld.
They can be divided between Movement and QoL(Quality of Life).

sections.heading: Movement
sections.text: Movement items are Weapons and other equipment used to help you traverse the land of Palworld. They can be unlocked by using the Technology branch.

rows.t: Grappling Gun is a type of Grappling Gun in Palworld. It is a tier 12 ancient technology item and costs 1 point to unlock. It can be crafted at a Primitive Workbench. For more information on Grappling Guns, see Grappling Guns.

Grappling Gun is a type of Grappling Gun in Palworld. It is a tier 17 ancient technology item and costs 2 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a Weapon Workbench or Assembly Line. For more information on Grappling Guns, see Grappling Guns.

Grappling Gun is a type of Grappling Gun in Palworld. It is a tier 32 ancient technology item and costs 3 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a Weapon Workbench or Assembly Line. For more information on Grappling Guns, see Grappling Guns.

Grappling Gun is a type of Grappling Gun in Palworld. It is a tier 47 ancient technology item and costs 4 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a ((Waiting on more information)) For more information on Grappling Guns, see Grappling Guns.

Normal Parachute is an item in Palworld that lets you glide through the air. It's a tier 5 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a Primitive Workbench or better. For more information on Parachutes see Gliding.

Mega Glider is an item in Palworld that lets you glide through the air. It's a tier 18 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a Quality Workbench. For more information on Gliders see Gliding.

Giga Glider is an item in Palworld that lets you glide through the air. It's a tier 40 technology item and requires 3 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a Quality Workbench. For more information on Gliders see Gliding.

sections.heading: Quality of Life
sections.text: QoL items are Accessories that can help make your life easier while you are on your journey in Palworld. They can be unlocked by using the Technology branch.

rows.t: Hip Lantern is a Key Item in Palworld. It is a tier 22 technology and costs 2 points to unlock. It can be crafted at a Quality Workbench.

Average Feed Bag is an item in Palworld. It is unlocked at technology tier 20 and costs 2 points in the Ancient Technology tree. It can be crafted at a Quality Workbench.

Vanwyrm (Japanese: カバネドリ Kabanedori) is a Fire / Dark element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Vanwyrm resembles a condor with a gray head and gray spikes on its back. Its plumage is mainly black shifting to red at the border of its wings. The feathers of its tail are black on the upper surface and red on the lower surface.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text:
Wild Vanwyrm are typically encountered in pairs, flying at higher altitudes or hovering close to the ground. This Pal can exhibit aggression upon detecting the presence of players, launching unprovoked attacks.
Very rarely in Astral Mountains, a wild Vanwyrm can be seen fight a Vanwyrm Cryst, despite Astral Mountains not being its habitat.

• Increases damage player deals to enemy weak points while mounted.
Work Suitability:

• Transporting 3
Possible Drops:

• Gold Coin

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Its Japanese name may come from 屍 shikabane (corpse) and 鳥 tori (bird).

Its body is predominantly cloaked in a bluish-white hue, accentuated by the crystalline appearance of its feathers as well as the spikes found on its forehead and along its back. The waist and hindlegs are tinged with blue, contrasting with the bright yellow coloring found on the distal portions of its legs

Verdash (Japanese: ラベロット Raberotto) is a Grass element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Verdash has a sleek humanoid rabbit-like appearance. It's torso, arms, head, and bottom half of it's legs are a lemon chiffon color. It has white sclera and brown irises.
Verdash has long rabbit-like ears, that are a gradient of lemon chiffon and pale green, while the inside of it's ears are sandy brown. In between it's ears is a tuft of pale green hair, with a yellow highlight in the middle. On the pal's neck resides a flower like pale green main, with yellow highlights.
On the lower half of it's torso and upper legs, is pale green fur, similar to shorts. The waistline is sandy brown. Right above it's knee, the green fur stops, replaced with a yellow flower-like rim. It has a pale green fluffy tail on it's back side.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Verdash is an aggressive pal. When it spots the player, it does a hand gesture similar to Anubis, challenging the player to a battle.
