As of today, Palworld is still in Early Access, so it's actively receiving updates and changes. Staying connected to official channels and community discussions will help you stay informed about the latest developments. A roadmap detailing various upcoming features is available.

sections.heading: Early Access
sections.text: These updates are about the Early Access period of Palworld.

rows.t: Update to the Moonlord, Summoning Pals and various other fixes.

• Crossplay Enabled across all platforms
• Global Palbox for cross-world Pal transfers
• 10x Storage Upgrade via Dimensional Pal Storage
• Cosmetic Armour System (appearance-only gear)
• Photo Mode for screenshots
• Blueprint Crafting with Drafting Table
• Mac Dedicated Servers now available
• Balance & UI Tweaks, New Achievements, Major Bug Fixes
Feybreak balance changes and bug fixes.
Xenolord Raid balancing and bug fixes.
Adjustments to Base and Bug fixes.
Added Meteorite Event to World Settings.
Buffing Alpha Pals, Making boss fights more challenging, and Improvement in multiplayer functionality.
Faster Hatching, Server Optimization and various Bug Fixes.
Fixed an issue where eggs could not be placed in the egg incubator if you loaded save data from v0.1.5.x or earlier.
New Raid Bosses, Base Building Improvements, Character Customization, Balance Adjustments and various Bug Fixes.
Fixed base management issues, improves gameplay mechanics, and adds quality of life features like keybinds and server browsing.
Addressed save data corruption related to high guild Pal capture numbers. Resolved disappearing weapons issue in multiplayer with grenades.
Fixed crashes related to capturing over 7,000 Pals. Addressed Lifmunk Effigies capture power reset bug. Fixed equipping armor in wrong slots. Various stability and performance improvements.
Fixed loading screen issue, incorrect world date display, text corrections, and anti-cheat measures.
Early Access Launch. Introduced non-buildable zones around Alpha Pal spawn locations to improve server stability. Addressed various critical issues and exploits.
Resolved issues causing save data over 16MB to fail and multiplayer crashes.
Fixed crashes, addressed exploits (not officially labeled as a separate version, but implemented on Xbox).
Major update focuses on bug fixes, crash mitigation, save data corruption prevention, anti-cheat measures, and various gameplay improvements.
No official patch notes available for Xbox, but it can be assumed that stability fixes similar to the Steam update were implemented.
Launch in Early Access on Xbox One and Xbox Series X/S, available with Xbox Game Pass.

sections.text: Victor Ashford is the leader of the Pal Genetic Research Unit and owner of the artificially enhanced Pal Shadowbeak. His tower is located in the Astral Mountains, in the Land of Absolute Zero.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Victor is a pale, thin man with a black and white wavy, shoulder length hair. His eyes are covered with a black blindfold. 
He wears a black high-collared long coat with golden topstitching and a triangle shaped button. A badge is displayed on the left side of his coat. The coat has a waist belt hanging from both sides that ends with belt ornaments. He also wears black gloves that cover his hands and black boots.

sections.heading: Biography
sections.text:
Victor Ashford, a cold and calculating man of science, is very ambitious and aims to create powerful Pals by experimenting on them and employing genetic engineering.
Victor works on different experiments to achieve his goals. One of these experiments is focused on creating an artificial powerful Pal through genetic engineering, while the other involves enhancing Pals' power and abilities through the use of distillation.
For the interspecies Pal experiment, he attempted to combine different Pals species, but most resulted in failures. Although some of the combinations were successful, the bodies that were produced deteriorated in few seconds, which he ordered disposal of.
These failures in getting the results he wanted made him reconsider and adjust the materials he used in his experiments, such as ordering the extracting of chemicals from Eikthyrdeer antlers to assist in creating a compound to help stabilizing the experiments. He also ordered the acquiring of an ice Pal to have a better storage temperatures for the compound.
He expanded the range of the Pals species that will be included in the experiment and ordered their re-examination so that those with the more desirable DNA can be selected, which will increase the experiment's chances of success.
However, he lacked the necessary equipment for this process as the equipment he is using currently is sourced from outside and had a limit to how far they can be upgraded, for that he ordered the building of new equipment made by his organization so he can upgrade them with ease when necessary.
For the enhancement of Pals through the use of distillation, Victor liquefied a Pal and injected the liquid into a Pal of the same species. The result was a success, the Pal's power and abilities were enhanced.
This experiment was meant to give Victor a different way to generate powerful Pals, and its success surprised him. It made him theorize that Pals of the same species might have important nutrients or an energy source within them that is necessary for their own species' existence.
He deemed that more investigation is needed on the matter.
After acquiring the necessary equipment, Victor made his second attempt at interspecies Pal experiments and had a breakthrough, resulting in a new artificially created species of Pal with four legs and wings, which was immediately moved to the cultivation equipment and put under observation. He attempted to replicate the process but couldn't and attributed the success to unknown external factor.
Despite the failure to create another, Victor was extremely pleased with this experiment's result. He named the new species Shadowbeak, which showed promising progress, but not being able to replicate the process made him concerned, as he wished to enhance Shadowbeak's power and abilities through the use of distillation. He planned to continue experimenting with slight adjustments based on the result of this experiment.
He noted the disappearance of his lab assistant, Alex, during these experiments as concerning.

sections.text: (story mode wip)

sections.text: (wip)

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Victor is one of the original five tower bosses that were first released alongside Marcus, Axel, Zoe and Lily.
• Unlike the other tower bosses' Pals, Shadowbeak is an artificial Pal that was created by Victor Ashford through the use of genetic manipulation.
• As he shares his surname with the Resident Evil villain Alexander Ashford, he might be inspired by him, particularly in his motivations. His attitude and personality though, are more reminiscent of Albert Wesker.
• According to concept art released by Pocketpair, his eyes were intended to be red in colour.

Vixy (Japanese: タマモ Tamamo) is a Neutral element Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Vixy is a small, fox-like Pal with pointed ears and a fluffy tail. Its fur is primarily light beige, with highlights of brighter gold on the tips of ornamental fur on its head and paler beige on its ruff and tip of the tail. Its face and feet are patterned white with a mask and boots, respectively; and the interior of its ears are pink. Its nose is short and pointed, and its eyes are large and emerald green and ringed with fur that looks like black mascara.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Vixy are typically found in pairs, though solitary Vixy may be found, as well. They are timid, fleeing any potential predators. It seems to have a nose for digging, and is prized for its innate ability to unearth Pal Spheres.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Vixy are only found in the area around the Desolate Church north to the beach near the Sealed Realm of the Frozen Wings in the Windswept Hills. There are usually no more than five groups of Vixy in this area during any given time period.

sections.heading: Acquisition

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: Interacting with a sign will allow a player to type a short message on it for others to see, or to help organise one's base. Only the sign's original builder can edit what the sign says.

Wandering Merchant is a non-playable chracter that will buy and sell items for Gold Coins.
Like other NPCs, they can be captured in a Pal Sphere, but doing so is considered a crime and will spawn hostile human NPCs, and the player is marked as "wanted."
If captured, the merchant will still be able to sell and buy goods when talked to, making them useful on player bases.

sections.heading: Types
sections.text:
• Red Coat: these merchants are the most common, offering a selection of basic supplies and common items like food, materials, and equipment. They're a good source for restocking essentials and finding early-game necessities.
• Green Coat: these merchants offer more advanced equipment and materials, including schematics and even rare ores. They're a great resource for seasoned players looking to upgrade their gear and craft powerful items;
• Blue Coat: known in-game as Pal Merchant, as the name implies, they buy and sell Pals.
• Black Marketeer: these elusive merchants deal in illegal or restricted goods, their inventory constantly changing and often containing high-value items. Finding them is a challenge, but their wares can be incredibly lucrative. 
• Medal Merchant: this merchant appears at the Desolate Church and offers various armors, accessories, and consumables in exchange for Dog Coin.

sections.heading: Shop inventory
sections.text: While there are a few wandering traders, the green and red coats have static inventory and prices based on their location.

sections.heading: Locations
sections.text:
For convenience, see this interactive Map
Wandering Merchants are nomadic, appearing in random locations across the map. However, there are a few places where they're more likely to show up:
• Settlement Outskirts: merchants might stop to trade with travelers near the outskirts of settlements.
• Crossroads: major crossroads and travel hubs often attract Wandering Merchants looking for customers.
• Random Encounters: While exploring the wilderness, the player might stumble upon a Wandering Merchant setting up camp.

Weight is a mechanic in Palworld used for items in the player's inventory.

sections.heading: About
sections.text:
The player can carry a maximum number of items. This is calculated using the weight of each item. Going over this weight limit will disable some mechanics (see Consequences). The player can see their current and maximum weight in the bottom of their inventory. When the player goes past halfway of their maximum capacity, their current weight load will also be shown on the HUD as they pick up or drop items.
Every item has a weight value. Some items have a weight of 0.0, such as Pal Gear or Gold Coins. The weight of a stack of an item can be found in the inventory, in the top right of an item's picture. You can also hover over the item. Its weight will then be displayed in the bottom right of the information modal.

sections.heading: Consequences
sections.text:
When your character carries too much, they will be 'weighed down'. While weighed down, the following things will be disabled:
• Jumping
• Running
• Flying (on a pal)
• Rolling (Makes it hard to dodge incoming attacks)
• Gliding (Will fall down if weighed down while mid-air)
• Climbing (Can continue climbing if already on a wall. Can still get on wall using Grappling Gun; but you can't do it while standing on the ground)
• Crouching (You can't stop sliding if you overweight during a slide)
As soon as you go over your weight limit, your walking speed will decrease, and slow down more until you go 50kg over your maximum weight limit. Afterwards, there is no extra penalty. The speed decrease can make it difficult to deposit the massive items, requiring you to have to drop or dispose of items to continue. Your pal's movement will also be affected by your overweight.

sections.heading: Increasing Max Weight
sections.text:
The maximum carrying capacity can be upgraded using points when leveling up.
Some pals have Partner Skills that increase your maximum carrying capacity.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
You can still mount and dismount pals while weighed down. You can also use your Grappling Gun to move around. By combining the mount's height and your grappling gun, you can move around fairly well even when weighed down. 
You need to have created the equipment for that pal before it increases the carry weight.

sections.heading: Changes
sections.text: 0.1.4.0 - Your movement is no longer completely halted when you are weighed down too much. Being weighed down now only linearly slows you down until you go 50kg over your weight limit.

The Wildlife Sanctuaries are strange, possibly artificially-created islands that serve as unique spawn locations for wild versions of Alpha Pals and Pal subspecies. 
Visiting a Wildlife Sanctuary is considered Trespassing and will spawn varying levels of PIDF Officers. While on one of these islands, the offending player's screen will display a warning that "Criminal Activity is Underway", and being spotted will warrant a level 1 Trespassing charge from the PIDF.

sections.heading: Locations
sections.text:
There are currently 3 Wildlife Sanctuaries in Palworld:
• No. 1 Wildlife Sanctuary
• No. 2 Wildlife Sanctuary
• No. 3 Wildlife Sanctuary

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: • The PIDF that spawn at the island give much less XP than they should for their level. This is most likely intentional to prevent players from getting a trespassing charge and perpetually level grinding.

sections.heading: Bugs
sections.text:
• Visiting a Wildlife Sanctuary while on a mount, specifically a flying one, will bypass the Trespassing warning so long as the player does not get off the mount.
 ◦ As of Early Access 0.1.5.0, this bug has now been fixed.

sections.text: Wixen Noct (Japanese: フォレーオ Forēo) is a Fire and Dark elemental Pal. It is a subspecies of Wixen.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Wixen Noct is a fox-like Pal that stands on two legs. It appears to wear a wide brimmed violet witch hat that ends with a glowing violet-magenta flame. At the base of the hat are two short, cat like ears with magenta ear fluff that stick outwards. Flowing from the front of the witch hat are two long tufts of violet fur that flow in the wind. It has narrowed aqua eyes, with red-pink marks under its eyes. It has a short snout and a rather large black nose. Its face is covered with white fur. On its chest, it has light mauve fur that fades to a slight pink near the shoulders. It looks like it wears long, dark purple sleeves that have a pink inside. Its paws are white, with three sharp black claws on each hand. It looks like it has violet pants on that end at the knees. Attached to the waist, is a long, flowy violet cape that is pink on the inside. Its feet are paws, mostly a charred black colour rather than the white like the rest of its body.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: Wixen Noct do not react to seeing the player character. However, they become aggressive upon being attacked.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: • Sakurajima island.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Wixen Noct was featured in Paldeck No.093 on PocketPair X and youtube channel.

Wooden Structure Set is a set of structures in Palworld.
It's a tier 2 technology item and requires 2 points to unlock.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text:
Once unlocked, one can then build 8 basic wooden structures. These include a wooden wall, wooden foundation, wooden roof, wooden wall and window, wooden triangular wall, wooden slanted roof, wooden stairs, and a wooden door.
All structures need a form of contact with the ground. Wooden walls and doors can be built when vertically connecting to a roof or foundation. If this foundation is broken, all walls connected to it will break if there is no additional gripping point above or below them (and drop their ingredients), even if they are touching another wall.

sections.heading: Fire
sections.text:
Fire attacks can cause wooden structures to be set on fire. This fire will spread to other wooden structures that are directly connected. This fire will slowly damage the structure until it breaks. Water Pals will try to extinguish the fire, but it may spread too quickly. Be careful with wooden houses!
Campfires do not burn wooden structures.

sections.heading: Patch Notes
sections.text: 0.1.4.0 - Reduced the speed and range of fire spreading on wooden buildings

See also: List of Pals by Work Suitability
Each Pal in Palworld is unique, with specific traits that make them better suited for particular tasks. These traits, known as Work Suitabilities, govern their efficiency in activities such as farming, mining, crafting, and more. Assigning the right Pal to the right task is essential for maximizing productivity and conserving resources.
Natural Work Suitabilities: Each Pal is born with inherent abilities ranging from 0 (lowest) to 4 (highest).
Boosting Suitabilities: Players can use a Pal Essence Condenser to increase a Pal’s Work Suitability by one level, giving even lower-ranked Pals the chance to excel in specific roles.
Task Specialization: Some tasks, like Cooling or Medicine Production, require specialized traits, making Work Suitabilities crucial for managing advanced gameplay mechanics.

sections.heading: List of Work Suitabilities
sections.text:
Here is a detailed list of Work Suitabilities, including their roles and benefits: 
• Kindling: Allows Pals to power furnaces and help cook food.
• Watering: Enables Pals to water crops and power water wheels.
• Planting: Lets Pals plant seeds in farms.
• Generating Electricity: Pals can power electric devices.
• Handiwork: Enables Pals to manage crafting benches, conveyor belts, and similar setups.
• Gathering: Allows Pals to harvest crops and carry bodies.
• Lumbering: Lets Pals cut down trees.
• Mining: Enables Pals to mine rocks.
• Medicine Production: Lets Pals create medicine at specific workbenches.
• Cooling: Allows Pals to cool down containers.
• Transporting: Lets Pals move items into storage units from harvest stations or the ground.
• Farming: Allows Pals to drop items when placed in a ranch.
Each suitability is tailored to specific activities, making it vital to align a Pal’s strengths with the appropriate task.

sections.heading: Work Priority System
sections.text: The Work Priority System in Palworld determines the order in which tasks are performed. Tasks are assigned priority values, with lower numbers indicating higher priority. This system ensures critical tasks, such as defense or electricity generation, are completed before less urgent ones like gathering or cooling.

sections.heading: How Work Priority Works
sections.text:
Critical Tasks First: Activities like Attack or Defense are always prioritized to ensure survival.
Resource Management: Tasks like Harvesting and Transporting follow, ensuring a steady flow of resources.
Specialized Roles: Tasks like Medicine Production or Handiwork are handled once foundational needs are met.
Here is a breakdown of Work Priorities based on their priority value: 
• Attack and Defense tasks are the highest priority (value: 1).
• Reviving characters and extinguishing fires come next (value: 2).
• Construction tasks follow (value: 3).
• Grazing and electricity generation are mid-priority (values: 4–5).
• Tasks like smelting, cooking, and harvesting are lower priority (values: 6–8).
• Transporting, planting, and watering tasks are even lower (values: 9–10).
• Specialized tasks like medicine production and cooling are the lowest priority (values: 10+).

sections.heading: Maximizing Work Suitability in Palworld
sections.text:
To optimize gameplay, players must strategically assign Pals to tasks based on their Work Suitabilities and the priority of the task. Here are some tips:
• Enhance Suitabilities with Pal Essence Condenser: Use the Pal Essence Condenser to boost a Pal’s Work Suitability, particularly for critical tasks like Generating Electricity or Lumbering.
• Match Pals to High-Priority Tasks: Assign Pals with high Mining suitability to resource extraction or those with high Kindling suitability to fuel furnaces.
• Plan for Specialized Tasks: Keep a roster of Pals with unique abilities like Cooling or Medicine Production for advanced tasks that require precision.
• Monitor Resource Flow: Ensure tasks like Gathering and Transporting are regularly performed to maintain a steady supply of materials.
• Make use of Monitor Stand: In addition to setting the pace of Pal work, you can allow/disallow certain work for Pals at Bases.
• Perform Research: Upon building a Pal Labor Research Laboratory, various research tasks can be completed to improve task speed, decrease material costs, and so forth within Bases.

Workload is an unit of effort in Palworld. The workload of a building or ingredient defines how long the player or their Pals must take to finish its construction or crafting.
Workload often increases with an item's rarity. Better quality items also often have a higher workload. For example, comparing Stone Pickaxe and Metal Pickaxe, 5 and 50, respectively, workload are required.

World Settings are various settings you can add to your world when creating one to play in. They can also be settings for Dedicated Servers and Official servers.
These consist of How many guilds can be created and how long eggs hatch in the world.
The current available World Settings as of v0.2.4.0 (on a slider unless otherwise noted)
• Multiplayer [On/Off]
• Day Speed
• Night Speed
• EXP Rate 
• Pal Capture Rate
• Pal Appearance Rate *Note; Affects game performance
• Damage from Pals Multiplier
• Damage to Pals Multiplier
• Pal Hunger Depletion Rate
• Pal Stamina Depletion Rate
• Pal Auto Heal Regeneration Rate
• Pal Sleep Health Regeneration Rate (Health Regeneration Rate in Palbox)
• Damage from Player Multiplier
• Damage to Player Multiplier
• Player Hunger Depletion Rate
• Player Stamina Reduction Rate
• Player Auto Health Regeneration Rate
• Player Sleep Health Regeneration Rate
• Damage to Structure Multiplier
• Structure Deterioration Rate
• Maximum number of Dropped Items in a World (3,000 default, scale of 0-5,000)
• Gather Items Multiplier
• Gatherable Objects Health Multiplier
• Gatherable Objects Respawn interval
• Dropped items Multiplier
• Time (H) to incubate Massive Egg. Note; Other eggs also require time to incubate. ((Editor's note; Massive Egg is a Holdover from previous Dev Build, it is referring to what is now; Huge Egg,))
• Enable Raid Events [On/Off] ((May be bugged, as I still get Raided with this setting toggled OFF))
• Death Penalty (No Drops/Drop all items except equipment/drop all items/drop all items and all Pals on team)
• Maximum Number of Guilds (1-100, Default 20)
• Max Pals Working at Base (1-20, may have to adjust depending on your system specifications)

Xenolord (Japanese: ゼノドラン Xenodoran) is a Dark and Dragon elemental Pal.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Xenolord is a tall, bipedal, dragon-like Pal. It has a narrowed head and mean look, the head being a smoky grey colour. Sticking out from the sides of its head, Xenolord has two long spikes. It has narrowed eyes, the sclera in its eyes pitch black. Its eye colour is a blood red colour, and its pupils are a slitted black colour. On the top of its head, it has two, thick cyan horns that point back. Its bottom jaw appears to be a rusted bronze colour. It has a thick neck and round body, with thick legs and thin arms. It has a rusted bronze underbelly, going from its bottom jaw and all the way to its rear. It has two glistening cyan plates that point downwards on its chest, and the back of its body is a smoky grey colour. It has two smokey grey arms that are tipped with three glistening cyan claws on each hand. It has two bulky legs, the legs smokey grey. On the upper portion of the legs, it has a glistening cyan-coloured diamond shape. It has three thick and sharp cyan claws on each foot. On its back, it has two big diamond shaped cyan parts that seem to be de-attached from the main body.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is aggressive and will attack the player character upon notice.

sections.heading: Raid Boss
sections.text: • Summoned at a Summoning Altar by using Xenolord Slab. When defeated will receive an Egg that will hatch into it.

sections.text: Yakumo (Japanese: ヤクモマル Yakumomaru) is a Neutral elemental Pal. It was first shown in the Sakurajima Update trailer.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text: Yakumo is a tall, canine-like Pal that appears like a haetae. It has a apricot coloured snout, with a large black nose. It has two white fangs that stick out from the bottom jaw, already giving it a mean look. It has a large, pointed golden horn on its head, and wide ears. Its head is mostly coloured a creamy white colour. Above its eyes are a muted dark blue line, the line thick and wavy. It gives Yakumo a narrowed eyed look. This Pal has red-pink eyes, and white pupils that are rather circular. On the sides on its eyes, this Pal has raw salmon pink circles. Its ears are wide and triangular, pointing out to the sides. These ears are tipped with a muted dark blue colour, and the insides of tis ears are a raw salmon pink colour. It appears to have a creamy white coloured mane, the mane ending with spiked fur. The spiked fur is lined with a muted dark blue colour. On the sides of its head, right under its ears, Yakumo has creamy white swirls that are tipped with a muted dark blue colour. It has a strong build, standing on all four legs firmly. Around its creamy white coloured legs, it has red bands. The bottom band is notably much bigger than the thinner band above it. These bands are on all four legs. On the outer sides of the legs, this Pal has soft yellow swirls. Yakumo's claws are short, and are the same colour as the rest of its body. Near the top of the legs, muted dark blue fur sticks out from the sides. On its back, Yakumo appears to have muted dark blue stripes. It has a thick, swirly tail that resembles a cloud, ending with a muted dark blue tip and curls and flows in the wind.

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.text: This Pal is aggressive and will attack the player character upon noticing them.

sections.heading: Wild Spawn
sections.text: Missing Information.

sections.heading: Utility
sections.text: Birds of a Feather - When fighting together, it becomes easier to encounter pals with the same passive skills as this pal.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: • Yakumo means "Thick Clouds" in Japanese.

sections.text: Zoe Rayne is the current leader of the Rayne Syndicate and owner of the electric Pal Grizzbolt. Her tower is located in the starting region, Windswept Hills.

sections.heading: Appearance
sections.text:
Zoe is a young woman with fair skin and pink eyes. Her hair is tied into two pigtails, with the left being black and the right being pink, but her bangs have the opposite colors of her pigtails, with the pink on the left and black on the right. She has a black choker and black-dyed fingernails. Around her neck, she wears a necklace with a green gem in the middle. 
Her black beanie has two ear-like shapes that are meant to loosely resemble Grizzbolt ears. It has different patches on it, including one resembling a pink triangle, pink lightning, and a Sparkit logo. There are also two safety pins attached to the beanie on the left side. 
She wears a short black leather jacket with pink lines on the sleeves and pink flared sleeves showing from underneath the jacket sleeves. A red armband is on the jacket’s left sleeve. The pink tube top she wears has a black lightning pattern. 
Her layered short skirt has the top layer being black leather with a pink line, while the underlayer color is pink, both held by a black belt. Over the top layer of the skirt, she has a belt decorated with silver triangle studs, and on the left side of her belt, there is a Grizzbolt plush keychain. 
Zoe wears one long torn stocking on her right leg, which fades from pink at the bottom to black at the top, while the one on her left leg is not torn and has pink and black lines on it. She also has a thigh strap on her left side and wears short black boots.

sections.heading: Biography
sections.text:
The Rayne Syndicate is a criminal organization led by the ruthless Zoe Rayne. Her organization is heavily involved in illegal Pal poaching. While this is their main operation, they are also involved in other criminal activities such as raiding settlements and villages, selling stims, and operating oil rigs around the islands. Because of their activities, the Palapagos Islands Defense Force (PIDF), led by Marcus Dryden, has been dispatched and stationed around these settlements to protect them from the Rayne Syndicate's raids and to keep an eye on their oil rigs operations around the islands.
Zoe grew up without a family. Her father disappeared when she was young and she never knew what happened to him. She also never knew her mother. Unknown to her, her father went missing after Bjorn Seligsson, the leader of the Feybreak Warriors, assigned him a task to finish, which left her as a baby with her mother and her father's followers. As time passed, her father's followers, the Rayne Syndicate members, took care of her. However, Zoe believed that their acts of kindness toward her were more out of obligation because she was the daughter of their leader, a belief she still holds, even after becoming the Rayne Syndicate leader. Later on, Grizzbolt took care of her too, after the two became friends when Zoe was still a child.
She met Grizzbolt for the first time when she saw her in her cage at the Syndicate base. The Syndicate members had captured Grizzbolt, planning to sell her along with other Pals. Seeing Grizzbolt's loneliness reminded Zoe of herself and her father. Being a child, she made a rash decision and decided to free Grizzbolt without any real plan of what to do after or where to go. She waited until everyone fell asleep, then unlocked Grizzbolt's cage and ran away with her.
While on the run, Zoe shared a portion of her toast with Grizzbolt, but it wasn't enough and by evening, they went back to the base because of hunger. The Syndicate members were furious at her for freeing Grizzbolt and ruining their plans to sell her, so as punishment, they threw her into a cage with Grizzbolt. In that moment, Grizzbolt defended her from them, showing how strong she was as their weapons couldn't harm her. This made Zoe realize that Grizzbolt may have never even tried to escape, even though she clearly could have with ease. At Zoe's command, Grizzbolt summoned a huge bolt of lightning that knocked out all the Syndicate members, who didn't wake up until the next morning. From that day on, the two became inseparable.
The Rayne Syndicate is always in conflict with the Free Pal Alliance (FPA), a faction that opposes them because of the Syndicate's methods of using weapons and their treatment of Pals. Zoe wondered about the real reason behind the two factions' fight. She assumed that because of the FPA's love for Pals and their demand for Pals to be treated well, they were not bad, unaware of the faction's extreme and fanatic ways. She attempted to find the answer by asking a veteran, from whom she learned that their faction has been around for a longer time than she knew and that both factions have always been in conflict. He told her he didn't know the real reason behind it and that since both factions have been like this for so long, it was unlikely they would get along anytime soon.
Like her father before his disappearance, Zoe guards one of the many towers around the island. As time went by, she questioned the true reason behind guarding the tower. Trying to figure it out, she remember hearing about a legend connected to the tower, according to it, the tower hold great power that need to be protected. She wondered about this power and why it need to be protected, however, she still felt it was her duty to protect it. Zoe is aware there are other towers around the islands and guardians like her who keep the towers and the power they hold safe.
Despite her position as leader of the Rayne Syndicate and the duty she feels she has toward protecting the tower, it didn’t stop Zoe from being curious about what lies beyond the Palpagos islands. She heard stories about outsiders from beyond the sea who came to the islands possessing tools different from those of the island's natives. She also heard a story from long ago about a man who left to explore the outside world and never came back. While these stories might be scary, they never stopped her dreams of wanting to explore new places and experience new things outside the islands, which she hopes to do one day.

sections.heading: Story events
sections.text: (story mode wip)

sections.heading: Missions
sections.text:
Post defeat, you can find her at the Desolate Church(68, -421). She will have a list of quests for you to do.
• Don't Get Cocky
Speak to Zoe while a Grizzbolt is summoned (does not need to be wild caught bred will do)
• I still don't trust you
Capture or defeat 5 Free Pal Alliance members, then talk to Zoe
• A Warm Memory
Find Zoe's Tattered Memento at the ruins then return it to her.
Reward: EXP and a "Zoe & Grizzbolt" recruited
• Happy Halloween!
Craft Zoe's Halloween Costume and hand it over to her.
Reward: EXP, and the costume
• Zoe's Sphere (repeatable)
Capture or defeat 30 Free Pal Alliance members, then talk to Zoe
Reward: another "Zoe & Grizzbolt" pal

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
• Zoe is one of the original five tower bosses that were first released alongside Marcus, Axel, Victor and Lily.
• Zoe's Grizzbolt is a female.
• The Rayne Syndicate are the first human enemies the player encounters, and defeating Zoe completes the tutorial.
